document.document_summary: This issue of the Kuwait Today Official Gazette, dated April 13, 2025, provides a comprehensive overview of various governmental and commercial activities in Kuwait. It features numerous official decrees and decisions from the Council of Ministers and various ministries, alongside legal announcements including bankruptcy judgments and property auctions. A significant portion of the gazette is dedicated to extensive tender announcements from a wide range of public entities, covering diverse sectors such as infrastructure, IT, medical supplies, oil and gas, and general services. Furthermore, it includes detailed company announcements concerning liquidations, legal status changes, commercial agency registrations, and minutes from general assembly meetings, reflecting corporate governance and financial adjustments. The document also contains financial reports, public notices, and amendments to company articles of association, serving as a vital record of official proceedings and business activities within the State of Kuwait.

chunks.title: Regarding the Repeal of the Third Paragraph of Article 41 of the Decree Issued on April 4, 1979 Concerning the Civil Service System
chunks.text_en: - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And the Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH corresponding to May 10, 2024 AD, - And Decree-Law No. 15 of 1979 concerning the Civil Service, and its amending laws, - And the Decree issued on April 4, 1979 concerning the Civil Service System, and its amending decrees, - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 concerning Ministerial Succession and Delegations, and its amending decrees, - And based on the presentation of the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of State for Cabinet Affairs, - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers, **We have decreed the following:** **Article One** The third paragraph of Article 41 of the aforementioned Decree concerning the Civil Service System shall be repealed. **Article Two** Ministers – each within their respective jurisdiction – shall implement this Decree, which shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette.

chunks.title: Announcement <orig>إعلان</orig>
chunks.text_en: Regarding the issuance of the judgment of the General Court – dated 25/3/2025 in Case No. (16/2024) Bankruptcy / 3. Filed by: 1- Tha'er Abdullah Rashid Al-Saif . Against: 1- Nasrallah International General Trading Company . 2- Fatima Abdulwahid Sukkar Nasrallah . Head of Bankruptcy Department In the Name of His Highness the Amir of Kuwait Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah General Court Bankruptcy Circuit / 3 At the session held publicly at the Court on Tuesday, 25/3/2025 AD. Presided by Professor: Abdullah Ali Al-Harbi , Court Agent With the membership of Professor: Mohammed Abdulrahman Al-Nuwaif , Court Agent And the membership of Professor: Waleed Khalid Al-Rabie , Court Agent And in the presence of Mr.: Yousef Ahmed Al-Bakr , Session Secretary (The following judgment was issued) In Case No.: (16/2024) Bankruptcy Circuit / 3 Automated Number: 242070020 Filed by: 1- Tha'er Abdullah Rashid Al-Saif . Against: 1- Nasrallah International General Trading Company . 2- Fatima Abdulwahid Sukkar Nasrallah . (Reasons) After hearing the pleadings, reviewing the documents, and deliberating legally: The facts of the case, the parties' documents, and their defense were previously summarized in the referral decision issued by the Bankruptcy Judge on 30/5/2024, to which the Court refers to avoid repetition. However, it summarizes them to the necessary extent, as the plaintiff submitted a request to the Bankruptcy Department to initiate bankruptcy declaration procedures for the defendants / Nasrallah International General Trading Company , and Ms. Fatima Abdulwahid Sukkar Nasrallah ; along with the resulting implications. Based on the premise that he obtained judgment No. 2021/10086 Leases around 28, issued on 17/5/2022, filed by him against the first defendant company, which ruled for the eviction of the property specified in the lease agreement dated 17/6/2018, and its delivery free of occupants, and that it should pay the plaintiff an amount of 58800 K.D. for the period from 1/5/2021 to 30/11/2022, and any new rent at a rate of 8400 K.D. monthly starting from 1/12/2021 until full eviction, and obligated it to pay the expenses. <footer> Amir of Kuwait Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yousef Saud Al-Sabah Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of State for Cabinet Affairs Shuraida Abdullah Saad Al-Maoosharji Issued at Seif Palace on: 8 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 6 April 2025 AD </footer> KD in exchange for actual legal fees. The judgment became final due to no appeal being filed against it. When the plaintiff commenced execution of this judgment, having executed the part related to eviction, the company did not pay the amount allocated to it, which is KD 178,480, as per the judgment. The plaintiff requested to issue a notice of payment and debt settlement to the defendant company on 5/9/2023, and to the director and partner against whom the claim was filed, Fatima Abdul Wahid Sukkar Nasrallah , on 16/7/2023. However, they did not make the payment, which prompted him to submit the aforementioned request. Whereas the request was heard before the Bankruptcy Judge , and the plaintiff appeared through his representative and submitted a portfolio of documents including the following: photocopies of the articles of association of Nasrallah International General Trading Company , a copy of the commercial register extract for Nasrallah International General Trading Company showing partner and director data, a photocopy of a statement issued by the Execution Department of the Ministry of Justice indicating the allocated amounts and a list of cases filed against the first defendant company, a photocopy of Judgment No. 2019/5826, Hawally Total Rents 7, and a photocopy of the bank accounts of the first defendant company. The defendants were represented by a lawyer who stated that settlement was not possible and requested to proceed with the procedures. On 19/2/2024, the Bankruptcy Committee was contacted to provide an opinion on the submitted request. The Committee's opinion, dated 19/3/2024, concluded that the Committee was unable to study and prepare a report on the debtor due to the absence of the debtor's financial position, with a proposal to appoint a trustee from the list of licensed auditors with the Capital Markets Authority to inventory the debtor's assets and properties and prepare an updated financial position, with the trustee's fees estimated based on 40 working hours, totaling KD 2,000. Whereas the Bankruptcy Judge issued his decision dated 28/4/2024 to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures for Nasrallah General Trading Company , setting 17/5/2022 as a provisional date for cessation of payments, publishing and announcing the decision in the Official Gazette , notifying concerned parties, and appointing Ms. Rabia Ilahiya as trustee of the bankruptcy estate. Her fees were estimated at a total of two thousand Kuwaiti Dinars from the date of this decision until the closure of the bankruptcy, and accordingly, she is to inventory the debtor company's assets and properties, take all necessary precautionary measures, and prepare a list of debts and a plan for liquidation and distribution. On 30/5/2024, the Bankruptcy Judge issued his decision (Firstly: referring the documents to the Bankruptcy Court along with the report, in preparation for a judgment regarding the bankruptcy declaration of the first defendant, Nasrallah International General Trading Company . In execution of the Bankruptcy Judge's decision, the documents were referred to the Bankruptcy Court and registered under the number stated at the beginning of the judgment. The plaintiff appeared through a lawyer representing him, insisting on the requests. The bankruptcy trustee also appeared in person and submitted unsegregated documents, which the Court reviewed, consisting of a report regarding the bankruptcy of the first defendant company. Upon the Court's review, it was found that the defendant company has no assets except for an account in Gulf Bank containing KD 52.267 (fifty-two Dinars and 267 Fils). The Court decided to reserve the case for judgment at today's session. Whereas regarding the subject of the request submitted by the plaintiff, it is stipulated in Article 1 of Bankruptcy Law No. 71/2020 that "In the application of this Law, the following words and phrases shall have the meaning indicated opposite each of them... Financial Imbalance: The debtor's funds are insufficient to pay his debts." It is also stipulated in Article 2 of the same Law that "The provisions of this Law shall apply to: 1- Every natural person proven to have the status of a merchant. 2- Kuwaiti companies and branches of foreign companies, with the exception of joint ventures." ...3. It is stipulated in the first paragraph of Article 13 of the same Law that "The debtor may submit a request to the Bankruptcy Department to commence preventive settlement procedures, restructuring, or bankruptcy declaration within a maximum period of two months from the date of cessation of payments or from the date on which he obtained information suggesting that he will be unable to pay his debts when they fall due, unless any of the creditors or regulatory bodies have submitted a request to commence procedures within the aforementioned period. Failure to submit the request within the deadlines specified in this paragraph shall not result in the inadmissibility of the request." It is stipulated in Article 131 of the Bankruptcy Law that "Subject to the provisions stipulated in this Law regarding the request to commence procedures, as well as the cases specified in the chapter on preventive settlement and the chapter on restructuring, in which the Bankruptcy Judge may issue a decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures, the Bankruptcy Judge shall issue his decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures if the following conditions are met: 1- The debtor has ceased payments. 2- There is a financial imbalance in the debtor's financial position. 3- The debtor's business is not viable for continuity." It is stipulated in Article 132 of the same Law that "In all cases where the Bankruptcy Judge issues his decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures, the Bankruptcy Department shall refer the file of the request to commence procedures to the Bankruptcy Court within ten days from the date the Bankruptcy Judge issues the decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures. A report on the procedures taken regarding the request from its submission until the issuance of the decision to commence procedures shall be deposited in the file, along with a statement of the factual and legal grounds for the decision." Article 172 of the same law stipulates that "a state of bankruptcy shall not arise except by a judgment declaring bankruptcy. The Bankruptcy Department shall, within ten days from the date of the bankruptcy declaration judgment, announce its operative part, publish it, disclose it on the Kuwait Stock Exchange / Boursa Kuwait if the debtor is listed therein, instruct the debtor to disclose it on their website, and register it as specified in Article 33 of this law. It shall also, within ten days from the date of that judgment, publish and announce its summary." It is established that "a Limited Liability Company has its own independent financial liability separate from that of its partners. Consequently, the managing director representing this company has their own independent personal financial liability, and their liability for damage caused to third parties is a tort liability that is established by proving the director's fault and the existence of a causal link between this fault and the actual damage." (Appeal No. 1022/2003 Commercial, Session 30/6/2024) It is established that "companies acquire a legal personality independent of the persons of their partners, and a consequence of this is that they have an independent and separate financial liability from the liabilities of their partners." (Appeal No. 1053/2004 Commercial, Session 11/6/2005) It is established that "the trial court has full authority to ascertain the facts of the case from its documents and papers, and from the evidence and presumptions presented. It may infer from the facts of the case and the conduct of the litigants the presumptions upon which it bases its conviction, provided that its inference is sound and supported by evidence and grounds that are firmly established in the papers and lead to the conclusion it reached. The court may intervene if the conclusion it drew from those facts does not comply with its legal requirements ." (Appeal No. 704/2000 Commercial, Session 11/2/2002) Regarding the request for the declaration of bankruptcy of the First Defendant, Nasrallah International General Trading Company , since the Court found that it ceased payment of its outstanding debt and it was not evident that it had any balance sheets or assets to show its financial position, and the debt was established against it by a final judicial judgment which obligated the First Defendant to pay the claimed amount, in addition to the Bankruptcy Judge having issued a decision to commence bankruptcy declaration proceedings and considering May 17, 2022, as the date of cessation of payment, which is the date of the final judgment issued against it, the Court therefore concludes that there was a financial deficit in the financial position of the First Defendant company, which resulted in the cessation of payment and the non-viability of the company's operations for continuity. Accordingly, based on the foregoing, the plaintiff's request for the declaration of bankruptcy of the defendant company is based on a correct foundation, necessitating a judgment in its favor, as will be stated in the operative part. Regarding the request for the declaration of bankruptcy of the Second Defendant, Fatima Abdul Wahid Sukkar Nasrallah , since it was established to the Court that the First Defendant company is a Limited Liability Company and the Second Defendant was a partner in it, and the provisions of the law have stipulated that a partner in a Limited Liability Company is not liable for the company's obligations except to the extent of their share in the capital, and the company's director is not liable for the company's obligations except in the event of their violation of the provisions of the law or the company's articles of association or error in management, which was not found in the papers. And since the plaintiff submitted a request for her bankruptcy in her capacity as the owner of the aforementioned First Defendant company, their request is based on an incorrect foundation, because the company has a legal personality and an independent financial liability separate from that of the Second Defendant. Therefore, it is necessary to rule to reject their request for these reasons, without explicitly stating it in the operative part. Regarding the expenses, including legal fees , the Court obligates the defendant to pay them in accordance with the provisions of Article 1/119 of the Code of Civil Procedure and Article 23 of the Bankruptcy Law . (For these reasons)

chunks.title: The Court ruled:
chunks.text_en: To declare the bankruptcy of the First Defendant, Nasrallah International General Trading Company , and to set May 17, 2022, as the provisional date for cessation of payment. The Bankruptcy Department shall register this judgment in the Commercial Register and publish its summary in the Official Gazette according to the procedures followed in this regard, and to add the expenses and an amount of ten Dinars for legal fees to be borne by the bankruptcy estate . <footer> Session Secretary Court Agent </footer>

chunks.title: Announcement <orig>إعلان</orig>
chunks.text_en: Regarding the issuance of the First Instance Court judgment - dated 25/3/2025 in Case No. (19/2024) Bankruptcy/3. Filed by/ Winsy General Trading and Contracting Company (W.L.L.) and another. Against/ 1- Warba Island Real Estate Company and others. Pursuant to the provisions of Article (172) of Bankruptcy Law No. (71/2020) and its Executive Regulations . Head of Bankruptcy Department In the Name of His Highness the Amir of Kuwait Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah The First Instance Court Bankruptcy Circuit / 3 At the session held publicly in the Court on Tuesday, 25/3/2025 AD. Presided by Professor: Abdullah Ali Al-Harbi , Court Agent And with the membership of Professor: Mohammed Abdul Rahman Al-Nuwaif , Court Agent And with the membership of Professor: Waleed Khalid Al-Rabee , Court Agent And in the presence of Mr.: Yousef Ahmed Al-Bakr , Session Secretary (The following judgment was issued) In Lawsuit No.: (19/2024) Bankruptcy Circuit / 3 Automated Number: 242157090 Filed by: 1- Winsy General Trading and Contracting Company (W.L.L.) . 2- Mohammed Younis Zattam . Against: 1- Warba Island Real Estate Company . 2- Al-Sayer Facilities General Trading and Contracting Company . 3- Commercial Exchange General Trading Company . 4- Al-Jaradi Group for Household Utensils . 5- Ahmed and Yousef Abdullah Al-Fahad General Trading and Contracting Company . 6- Adnan Al-Saleh Commercial Group . 7- Ajyal Recreational Real Estate Company . 8- Kuwait Finance House - in its capacity. 9- Warba Bank - in its capacity. 10- Al-Mulla International Finance Company . 11- Aayan Real Estate Company . (Reasons) After hearing the pleadings, reviewing the documents, and deliberating legally: Whereas the facts of the lawsuit and the documents of the two plaintiffs were previously obtained by the referral decision issued by the Bankruptcy Judge on 30/5/2024 in Application No. (19/2022 Declaration of Bankruptcy ), to which the Court refers to avoid repetition, but it summarizes them to the necessary extent that the two plaintiffs submitted an application to commence bankruptcy declaration proceedings , based on the fact that they are indebted to the defendants by virtue of several judgments and bank checks on different dates, and that they have ceased payment of debt in their financial position, and therefore the two plaintiffs had the right to submit an application to commence bankruptcy declaration proceedings against them, hence the lawsuit. And whereas the application was considered before the Bankruptcy Judge , and on 19/12/2022, he issued his decision to address the Bankruptcy Committee to examine the availability of the substantive conditions thereof as stated in the decision, to which the Court refers to avoid repetition. And in implementation of the Bankruptcy Judge's decision, the Bankruptcy Committee submitted its Report No. 13 dated 26/3/2023, in which it concluded that "the continuation of the company's activity is futile due to negative financial results, therefore it is in the interest of the creditors to declare their bankruptcy and appoint a trustee to sell the company's assets and stop the waste of company assets, with a proposal to take the following precautionary measures: - promptly appoint an inventory trustee and seize the company's assets - promptly stop administrative and general expenses. - impose restrictions on the two debtors in disposing of their funds and assets. The trustee's fees were estimated at 4000 K.D. ". And after the submission of the Bankruptcy Committee's report, the Bankruptcy Judge issued his decision on 27/5/2024 to: 1- commence bankruptcy declaration proceedings for both: 1- Winsy General Trading and Contracting Company (W.L.L.) . 2- Mohammed Younis Zattam , and to set the date of this decision as a temporary date for cessation of payment, and to publish and announce the decision in the Official Gazette and notify the concerned parties. 2- appoint Professor/ Ali Owaid Rakhees Awad as trustee for the bankruptcy estate , and his fees were estimated at 4000 K.D. from the date of this decision until the closure of the bankruptcy estate, and he is required to prepare a list of debts and a liquidation and distribution plan. And on 30/5/2024, the Bankruptcy Judge also issued his decision to refer the bankruptcy declaration application documents in their current state to the Bankruptcy Court along with the report regarding the defendant Aayan Real Estate Company , and he insisted on his previous defense. And a representative for the defendant Adnan Al-Saleh Commercial Group appeared and requested a postponement for review. None of the remaining defendant parties appeared despite being legally notified. Whereas the court decided to reserve the case for judgment at today's session. And whereas, regarding the subject matter, it is stipulated in Article 1 of Bankruptcy Law No. 71/2020 that "For the purpose of applying this Law, the following words and phrases shall have the meanings assigned to each of them ...... Financial Imbalance: The debtor's funds are insufficient to pay his debts." And it is stipulated in Article 2 of the same Law that "The provisions of this Law shall apply to: 1- Every natural person proven to be a merchant. 2- Kuwaiti companies and branches of foreign companies, with the exception of joint ventures. (... 3 And it is stipulated in the first paragraph of Article 13 of the same Law that "The debtor may submit a request to the Bankruptcy Department for the commencement of preventive settlement, restructuring, or bankruptcy declaration procedures within a maximum period of two months from the date of cessation of payment or from the date on which he had information indicating that he would be unable to pay his debts when due, unless any of the creditors or regulatory bodies have submitted a request for the commencement of procedures within the aforementioned period, and failure to submit the request within the deadlines specified in this paragraph shall not result in the rejection of the request." And it is stipulated in the first paragraph of Article 3 of Ministerial Decision No. 31 of 2022 regarding the amendment of some provisions of the Executive Regulations of Law No. 71/2020 issuing the Bankruptcy Law that "The debtor may submit a request to the Bankruptcy Department for the commencement of preventive settlement, restructuring, or bankruptcy declaration procedures within a maximum period of two months from the date of cessation of payment or from the date on which a financial imbalance occurred in his position or from the date on which he had information indicating that he would be unable to pay his debts when due, or that a financial imbalance would occur in his position, provided that the indebtedness for which the debtor ceased or is expected to cease payment is not less than K.D. 10,000 (ten thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), and also provided that the amount of the actual or expected financial imbalance is not less than the aforementioned amount, unless any of the creditors or regulatory bodies have submitted a request for the commencement of procedures within the aforementioned period." And it is stipulated in Article 131 of the Bankruptcy Law that "Subject to the provisions stipulated in this Law regarding the request for the commencement of procedures, as well as the cases in preparation for the judgment declaring the bankruptcy of the applicant/ Mohammed Younis Zimam and Wensi General Trading and Contracting Company (LLC) , with the Bankruptcy Department being tasked with determining the circuit and the specific session for its consideration and notifying the concerned parties thereof." In execution of the decision of the Bankruptcy Judge , the papers were referred to the present circuit and registered under its current number. Upon reviewing the case as indicated in the session minutes, at the session on 26/11/2024, a representative for the defendants appeared and submitted a memorandum requesting, at its conclusion, a judgment: declaring the bankruptcy of the two plaintiffs and setting the date of their cessation of payment as 27/5/2023, with the judgment being enforceable against all defendants in accordance with the letter from the Bankruptcy Court issued to the court on 30/5/2024, and including immediate enforcement of the judgment. A representative for the defendant Aayan Real Estate Company appeared and submitted a portfolio of documents, the most important of which included (a copy of the judgment in Case No. 2021/1577 Misdemeanors – 2022/16 Conciliation). A representative for the defendant Al-Sayer Facilities Company appeared and requested a postponement for review. A representative for the defendant Adnan Al-Saleh Group appeared and requested a postponement for review. A representative for the defendant Agyal Entertainment Real Estate Company appeared and requested a postponement for review. At the session on 24/12/2024, a representative for the plaintiffs appeared and insisted on the requests. A representative for the second defendant (Al-Sayer Facilities General Trading and Contracting Company ) appeared and submitted a portfolio of documents which included (a copy of the statement of claim No. 2024/13048 Commercial Full/45, a copy of a request for an order of payment based on a promissory note for the amount of three hundred thirty-five thousand two hundred Dinars dated 7/2/2019). A representative for the sixth defendant (Adnan Al-Saleh Commercial Group ) appeared and submitted a portfolio of documents, the most important of which included (a copy of the judgment in Case No. 2021/2895 Rents Full Al-Ahmadi/5 , a copy of the judgment in Appeal No. 2021/546 Rents/1 ). A representative for Aayan Real Estate Company appeared and requested a judgment dismissing the case. At the session on 4/3/2025, the representative for the two plaintiffs appeared and stated that he was not waiving the litigation against Aayan Real Estate Company and that he had filed a new statement of claim including it among the defendants, and he presented a copy of the statement of claim, which he announced to those present, requesting at its conclusion a judgment: declaring the bankruptcy of the two plaintiffs against the defendants and setting the date of their cessation of payment as 27/5/2023 according to the report of the bankruptcy trustee , with the judgment being enforceable against all defendants and including immediate enforcement of the judgment. A representative for the defendant Al-Sayer Facilities Company appeared and insisted on his previous defense, and a representative And whereas regarding the request to include the judgment with immediate enforcement without bail, the judgments issued by the Bankruptcy Court in accordance with the Bankruptcy Law are enforceable, and there is no need to state that in the operative part of the judgment. And whereas regarding the comprehensive expenses for legal fees, since the bankruptcy case is a procedural case based on guaranteeing the rights of creditors, therefore, the Court rules to add them to the bankruptcy estate , in accordance with the provisions of Article 1/119 of the Code of Civil Procedure and Article 189, paragraph (c) of the Bankruptcy Law . (For these reasons) The Court ruled: - To declare the bankruptcy of both plaintiffs: 1- Wensi General Trading and Contracting Company W.L.L. 2- Mohammed Younis Rattām , and to set May 27, 2024, as the provisional date for cessation of payments, and the Bankruptcy Department shall register this judgment in the Commercial Register , publish its summary in the Official Gazette , and announce it according to the procedures followed in this regard, and take all necessary legal measures concerning their bankruptcy declaration, and added the expenses and an amount of twenty Dinars for legal fees to the bankruptcy estate . Session Secretary Court Agent as stipulated in the chapter on preventive settlement and the chapter on restructuring , in which the bankruptcy judge may issue a decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures. The bankruptcy judge issues his decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures if the following conditions are met: 1- The debtor has ceased payments. 2- There is a deficit in the debtor's financial position. 3- The debtor's business is not viable for continuity." And it is stipulated in Article 132 of the same law that "In all cases where the bankruptcy judge issues his decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures, the Bankruptcy Department shall refer the bankruptcy commencement application file to the Bankruptcy Court within ten days from the date the bankruptcy judge issues his decision to commence bankruptcy declaration procedures, and a report on the procedures taken regarding the application from its submission until the issuance of the decision to commence procedures shall be deposited in the file, along with a statement of the factual and legal grounds for the decision." And it is stipulated in Article 172 of the same law that "The state of bankruptcy shall not arise except by a judgment declaring bankruptcy, and the Bankruptcy Department shall, within ten days from the date of the bankruptcy declaration judgment, announce its operative part, publish it, and disclose it on the Boursa Kuwait if the debtor is listed therein, and instruct the debtor to disclose it on his website and register it as stipulated in Article 33 of this Law, and shall also, within ten days from the date of that judgment, publish its summary and announce it." Furthermore, it is stipulated in Article 254 of the same law that "The provisions of this Law shall generally apply to preventive settlement , restructuring , and bankruptcy declaration procedures submitted concerning corporate debts....." And it is established that "The trial court has full authority to ascertain the facts of the case from its documents, papers, and the evidence and presumptions presented, and it may infer from the facts of the case and the conduct of the litigants the presumptions upon which it relies in forming its conviction, provided that its inference is reasonable and supported by evidence and grounds firmly established in the documents and leading to the conclusion it reached, and the Court of Cassation may intervene if the conclusion drawn from those facts is not in accordance with legal requirements." (Appeal No. 704/2000 Commercial, Session of 11/2/2002) Given the foregoing, and as it is established from the documents that the two plaintiffs submitted a request for bankruptcy declaration due to their cessation of payment of the debt owed to each of the defendants by virtue of judgments and bank checks, and the amount of the debt exceeded the amount specified by the Executive Regulations of the Bankruptcy Law , and also that * Decree-Law No. (67) of 1980 promulgating the Civil Code. * Law No. (1) of 1993 concerning the Protection of Public Funds. * Article (22) of Law No. 106 of 2013 concerning Combating Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing. * Article (36) of Law No. 2 of 2016 concerning the Establishment of the Public Anti-Corruption Authority and Provisions for Financial Disclosure. * Decision of the Public Prosecutor No. (2025/17) in Case No. (2022/90) regarding Money Laundering Restriction. - Decided -

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Agents for the management of seized funds (real estate) appointed in accordance with the provisions of Article (24) of Law No. (1) of 1993, referred to above, shall adhere to the following controls in performing their assigned tasks: 1. The agent shall, in performing their task, adhere to all obligations of an agent in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Two of Part Three of Decree-Law No. (67) of 1980, referred to above, provided that it does not conflict with the provisions stipulated in this decision or with future decisions issued by the President of the State Audit Bureau . 2. The agent shall preserve the funds (real estate) entrusted to their management and must exercise the care of an ordinary person in doing so, as required by the aforementioned agency provisions. 3. The agent may not delegate all or part of their task to anyone except with written permission from the Public Prosecutor . 4. The agent's authority is limited to management acts, and they may not undertake any disposition that is not a management act except with written permission from the Public Prosecutor . 5. The agent may not use the seized funds (real estate) for their own benefit, and it is risky for them to deal with the seized persons. 6. The agent must refer to the Public Prosecutor , as appropriate, regarding all requests or grievances presented to them concerning the seized funds (real estate) that are prohibited from disposition. 7. The agent must disclose any conflicts of interest between themselves and the seized funds (real estate), as well as between themselves and the seized persons. 8. The agent must submit to the Public Prosecutor every three months an accurate statement of the assigned tasks, including the financial position and supplementary explanations, a statement of revenues and expenditures for the period covered by the statement, a cumulative statement from the start of management until the end of this period, a cash flow statement, and a comprehensive administrative report on dispositions related to the management of the seized funds (real estate). A copy of the foregoing shall be submitted to the President of the State Audit Bureau within fifteen days following the end of each period, and the Bureau shall notify the Public Prosecutor and the agent of any observations it deems necessary. 9. The agent shall continue their management duties until the case is decided or a decision is issued to lift the seizure on the seized funds (real estate). 10. If the agent wishes to terminate their agency, or if their agency terminates for any reason, they must complete the tasks they have started to a degree that no harm is feared to the funds (real estate) managed by them.

clauses.text_en: The agent shall, in performing their task, adhere to all obligations of an agent in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Two of Part Three of Decree-Law No. (67) of 1980, referred to above, provided that it does not conflict with the provisions stipulated in this decision or with future decisions issued by the President of the State Audit Bureau .
The agent shall preserve the funds (real estate) entrusted to their management and must exercise the care of an ordinary person in doing so, as required by the aforementioned agency provisions.
The agent may not delegate all or part of their task to anyone except with written permission from the Public Prosecutor .
The agent's authority is limited to management acts, and they may not undertake any disposition that is not a management act except with written permission from the Public Prosecutor .
The agent may not use the seized funds (real estate) for their own benefit, and it is risky for them to deal with the seized persons.
The agent must refer to the Public Prosecutor , as appropriate, regarding all requests or grievances presented to them concerning the seized funds (real estate) that are prohibited from disposition.
The agent must disclose any conflicts of interest between themselves and the seized funds (real estate), as well as between themselves and the seized persons.

The agent must submit to the Public Prosecutor  every three months an accurate statement of the assigned tasks, including the financial position and supplementary explanations, a statement of revenues and expenditures for the period covered by the statement, a cumulative statement from the start of management until the end of this period, a cash flow statement, and a comprehensive administrative report on dispositions related to the management of the seized funds (real estate). A copy of the foregoing shall be submitted to the President of the State Audit Bureau  within fifteen days following the end of each period, and the Bureau shall notify the Public Prosecutor  and the agent of any observations it deems necessary.
The agent shall continue their management duties until the case is decided or a decision is issued to lift the seizure on the seized funds (real estate).

If the agent wishes to terminate their agency, or if their agency terminates for any reason, they must complete the tasks they have started to a degree that no harm is feared to the funds (real estate) managed by them.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The President of the State Audit Bureau may issue a decision with special additional controls for the performance of some agents' work in cases that necessitate it from seizure cases, according to the requirements of the interest of the funds (real estate) under management.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: This decision shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force from the day following its publication. All concerned parties shall implement it, each within their respective jurisdiction. <footer> President of the State Audit Bureau Essam Salem Al-Roumi Issued on: 12 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 10 April 2025 AD </footer>

chunks.title: Article One

chunks.title: Announcement of Factory Sale
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Justice - General Department of Enforcement - Capital Governorate - Enforcement File No. (200257630), announces that on Tuesday, 27/05/2025, starting from 10:00 AM, the entire factory known by the trade name "Green Carbon Factory " for the production of liquefied carbon dioxide gas, Industrial Establishment License No. (11960) issued by the Public Authority for Industry on 14/12/2011, located in Shuaiba Industrial Area (Eastern) - Plot No. (16) of Block No. (2) with an area of 20000m², will be sold by public auction. This is in execution of Judgment No. 2020/2384 Commercial Appeal Debtor Government/9 dated 16/02/2021, which became final by virtue of the Consultative Chamber's decision at the Court of Cassation No. 2021/1277 Commercial Cassation/2 dated 16/10/2022, and mortgaged in favor of the Industrial Bank of Kuwait (Mortgagee Creditor) by virtue of Loan Agreement documented under No. 2368 Vol. 3 on 23/09/2012, and Banking Facilities Agreement documented under No. 612 Vol. 3 on 28/04/2012, and Loan Agreement documented under No. 649 Vol. 3 on 08/04/2015, considering it established with all its material and moral components and elements, in fulfillment of the bank's debt owed by the debtor/Green Carbon General Trading Co. W.L.L. in the amount of K.D. 12,377,302.008 (twelve million three hundred seventy-seven thousand three hundred two Kuwaiti Dinars and eight fils) as of 21/11/2018, in addition to expenses and interest from that date until full payment. The sale will take place at the factory premises in accordance with the procedures stipulated in Article (44) of the Commercial Law . If this day is not sufficient to complete the sale, it will be completed on the following three days, noting that the Public Authority for Industry (the Authority) does not object to the transfer of the aforementioned industrial license and its associated benefits, including the right to the plot on which the factory is built, to the successful bidder, after the bidder pays the Authority the financial amounts due for the said plot. Therefore, those wishing to purchase should proceed to the Capital Enforcement Department to obtain the necessary entry permits, no later than one week from the date of publication of this announcement - Phone: 97485862. For more information, the General Department of Enforcement - Capital Governorate can be consulted during official working hours. This announcement is made for clarification. Head of Capital Governorate Enforcement Department

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: Regarding the annual plan for topics to be presented during the fiscal year 2026/2025 for the General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers and its affiliated entities, based on Article No. (148) of Ministry of Finance Circular No. (5) of 2020 regarding public procurement systems for public entities, and Article No. (33) of Law No. 49 of 2016 regarding public tenders. <table_caption>General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers - Annual Plan for Supplies and Services</table_caption> <table> | | General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers | |:---|:--- 1. Purchase of Citrix devices and licenses. 2. Software maintenance for the Ministry of Electronic Administration's development and access project (eCom). 3. Provision of specialists and specialized technical labor for the General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers . 4. Purchase of a Tape Library device. 5. Purchase of a PAM Tools system. 6. Purchase of licenses for Oracle database encryption. 7. Purchase of server devices. 8. Central Anti-Corruption Agency upon issuance of a legal decision: - Updating network and backup infrastructure. - CCTV security surveillance system. - Electronic services (Website, E-service, Mobile App Solution). - Development and maintenance of databases for the Agency's systems. - Development of identification cards, health insurance, and their requirements. - Fixed asset inventory system. - Visitor management and permit registration system. 9. Supreme Council for Privatization : - Renting the new agency headquarters. - Privatization procedures for the main hospitals of the Ministry of Electricity and Water , and deals, and the Project Management Office / Legal / Human Resources. - Asset valuation of the main hospitals of the Ministry of Electricity and Water . - Office of the Minister of State for National Assembly Affairs . 10. Network firewall devices (FortiGate Firewall Quantity). - Purchase, supply, installation, operation, and renewal of Fortinet firewall device licenses, renewal of Ministry website protection licenses (Datacenter Firewall License), renewal of SSL firewall device licenses, renewal of Fortinet Internet network protection device licenses, renewal of network security and protection software licenses (Falcon Firewall License), and renewal of network security and protection software licenses (EndPoint Protection Antivirus). - Design, development, and operation of the automated Human Resources system, and design and development of smartphone applications on Android and iOS systems for facial recognition-based attendance and departure. 11. Government Performance Monitoring Agency : - Supply of spare parts for printers, computers, and storage media. - Provision of ink for all printers in Seif Palace and Twin Tower . - Supply of stationery (Flash and CD's). - Veracode program. - Renewal of various software licenses. - Renewal of backup device licenses and peripheral devices. - Penetration testing service. - Provision of a software developer / programmer. - Information security confidentiality consultations. - Provision of internet service, data connectivity, and 5G subscriptions. - Development of the current PABX system. - Equipping PABX devices. - Equipping a meeting room. - Devices for equipping new floors. - Email protection device. - Server expansion device (Nutanix expansion node). - Peripheral devices. <header>

* **Statement** * **Letter No.:** 3467 * **Letter Date:** 2025/04/08 * **Subject** * Tenders Committee Resolution No. (2024/40) regarding the system submitted by Energy Solutions General Trading and Contracting Company for Tender No. WKM/2021/7310, Supply of 11 kV Medium Voltage Aluminum Cables. * Firstly: Acceptance of the system in form. * Secondly: Regarding the subject, cancellation of the Central Agency for Public Tenders Board of Directors Resolution No. (71), adopted at its meeting No. (34/2024) and issued on 2024/8/21, the text of which is as follows: * The Agency's Board of Directors' resolution, by majority of its members, to approve the application of the provisions of Article No. (85) of Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016, Clause (c) to: * - Gulf Cables and Electrical Industries Group Company * - Energy Solutions General Trading and Contracting Company * This is by deleting them from the register and prohibiting them from participation for two years. * Resolution No. (84) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Board of Directors of the Agency was informed, and the entity was notified.

* **Statement** * **Letter No.:** 245 * **Letter Date:** 2025/01/29 * **Subject** * Bazadis Kuwait General Building Contracting Company (Lowest Prices) meeting the required terms and specifications after applying the preference percentage for small and medium-sized enterprises, with a total amount of (K.D. 145,207,639), after completion. * Resolution No. (78) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Agency's Board of Directors decided to approve the award to Bazadis Kuwait General Building Contracting Company (Lowest Prices) meeting the required terms and specifications after applying the preference percentage for small and medium-sized enterprises, with a total amount of (K.D. 145,207,639), after completion. * Resolution No. (82) for Meeting No. (32/2025) regarding annual maintenance works for equipment in the Reverse Osmosis (R.O) Seawater Desalination Unit Project at Shuwaikh Power Generation and Water Distillation Station to BILFINGER ENGINEERING TECHNOLOGIES GmbH represented by Gulf Group for General Trading and Contracting Company for a total amount of (K.D. 7,994,277,250). * Resolution No. (79) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Agency's Board of Directors decided the following: * Firstly: Non-approval of the entity's recommendation. * Secondly: The committee is in the process of completion based on the provisions of Article 40 of Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016 with all bids and providing the Agency with the recommendation.

* **Statement** * **Letter No.:** 20241401000025 * **Letter Date:** 2024/12/19 * **Subject** * Request to prepare a development budget with Art in White General Building Contracting Company (Lowest Prices) for an amount of (K.D. 919,000) to price unpriced items in the total bid value for Tender No. WKM/2020/2019/51, Construction and Completion of a Building for Hawally Pipeline Networks Management in Al-Salam Area , in accordance with the text of Article (60) of Public Tenders Law No. (49) of 2016, with a specified period for bid study. * - The Agency reviewed the entity's letter No. (396) dated 2025/2/20, which included the documents. * Resolution No. (80) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Agency's Board of Directors decided to authorize the entity to prepare a development budget in accordance with Article (60) of Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016 with the lowest bids and provide the Agency with the recommendation.

* **Statement** * **Letter No.:** 3415 * **Letter Date:** 2025/04/07 * **Subject** * Request from the Tenders Committee for their approval of a copy of all documents and information related to System No. (2025/22) submitted by Fawaz Trading and Engineering Services Company regarding Tender No. WKM/2023/2022, Maintenance of Fire Fighting Systems and Replacement of Underground Fire Fighting Water Pipes at Doha East Power Generation and Water Distillation Station , as the committee is considering the subject of the system and deciding on it, which necessitates suspending the procedures related to the resolution regulating it until the matter is decided and the Agency is provided with the necessary resolution. * Resolution No. (81) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Board of Directors of the Agency was informed, and the entity was notified to suspend procedures. * Resolution No. (81) for Meeting No. (32/2025) * The Board of Directors of the Agency was informed, and the entity was notified to suspend procedures.

* **Statement** * **Letter No.:** 2025/828S * **Letter Date:** 2025/04/08 * **Subject** * Request for approval of company names and documents of suppliers and contractors submitted to the General Secretariat of the Central Agency for Public Tenders who have fulfilled the required documents and data for registration according to Articles No. (15) and (16) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. 49 of 2016: * 1- Approved Sukuk General Trading Company * 2- Al-Sayighiya Advanced Group Company for Installation of Cooling and Air Conditioning Systems and Extension of Air Conditioning Pipes W.L.L. * Resolution No. (85) for Meeting No. (32/2025) - Indivisible. - A valid certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage issued by the Public Authority for Manpower must be submitted with the bid. - The bidder must attach a valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority for any of the violations mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector, noting that bids not including this certificate will be excluded. - This announcement serves as an invitation for interested bidders to attend, as companies wishing to attend the bid opening session are entitled to do so. - Companies wishing to attend must coordinate with the Bid Opening Department before the scheduled date of the bid opening session and bring the following documents: 1. Copy of the Civil ID. 2. Copy of the signature authorization for the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. If a person other than the authorized signatory attends, a written letter from the authorized signatory at the Central Agency for Public Tenders must be presented, authorizing whomever they deem fit to attend the session. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempt from the fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with Article (39) of Law No. 74/2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49/2016. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to bid must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development and Care , confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise. - This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. - For more information, please visit our website WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders Central Agency for Public Tenders Decisions | Book Number | Book Date | Agency Board of Directors Decision Approving the Registration of the Above Companies | |:---|:---|:---| | 2025/816S | 2025/04/07 | Request for approval of termination of companies and documents of foreign consulting service providers and applicants to the General Secretariat of the Central Agency for Public Tenders for the documents and data required for registration according to Article No. (15) and (16) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. 49/2016 | | | | C.L.L INFINITY STUDIES --TECHNICAL SOLUTIONS-- | | | | Decision No. (86) for Meeting No. (32 / 25) | | | | The Agency's Board of Directors decided to approve the registration of the above companies |
Before concluding, the Chairman of the Board of the Central Agency for Public Tenders extended an invitation to the esteemed members of the Board to attend the next meeting. The Agency's Board of Directors concluded its work at 4:30 PM.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The esteemed owners of Abdulaziz and Dawood Trading Company ##Company under liquidation. submitted a request to the Department of Partnerships at the Ministry to perform: Dissolution and liquidation of the company, legal entity Limited Liability Company . Anyone with an objection is requested to submit it to the General Court within thirty days from the date of publication of this announcement, in accordance with the usual procedures for filing a lawsuit. The submission of an objection shall result in the continued obligation of the joint partners in the case of a general partnership or simple limited partnership only in the face of this creditor, until their objection is decided by a judicial ruling.

Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting for 2024 (Ooredoo) The Extraordinary General Assembly of National Mobile Telecommunications Company (K.S.C.P.) (Ooredoo) held its meeting at 11:00 AM on Wednesday, March 26, 2025, at the company's main building, chaired by Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Jassim Al Thani (Chairman of the Board of Directors ), and attended by Dr. Yousef Al-Saleeli and Mr. Hilal Al-Khulaifi (Board Members ). The Accountant: The Company's Articles of Association are as follows: Current Text: * The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors composed of (5) members elected by the General Assembly by secret ballot. Any shareholder, whether a natural or legal person, may appoint representatives to the company's Board of Directors in proportion to their shareholding. The number of Board members selected in this manner shall be deducted from the total number of Board members to be elected. Shareholders who have representatives on the Board of Directors may not participate with other shareholders in the election of the remaining Board members except to the extent exceeding the proportion used in appointing their representatives to the Board of Directors . chunks.text_en: a - The company shall maintain a separate account for subscribers in the field of insurance, in accordance with what is stipulated in the company's Articles of Association and regulated by the Board of Directors' decisions, after obtaining the approval of the Fatwa and Sharia Supervisory Board . b - If a deficit appears in the Subscribers' Fund Account, this deficit shall be covered by a Qard Hasan (interest-free loan) from the company's shareholders' equity, provided that the full amount of the loan is recovered from the net insurance surplus in subsequent years. c - Shareholders are entitled to a fee for managing the company's insurance operations, up to a maximum of 20% of the total Takaful insurance revenues, after deducting undistributed general and administrative expenses.
