chunks.title: Held on Wednesday, March 26, 2025
chunks.text_en: The Extraordinary General Assembly of the shareholders of Gas and Oil Fields Services Company – a Kuwaiti Shareholding Company (Closed) – was held on Wednesday, March 26, 2025, at 1:00 PM, at the company's headquarters in East Ahmadi area – Plot 6, Street (1). This meeting was held with the aim of regularizing the company's status and aligning the articles of the Memorandum of Association and Articles of Association with Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its Executive Regulations. The meeting session was chaired by the Chairman of the Company's Board of Directors, Mr. Ali Talib Muqeem . Shareholder attendance was recorded at the appointed time, with the percentage of shares represented at the meeting reaching 100% of the company's total capital. Number of Shareholders Present: * Attendance Rate: 100% * Number of Proxies: 7 After the announcement of the quorum for the validity of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting, the Chairman of the session opened the meeting, welcoming the attendees. He then proceeded to present the items on the agenda of the Extraordinary Assembly. Accordingly, after reviewing the agenda, the following decisions were taken: **First:** The majority of shareholders, owning 60% of the total attendance, approved the amendment of the texts of the following articles of the Company's Memorandum of Association and Articles of Association. Shareholders owning 40% of the total attendance objected to all amended articles in accordance with Article (52) of the Companies Law. Approval was given to amend the text of Article No. (2) of the Articles of Association and (3) of the Memorandum of Association to read as follows: **Article Text Before Amendment:** The company's principal place of business and legal domicile are in the State of Kuwait. The Board of Directors may establish branches, agencies, offices, operations centers, or appoint representatives for it in Kuwait or abroad. **Article Text After Amendment:** The company's principal place of business and legal domicile are in the State of Kuwait, which is the domicile recognized for directing correspondence and judicial notices. A change in this domicile shall not be recognized unless the change is registered in the Commercial Register. The Board of Directors may establish branches, agencies, offices, operations centers, or appoint representatives for it in Kuwait or abroad. **Voting Result:** * Approved: 60% * Disapproved: 40% Approval to amend the text of Article No. (4) of the Articles of Association and (5) of the Memorandum of Association to read as follows: **Article Text Before Amendment:** The purposes for which the company was established are: 1- Carrying out all maintenance operations for oil or gas wells, or oil or gas related facilities, or industries arising from, branching from, or associated with them, onshore or offshore (after obtaining the approval of the Ministry of Energy ). 2- The company may undertake all legal actions, especially concluding commercial and technical agreements with other companies and entering into all associations that

chunks.title: For the Year Ended December 31, 2024
chunks.text_en: The Extraordinary General Assembly of Top Car E-Commerce Company held its meeting on Monday, March 24, 2025, at 11:30 AM, online via the Zoom application. The meeting was chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors, Mr. Khalid Essam Al-Otaibi , with the attendance of: * Mr. Yousef Akram Mustafa , Board Member * Mr. Fahad Abdul Wahab Al-Khaled , Chief Executive Officer * Shareholders with an attendance rate of 84.5% * Mr. Omar Osama Al-Zawawi / representing Gulf Securities Depository Company (Custodian) * Mr. Bader Abdul Jader representing/ Independent Auditor Mr. Waleed Abdul Hameed Al-Sarraf from Ernst & Young Al-Aiban & Al-Osaimi & Partners The Chairman of the Board of Directors opened the meeting, thanking the attendees for accepting the invitation extended to them. He then proceeded to review and discuss the items on the agenda to deliberate on them and make the necessary decisions. **Item One:** Reviewing the company's future status. Unanimous approval was given for the company's continuity by reducing the authorized, issued, and paid-up capital from KWD 1,000,000 to KWD 250,000, a reduction of KWD 750,000, to offset part of the losses amounting to KWD 828,308 as of December 31, 2024, Accordingly, approval was given to amend Article 9 in the Memorandum of Association and Article 5 in the Articles of Association as follows: **Text Before Amendment:** The company's authorized capital was set at KWD 1,000,000 (One Million Kuwaiti Dinars ). Distributed over (10,000,000) shares, with a value of (100 fils) per share, and all shares are cash shares. **Text After Amendment:** The company's authorized capital was set at KWD 250,000 (Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). Distributed over (2,500,000) shares, with a value of (100 fils) per share, and all shares are cash shares. This is subject to the approval of the regulatory authorities. The Chairman of the Board of Directors thanked the attendees and concluded the meeting, as previously mentioned, at 12:00 PM. Approval of the Esteemed Board Members. Chairman of the Board of Directors 3 - Owning the movable and immovable assets necessary to conduct its activities within the legally permitted limits. 4 - Utilizing the financial surpluses available to the company by investing them in financial portfolios and funds managed by specialized entities, whether inside or outside Kuwait. The company shall have the right to conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either as a principal or as an agent. The company may have an interest in or participate in any way with entities that engage in similar activities or that may assist it in achieving its objectives in Kuwait or abroad. It may establish, participate in, acquire, or affiliate with such entities. Article Text After Amendment The purposes for which the company was established are: 1 - 091004 (Maintenance of oil installations, wells, oil refineries, and petrochemicals) 3 - 681091 (Ownership of real estate and movable assets for the benefit of the company). 4 - 649918 (Investment of financial surpluses in financial portfolios managed by specialized companies and entities). Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (10) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment Every share entitles its owner to an equal share of five others without discrimination in the ownership of the company's assets and in the distributed profits as specified hereinafter. Article Text After Amendment 1 - Founders and shareholders are considered members of the company, enjoying equal rights and subject to the same obligations, in accordance with the provisions of the law. 2 - A member of the company shall specifically enjoy the following rights: 1 - Receiving profits and obtaining bonus shares decided for distribution. 2 - Participating in the management of the company through membership in the Board of Directors and attending general assemblies and participating in their deliberations, in accordance with the provisions of the law and the company's articles of association. Any provision in the company's articles of association to the contrary shall be null and void. 3 - Obtaining, at least seven days before the ordinary general assembly meeting, the company's financial statements for the expired accounting period, the Board of Directors' report, and the auditor's report. 4 - Disposing of shares owned by him and priority in subscribing to new shares, bonds, and sukuk in accordance with the provisions of the law and the company's articles of association. 5 - Obtaining a share of the company's assets upon liquidation after settling its debts. 3 - A member of the company shall specifically be obligated to: 1 - Paying the installments due on the shares he owns upon their due dates and paying compensation for delay in payment. 2 - Paying the expenses incurred by the company to collect unpaid installments of the value of his shares, and the company may execute on the shares to satisfy its rights. 3 - Implementing the resolutions issued by the company's General Assembly . 4 - Refraining from any act that would harm the financial or moral interests of the company and committing to compensate for damages arising from violating this. 5 - Following the established rules and procedures regarding share trading. 4 - The shareholder's right to dispose of his shares is subject to the following two restrictions: 1 - A right of priority for shareholders in the company to purchase shares that their owner wishes to sell. 2 - Approval of the Board of Directors for the share buyer. Transactions referred to in Article (172) of the Companies Law are excluded from these two restrictions. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (11) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment Since the shares are nominal, the last owner whose name is registered in the company's register shall be the sole rightful owner to receive the amounts due on the share, whether they are profits or a share in the ownership of the company's assets. Article Text After Amendment 1 - The company shall have a special register kept with a clearing agency , in which the names of shareholders, their nationalities, domiciles, the number and type of shares owned by each of them, and the paid value for each share shall be recorded. 2 - Any changes occurring to the data recorded in the shareholders' register shall be endorsed in accordance with the information received by the company or the clearing agency . Any interested party may request the company or the clearing agency to provide him with data from this register. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (12) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment Capital may not be increased unless the share installments have been fully paid. New shares may not be issued for less than their nominal value. If issued at a higher value, the increase shall first be allocated to cover issuance expenses, then to the reserve or for share amortization. Every shareholder shall have priority in subscribing to a portion of the new shares proportionate to the number of his shares. A period of fifteen days from the date of publishing the invitation to shareholders for this purpose shall be granted for exercising the right of priority. Shareholders may waive their right of priority in advance or restrict this right by any limitation, and the waiver may be for the purpose of establishing an employee stock option plan. Article Text After Amendment The authorized capital of the company may be increased – by a resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly – based on a reasoned proposal from the Board of Directors in this regard, provided that the resolution issued for the capital increase specifies the amount and methods of the increase. The authorized capital may not be increased unless the value of the original shares has been fully paid. The Extraordinary General Assembly may authorize the Board of Directors to determine the date of its implementation. The capital increase shall be covered by shares whose value is paid by one of the following methods: 1 - Offering the increased shares for public subscription. 2 - Converting funds from the optional reserve or from retained earnings or from what exceeds the minimum legal reserve into shares. 3 - Converting a debt on the company or bonds or sukuk into shares. commercial or other laws, his membership shall cease from the date the condition is lost. Representatives of shareholders on the Board of Directors shall have the same rights and duties as elected members, and the shareholder shall be responsible for the actions of his representatives towards the company, its creditors, and its shareholders.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The following conditions must be met by anyone nominated for membership on the Board of Directors: 1. Must possess legal capacity to act. 2. Must not have been previously convicted of a felony involving deprivation of liberty, or a crime of negligent bankruptcy, or fraud, or a crime against honor or trust, or sentenced to a penalty involving deprivation of liberty for violating the provisions of this law, unless his rehabilitation has been restored. 3. Must personally own, or the person he represents must own, a number of shares in the company. 4. Any other conditions stipulated in the articles of incorporation. If a member of the Board of Directors loses any of the aforementioned conditions or the non-profits that allow it to distribute bonuses, in accordance with what was stipulated in the previous paragraph. The Board of Directors shall be obligated to submit an annual report to be presented to the Company's Ordinary General Assembly for approval, provided that it accurately includes a detailed statement of the amounts, benefits, and advantages obtained by the Board of Directors, regardless of their nature or designation. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (26) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers to manage the Company and carry out all actions required for the Company's management in accordance with its objectives. This authority is limited only by what is stipulated by law, these Articles, or any resolutions of the General Assembly. The Board of Directors may sell the Company's real estate, mortgage it, provide guarantees, or contract loans.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers to manage the Company and carry out all actions required for the Company's management in accordance with its objectives. This authority is limited only by what is stipulated by law, these Articles, or any resolutions of the General Assembly. The Board of Directors shall have the right to sell the Company's real estate, mortgage it, provide guarantees, contract loans, borrow, arbitrate, reconcile, or make donations, without referring to the General Assembly. Voting Result: Approved

chunks.title: Voting Result:
chunks.text_en: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (31) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: Each shareholder shall have a number of votes equal to the number of his shares. Representation by proxy is permitted for attending the meeting, and minors and interdicted persons shall be represented by their legal guardians. No member may participate in voting on his own behalf, or on behalf of whom he legally represents, in matters related to a special interest for him or a conflict existing between him and the company.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: Each shareholder, regardless of the number of their shares, has the right to attend the General Assembly, and shall have a number of votes equal to the number of votes allocated to the same class of shares. A shareholder may not vote on their own behalf or on behalf of those they represent in matters related to a special interest for them, or a conflict existing between them and the company. Any condition or decision that violates this shall be null and void. A shareholder may appoint another person to attend on their behalf. The regulatory authority shall issue the required disclosures for proxies related to the attendance of a representative on behalf of the shareholder at the General Assembly.

chunks.title: Voting Result:
chunks.text_en: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (32) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: Shareholders shall register their names in a special register prepared for this purpose at the company's headquarters at least twenty-four hours before the scheduled time for the General Assembly meeting. The register shall include the shareholder's name, the number of shares they own, the number of shares they represent, and the names of their owners, along with the submission of the power of attorney. The shareholder shall be given an attendance card for the meeting stating the number of votes they are entitled to in person and by proxy, and every shareholder has the right to attend the Assembly.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: Every shareholder registered in the company's shareholder register with the clearing agency has the right to attend General Assembly meetings. A shareholder may appoint another person to attend on their behalf by virtue of a special power of attorney or an authorization prepared by the company for this purpose. Anyone claiming a right over shares that conflicts with what is established in the company's shareholder register may apply to the judge of urgent matters to issue an order on petition to deprive the disputed shareholder of voting for a period determined by the judge or until the dispute is settled by the competent court, in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Civil and Commercial Procedures Law .

chunks.title: Voting Result:
chunks.text_en: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (33) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The provisions of the Commercial Companies Law shall apply to the quorum required for the valid convening of the General Assembly in its various capacities and to the majority required for adopting resolutions.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 shall apply to the quorum required for the valid convening of the General Assembly in its various capacities and to the majority required for adopting resolutions. not less than one-tenth of the capital. The invitation to attend the meeting shall be sent, including a clear summary of the agenda and the following attachments: - Registered letters sent to all shareholders at least one week before the scheduled meeting date. - An announcement in at least two daily newspapers published in Arabic, and the announcement must be made twice, provided that the second announcement is made after a period of not less than one week from the date of the first announcement's publication and at least one week before the General Assembly meeting, with the second announcement published in the Official Gazette in addition to the two daily newspapers. The invitation must include the agenda. The founders shall set the agenda for the General Assembly convened as a founding assembly, and the Board of Directors shall set the agenda for the General Assembly convened as an ordinary and extraordinary assembly.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The Annual Ordinary General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors within three months following the end of the financial year, at the time and place specified by the company's articles of association. The Board may invite the General Assembly whenever necessary. The Board of Directors must issue an invitation for the Assembly upon a reasoned request from a number of shareholders representing not less than 10% of the company's capital, or upon a request from the auditor. The inviting party shall prepare the agenda for the meeting. The Chairman of the Assembly, the Secretary of the Board of Directors, and one of the attending minority shareholders shall record the resolutions of the General Assembly and any shareholder observations, ratify them, and submit a report on these resolutions and observations to the Ministry. The provisions related to the Constituent Assembly shall apply to the procedures for inviting the Assembly, and the quorum for attendance and voting. The Ministry must invite the General Assembly to a meeting within fifteen days if the invitation for the Assembly meeting is not issued by the Board of Directors for any reason in cases where the Board is required to invite the Assembly to a meeting. The Ministry shall replace the Board of Directors in taking the necessary procedures for holding the meeting, and may preside over the meeting, unless the Assembly elects one of the shareholders for this purpose.

chunks.title: Voting Result:
chunks.text_en: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (30) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: In cases where the Assembly may be convened at the request of shareholders, auditors, or the Ministry of Commerce and Industry , the agenda shall be prepared by the party requesting the convening of the Assembly, and no matter not included in the agenda may be discussed.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: In cases where the Assembly may be convened at the request of shareholders, auditors, or the Ministry of Commerce and Industry , the agenda shall be prepared by the party requesting the convening of the Assembly. The General Assembly may not discuss matters not included in the agenda unless they are urgent matters that arose after the agenda was prepared or became apparent during the meeting, or if requested by a regulatory authority, the auditor, or a number of shareholders owning five percent of the company's capital. If, during the discussion, it becomes apparent that the information related to some presented matters is insufficient, the meeting must be postponed for a period not exceeding ten working days if requested by a number of shareholders. The Board of Directors may convene this General Assembly whenever it deems fit, and it must convene it whenever requested by a number of shareholders owning no less than one-tenth of the capital. The General Assembly shall also convene if requested by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . Article Text After Amendment The Ordinary General Assembly shall convene, and invitations to its meetings shall be issued in accordance with the provisions and procedures stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (37) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment The General Assembly , convened in ordinary session, shall be competent in all matters related to the company's affairs, except for what the law or these Articles of Association have reserved for the General Assembly convened in extraordinary session or in its capacity as a founding assembly. Article Text After Amendment a. The General Assembly , convened in ordinary session, shall be competent in all matters related to the company's affairs, except for what the law or these Articles of Association have reserved for the General Assembly convened in extraordinary session or in its capacity as a founding assembly. b. The meeting of the General Assembly shall be chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors or his deputy, or by whomever the Board of Directors delegates for that purpose, or by whomever the General Assembly elects from among the shareholders or others. c. The Board of Directors must implement the resolutions of the General Assembly unless those resolutions violate the law, the memorandum of association , or these Articles of Association . The Board of Directors must re-present the violating resolutions to the General Assembly in a meeting called for the purpose of discussing the aspects of the violation. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (38) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment The Board of Directors shall submit to the General Assembly , convened in ordinary session, a report containing a statement on the company's operations and its financial and economic status, the company's budget, a statement of the profit and loss account, a statement on the remuneration of the members of the Board of Directors and the auditors' fees, and a proposal for profit distribution in accordance with the provisions of the Commercial Companies Law . Article Text After Amendment Subject to the provisions of the law, the Ordinary General Assembly shall, in its annual meeting, adopt resolutions on matters falling within its jurisdiction, and in particular the following: 1. The Board of Directors' report on the company's activity and financial position for the ended fiscal year. 2. The auditor's report on the company's financial statements. 3. A report on any violations observed by regulatory authorities and for which penalties were imposed on the company. 4. The company's financial statements. 5. The Board of Directors' proposals regarding profit distribution. 6. Discharging the liability of the members of the Board of Directors . 7. Electing or dismissing members of the Board of Directors , and determining their remuneration. 8. Appointing the company's auditor, and determining their fees or authorizing the Board of Directors to do so. 9. A report on transactions that have occurred or will occur with related parties, and related parties are defined according to International Accounting Principles . Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (39) of the Articles of Association to become as follows: Article Text Before Amendment The Extraordinary General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors , or upon a written request from shareholders representing fifteen percent of the company's issued capital or from the Ministry . The Board of Directors must invite the Extraordinary General Assembly to a meeting within thirty days from the date of the request. If the Board of Directors fails to invite the General Assembly within the period stipulated in the preceding paragraph, the Ministry shall issue the invitation for the meeting within fifteen days from the date of the expiry of the period referred to in the preceding paragraph. Article Text After Amendment a. The provisions related to the Ordinary General Assembly shall apply to the Extraordinary General Assembly , taking into account the provisions stipulated below. financial year for which they were appointed. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (44) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: a. The auditor shall have the powers and obligations stipulated in the Commercial Companies Law and Law No. 5 of 1981 regarding the practice of the auditing profession. In particular, they shall have the right to inspect all the company's books, records, and documents at any time, and to request any data they deem necessary to obtain. They shall also have the right to audit the company's assets and liabilities. If they are unable to exercise these powers, they shall state this in writing in a report submitted to the Board of Directors and presented to the General Assembly , and they shall have the right to convene the General Assembly for this purpose.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: a. The auditor shall, at all times, have the right to inspect all the company's books, records, and documents, and to request any data they deem necessary to obtain. They shall also have the right to audit the company's assets and liabilities. If they are prevented from exercising these rights, they shall state this in writing in a report submitted to the Board of Directors and presented to the General Assembly , and the Ministry shall be notified thereof. b. The auditor shall be obligated to maintain the confidentiality of data and information obtained by virtue of their work, both during and after the termination of their work with the company. They shall not use this data and information to achieve personal gain or for the benefit of others, nor shall they disclose any company-related secrets. If the auditor violates this, they may be dismissed and required to pay compensation if necessary. c. The Board of Directors or a number of shareholders representing twenty-five percent of the issued capital may request the replacement of the auditor during the financial year. Any decision taken regarding their replacement without following the procedures specified in the Executive Regulations shall be null and void. d. In exceptional and urgent cases where the auditor appointed by the General Assembly does not perform their duties for any reason, the Board of Directors may appoint a replacement, provided that this matter is presented at the first meeting convened by the General Assembly for this purpose. e. The auditor may not be a chairman or a member of the Board of Directors of the company whose accounts they audit, nor be entrusted with any administrative work therein, nor supervise its accounts, nor be a relative up to the second degree of anyone supervising the company's management or accounts. Furthermore, they may not purchase or sell shares of the companies whose accounts they audit during the auditing period, or perform any consulting work for the company. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (41) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The following matters shall only be considered by the General Assembly convened in an extraordinary capacity: 1- Amending the Memorandum of Association or the Articles of Association of the company. 2- Selling the entire project undertaken by the company or disposing of it in any other way. 3- Dissolving, merging, converting, or splitting the company. 4- Increasing or decreasing the company's capital. Any amendment to the company's regulations shall not be effective except after the approval of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry .

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: Subject to other competencies stipulated by law, the Extraordinary General Assembly shall be competent to address the following matters: 1- Amending the company's Articles of Association . 2- Selling the entire project for which the company was established or disposing of it in any other way. 3- Dissolving, merging, converting, or splitting the company. 4- Increasing or decreasing the company's capital. Any resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly shall not be effective until the publication procedures have been completed. The Ministry's approval must be obtained if the resolution relates to the company's name, objectives, or capital, with the exception of increasing capital by issuing shares against profits achieved by the company or as a result of adding usable reserves to the capital. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (45) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The auditor shall submit a report to the General Assembly stating whether the balance sheet and profit and loss account are consistent with reality and honestly and clearly express the company's true financial position, whether the company maintains regular accounts, whether the inventory has been conducted in accordance with accepted principles, whether the data contained in the Board of Directors' report are consistent with what is recorded in the company's books, and whether there have been any violations of the company's regulations, legal provisions, or international accounting standards during the financial year that affect the company's activity or financial position, indicating whether these violations are still ongoing, within the limits of the information available to them. The auditor shall be responsible for the accuracy of the data contained in their report in their capacity as an agent. Voting Result: Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (42) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The company shall have one or more auditors from among certified public accountants , appointed by the General Assembly , which shall determine their fees. They shall audit the accounts of the financial year for which they were appointed.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The company shall have one or more auditors from among certified public accountants , appointed by the General Assembly , which shall determine their fees or authorize the Board of Directors to determine them. They shall audit the accounts of the year on behalf of all shareholders, and every shareholder, during the General Assembly meeting, shall have the right to discuss the auditor and seek clarification on what is stated in their report.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: a. The auditor or his representative from the accountants who participated with him in the audit work shall attend the meetings of the Ordinary General Assembly and submit a report on the company's financial statements, and whether these statements show the company's financial position at the end of the financial year and the results of the company's operations for that year, and a statement of whether the data contained in the Board of Directors' report are consistent with what is contained in the company's books and documents, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and what is stipulated by the Law. If the company has more than one auditor, they shall prepare a unified report. In case of disagreement among them on certain matters, this must be stated in the report, along with each of their viewpoints. The report must specifically include the following data: 1. Whether the auditor has obtained the information he deems necessary for performing his duties. 2. Whether the balance sheet and profit and loss accounts are consistent with reality, include all that is stipulated by the Law and the company's articles of association, and honestly and clearly express the company's true financial position. 3. Whether the company maintains regular accounts. 4. Whether the inventory was conducted in accordance with established principles. 5. Whether the data contained in the Board of Directors' report are consistent with what is contained in the company's books. 6. Whether there have been violations of the provisions of the Law or the company's articles of association during the financial year, with a statement of whether these violations are still ongoing, within the limits of the information available to him. 7. Any other data specified by the Executive Regulations. b. The auditor shall be responsible for the financial data contained in his report and for any damage incurred by the company, shareholders, or third parties due to errors committed by him during and because of his work. If the company has more than one auditor, they shall be jointly and severally liable unless one of them proves his non-participation in the error leading to liability. The auditor shall also be responsible for damages incurred by the company as a result of his resignation at an inappropriate time. Any shareholder may, during the Ordinary General Assembly meeting, discuss with the auditor and seek clarification from him regarding what is stated in his report. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% **Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (47) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:**

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: Net profits shall be distributed as follows: First - (10) ten percent shall be deducted and allocated to the statutory reserve account, unless the Articles of Association specify a higher percentage. The General Assembly may cease the deduction if the reserve exceeds half of the company's capital. Second - (1) percent shall be deducted and allocated to the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences account. Third - A percentage shall be deducted and allocated to the optional reserve account, proposed by the Board of Directors and approved by the General Assembly. This deduction shall cease by a resolution of the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors. Fourth - A portion of the profits shall be deducted based on a proposal from the Board of Directors and decided by the General Assembly to meet the company's obligations under labor laws. These funds may not be distributed to shareholders. Fifth - The necessary amount shall be deducted to distribute a first share of profits amounting to (5) five percent to shareholders, as determined by the Board of Directors and decided by the General Assembly. Sixth - After the foregoing, an amount decided by the Ordinary General Assembly shall be deducted, not exceeding (10) ten percent of the remainder, to be allocated for Board of Directors' remunerations. Seventh - The remaining profits shall then be distributed to shareholders as an an additional share in profits, or carried forward to the next year based on a proposal from the Board of Directors, or allocated to establish a general reserve fund or an extraordinary depreciation fund.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: a. Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors, a percentage of not less than ten percent of the net profits shall be deducted to form a statutory reserve for the company. The General Assembly may cease this deduction if the statutory reserve exceeds half of the company's issued capital. b. The Ordinary General Assembly must decide to deduct a percentage of the profits to meet the company's obligations under labor and social insurance laws. A special fund may be established to assist the company's workers and employees. c. Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors, a percentage not exceeding ten percent of the net profits may be deducted to form an optional reserve allocated for purposes determined by the General Assembly. d. The General Assembly, based on a proposal from the Board of Directors, may distribute profits to shareholders at the end of the financial year. For this distribution to be valid, it must be from real profits, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and must not affect the company's paid-up capital. e. 1% shall be deducted and allocated to the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences account from the company's net profits. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% **Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (49) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:**

chunks.title: Text of the Article Before Amendment
chunks.text_en: The reserve fund shall be used, based on a resolution of the Board of Directors, in what is most beneficial to the company's interests. The statutory reserve may not be distributed to shareholders, but it may be used to ensure a profit distribution to shareholders of up to (5) five percent in years when the company's profits do not allow for this limit. If the statutory reserve exceeds half of the company's capital, the General Assembly may decide to use the excess for purposes it deems beneficial to the company and its shareholders.

chunks.title: Text of the Article After Amendment
chunks.text_en: The statutory reserve may only be used to cover company losses or to ensure a profit distribution to shareholders at a rate not exceeding five percent of the paid-up capital in years when the company's profits do not allow for this distribution, due to the absence of an optional reserve that permits this percentage of profits to be distributed. What was deducted from the statutory reserve must be returned to it when the profits of subsequent years allow, unless this reserve exceeds half of the issued capital. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% **Approval of the amendment to the text of Article No. (51) of the Articles of Association to become as follows:** K.S.C. (Closed) Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting Held on Wednesday, 26/03/2025 The Extraordinary General Assembly of the shareholders of Kout Food Group K.S.C. was held at the premises of "Kout Food Group Company , Al-Qibla Area , Al-Sour Street , Sayyid Yaqoub Al-Humaidhi Building , Third Floor, Meeting Room" at 01:30 PM on Wednesday, March 26, 2025. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Faisal Ali Al-Osaimi , Chairman of the Meeting – Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors, and attended by Mr. Talal Yousef Al-Harbi , from Deloitte & Touche (Al-Wazzan & Co.) , the company's auditors. After reviewing the attendance percentage of shareholders, both in person and by proxy, Mr. Faisal Ali Al-Osaimi , Chairman of the Meeting – Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors, announced the availability of the legal quorum for the valid convening of the Extraordinary General Assembly, as the number of shares present in person and by proxy reached 36.381%, totaling 64,496,907 shares of the company's shares. Mr. Faisal Ali Al-Osaimi , Chairman of the Meeting – Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors, opened the meeting session by welcoming the attendees, then proceeded to discuss the agenda items. The Chairman of the Meeting read the Board of Directors' report, then presented the agenda items for voting, whereupon the following was approved by unanimous vote of the shareholders present and represented at the meeting: 1) Approval of the Board of Directors' proposal to reduce the company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital from KWD 6,691,869.500 (Six Million, Six Hundred Ninety-One Thousand, Eight Hundred Sixty-Nine Kuwaiti Dinars and Five Hundred Fils) to KWD 6,678,194.500 (Six Million, Six Hundred Seventy-Eight Thousand, One Hundred Ninety-Four Kuwaiti Dinars and Five Hundred Fils) by canceling KWD 13,675 (Thirteen Thousand, Six Hundred Seventy-Five Kuwaiti Dinars) through the cancellation of 136,750 treasury shares owned by the company in its capital, in accordance with the procedures followed by the Capital Markets Authority , and to authorize the Chairwoman of the Board of Directors and the Chief Executive Officer to take all necessary actions, sign all requests and documents of any nature, and undertake all required procedures in this regard. 2) Approval of the Board of Directors' proposal to amend the text of Article (6) of the Articles of Incorporation and the text of Article (5) of the Articles of Association, as follows, and to authorize the Chairwoman of the Board of Directors and the Chief Executive Officer to take all necessary actions, sign all requests and documents of any nature, and undertake all required procedures in this regard. **Text before amendment:** The company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital is set at KWD 6,691,869.500 (Six Million, Six Hundred Ninety-One Thousand, Eight Hundred Sixty-Nine Kuwaiti Dinars and Five Hundred Fils), distributed over 66,918,695 shares (Sixty-Six Million, Nine Hundred Eighteen Thousand, Six Hundred Ninety-Five Shares) at a value of one hundred fils per share, and all shares are cash shares. **Text after amendment:** The company's authorized, issued, and paid-up capital is set at KWD 6,678,194.500 (Six Million, Six Hundred Seventy-Eight Thousand, One Hundred Ninety-Four Kuwaiti Dinars and Five Hundred Fils), distributed over 66,781,945 shares (Sixty-Six Million, Seven Hundred Eighty-One Thousand, Nine Hundred Forty-Five Shares) at a value of one hundred fils per share, and all shares are cash shares. Approval of the amendment to the text of Article (52) of the Articles of Association to read as follows: **Text of Article before amendment** The company shall be dissolved for one of the reasons stipulated in the Commercial Companies Law. **Text of Article after amendment** The company shall be dissolved for one of the reasons stipulated in Companies Law No. 1/2016 and its Executive Regulations. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article (53) of the Articles of Association to read as follows: **Text of Article before amendment** The company's assets shall be liquidated upon its dissolution in accordance with the provisions contained in the Commercial Companies Law. **Text of Article after amendment** The company's assets shall be liquidated upon its dissolution in accordance with the provisions contained in Companies Law No. 1/2016 and its Executive Regulations. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article (54) of the Articles of Association to read as follows: **Text of Article before amendment** The provisions of Commercial Companies Law No. (25) of 2012 and its amendments contained in Law (97) of 2013 and its Executive Regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the company's Articles of Incorporation or Articles of Association, except after the approval of the competent regulatory authorities. **Text of Article after amendment** The provisions of Commercial Companies Law No. (1) of 2016 and its amendments and its Executive Regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the company's Articles of Incorporation or Articles of Association. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% Approval of the amendment to the text of Article (69) of the Articles of Association to read as follows: **Text of Article before amendment** The founders declare: * Firstly – that the provisions of the Articles of Incorporation and Articles of Association conform to the model stipulated in Article (69) of the Commercial Companies Law. * Secondly – that they have subscribed to all shares. * Thirdly – that they have appointed the necessary administrative bodies to manage the company, and the first administrative body of the company shall be selected at the first meeting of shareholders acting as a founding assembly. **Text of Article after amendment** The founders declare: * Firstly – that the provisions of the Articles of Incorporation and Articles of Association conform to the Law. * Secondly – that they have subscribed to all shares. * Thirdly – that they have appointed the necessary administrative bodies to manage the company, and the first administrative body of the company shall be selected at the first meeting of shareholders acting as a founding assembly. **Voting Result:** Approved: 60% Disapproved: 40% The meeting concluded at 1:30 PM on the same date of convening. Chairman of the Session Undersecretary of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry

chunks.title: Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee <orig>لجنة المواليد والوفيات</orig> held on 23/3/2025, No. 69, formed by Ministerial Resolution No. 2021/1650
chunks.text_en: After reviewing the requests submitted for amending data in the births and deaths registers, the Committee, after reviewing Article 15 of Law No. 36/69 and formed by Ministerial Resolution No. 2021/1650, decided: * Dr. / Head of the General Medical Council - Chairman * Mr. / Director of the Central Births and Deaths Registry Department - Member * Ms. / Head of the Research and Opinion Section - Member * Mr. / Head of the Registration Section - Member * Mr. / Head of the Deaths Section - Member * Mr. / Head of the Records Section - Member Ms. Alia Al-Saffar acted as the Committee's Secretary. The Committee reviewed requests from No. ((128)) to No. ((142)) in the attached list concerning data amendments in births and deaths registers for dropped birth and death registration requests. The Committee, after reviewing and ensuring the completeness of the documents and papers, decided to announce the amendments and requests in the Official Gazette (Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ) in two consecutive issues. If, after the legal period (fifteen days) from the date of the last announcement, no objection is submitted, the Committee will decide to issue the birth and death certificates.

chunks.title: Practice No. (1-2026/2025)
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Justice announces to companies and specialized institutions in this field its need for the determination and maintenance of licenses for the Ministry of Justice's Senior Management Data Project, in accordance with the terms and specifications outlined in the practice documents, which can be obtained from the Tenders Department - located in the Ministries Complex Building, Block (14), Third Floor - Financial Affairs Department, during official working hours, starting from Sunday, 13/04/2025. A subscription fee of (75 K.D.) only, seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars (non-refundable), is required. Offers must be submitted to the Tenders Department at the Ministry, accompanied by an initial security deposit of (2%) of the total bid value, provided that this security remains valid throughout the bid validity period (90) days. The deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Monday, 21/04/2025. Director of Financial Affairs Department

chunks.title: Practice No. (2-2026/2025)
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Justice announces to companies and specialized institutions in this field its need for the supply, installation, and equipping of mobile security rooms for the Ministry of Justice buildings, in accordance with the terms and specifications outlined in the practice documents, which can be obtained from the Tenders Department - located in the Ministries Complex Building, Block (14), Third Floor - Financial Affairs Department, during official working hours, starting from Sunday, 13/04/2025. A subscription fee of (75 K.D.) only, seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars (non-refundable), is required. Offers must be submitted to the Tenders Department at the Ministry, accompanied by an initial security deposit of (2%) of the total bid value. Please note that the bid opening procedures will take place at 11:00 AM on Monday, 05/05/2025, at the Ministries Complex Building, Third Floor, at 11:00 AM on Monday, 05/05/2025. Furthermore, a preliminary meeting will be held at the Ministry's headquarters in the Financial Affairs Department, located in the Ministries Complex Building, Block (14), Third Floor, at 11:00 AM on Monday, 28/04/2025. Whether the bidder attends this meeting or not, they will be bound by all decisions or recommendations resulting from the meeting, if any. Director of Financial Affairs Department

Sales Center Supervisor Announcement of Real Estate Public Auction The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Thursday, 8/5/2025, in Hall 52 - Second Floor, Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2023/102 Sales/3, Filed by: 1- Masha'el Mubarak Saad Al-Qaba 2- Shu'a Mubarak Saad Al-Qaba Against: 1- Sa'da Mohammed Al-Mukhalaf . 2- Director of the Real Estate Registration Department , in his capacity. 3- Undersecretary of the Ministry of Justice , in his capacity. 4- Kuwait Credit Bank . First: Property Description: According to the attached description certificate. - Property Document No. 3851/1983, located in Al-Jahra area - Block 3 - Plot No. 105 - from Plan No. T.M. / 325-2/11/3 - 3B - with an area of 750 m². For Inspection: On 17/10/2022, the committee moved to Al-Jahra area, Block 3, Al-Qasr , Street 3, Avenue 6, Plot 105, House 2, from Plan No. (T.M. / 325-2/11/3 - 3B), automated dispute property number 14266117, to inspect the disputed property on site, in the presence and guidance of the two plaintiffs personally, and with the assistance of Mr. Khair Al-Diraya / Saad Mubarak Al-Mutairi , who was sought for assistance by the department at the time of inspection, as follows: - The disputed property is a beige stone-clad house consisting of a ground floor and a first floor, located on two corner streets, with adjacent neighbors on the remaining sides. The property has two annexes on the two facades of the house, from the side street and from the front street. There is a large setback for the property from the front street, and there is an internal courtyard within the property. - The disputed property conforms in terms of general and specific specifications to Document No. 3851, registered on 24/4/1983, with an area of 750 m², as stated in the deed of ownership by inheritance and reciprocal relinquishment issued by the Ministry of Justice , Real Estate Registration and Documentation Department . - The ground floor consists of 5 rooms, 3 bathrooms, a kitchen, a large hall, and another hall with a preparation counter. - The annex located on the front street consists of 2 rooms, 2 bathrooms, and a kitchen, and is rented to others. The two plaintiffs explained its components to the expert. - The first floor consists of two apartments rented to others. The apartment located on the side street consists of a hall, two rooms, a bathroom, and a kitchen. The apartment located on the front street consists of 3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, and a kitchen. The two plaintiffs explained the components of both apartments to the expert. - The air conditioning in the disputed property is central on the ground floor, and split units and window air conditioners on the first floor and in the annexes. - The expert asked the two plaintiffs about the layout of the property subject of the lawsuit, and they clarified that it is in the possession of the first defendant, who lives there with her son, and that her son and his family have been paying them rent in recent years, and there are also tenants in the property as stated. Second: Auction Terms: First: The auction shall commence with a base price of KWD 526,500 (Five Hundred Twenty-Six Thousand Five Hundred Kuwaiti Dinars ). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of the price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Execution Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the conclusion of the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session, based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifth: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by the deposit of its full value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, execution procedure expenses amounting to KWD 200, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication expenses for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in accordance with the law and at the request of those directly involved in the sale procedures, and at their responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any liability. Announcement of Real Estate Sale by Public Auction The Registry Department at the General Court announces the public auction of the two properties described below, on Thursday, 8/5/2025 – Hall 52 – on the second floor of the Palace of Justice at 9:00 AM – in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2024/148 Sales/3 . Filed by: Jameela Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . Against: The heirs of the late/ Munira Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan , namely: 1- Munira Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 2- Hajeera Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 3- Amani Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 4- Nawaf Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 5- Khalid Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 6- Sara Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan . 7- Khazna Hudaib Marzouq Al-Shafnaa . 8- Saad Mohammed Saad Al-Baidan .

chunks.title: First: Property Descriptions: According to the attached description certificates.
chunks.text_en: **Property One:** * Property Document No. 2003/546 located in Farwaniya – Plot No. (31), Block No. (1) of Plan No. 27305, with an area of 710 m2, at a base price of K.D. 1,400,000 (One Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). * Note: There is a violation of balcony enclosure on all floors. * The building consists of 4 floors, with 4 identical apartments on each floor. Each apartment consists of (a hall + a room + a bathroom + a kitchen), noting that apartment rents vary from K.D. 190-220 per month, according to the plaintiff's agent's statement. * The rooftop is vacant. * The expert recorded his observations in preparation for estimating the value of the property subject to the lawsuit.

chunks.title: *Inspection (for Property One):*
chunks.text_en: The expert on 3/1/2023 moved to Farwaniya area, Block 2, Street 131, Building No. 31, Plot 7335 – Automated Number 90569902, to inspect one of the properties subject to the lawsuit, in the presence of the reading expert , the plaintiff's agent, the agents of the third, fifth, sixth, seventh, and eighth defendants, and the agents of the first, second, fourth, and eighth defendants, or their representatives, regarding attendance verification number, and the following was found: * The property is a building located at the intersection of (3 streets), including Al-Ghazali Street , and consists of a mezzanine and 4 identical floors. * The ground floor (mezzanine) has two apartments, each containing (a room + a hall + a kitchen + a bathroom + a guard's room with a bathroom). * The ground floor has a shop leased as a restaurant with a monthly rent of K.D. 1300, according to the plaintiff's agent's statement. **Property Two:** * Property Document No. 2006/678 located in Farwaniya – Plot No. (35), Block No. (73) of Plan No. 31585, with an area of 768 m2, at a base price of K.D. 1,650,000 (One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). * Note: There is a violation of increased shop area according to the Municipality's letter . * The expert at the same session moved to the second property subject to the lawsuit in Farwaniya area, Block 2, Street 81, Plot 31, Automated Number (82231358), with the same attendees as for the inspection of Property One, and the following was found: * The property is a building located on two streets (a wide alleyway ) and consists of a mezzanine and 6 identical floors. * The ground floor (mezzanine) contains 4 apartments, detailed as 2 apartments consisting of (a hall + a room + a bathroom + a kitchen). * And one apartment consisting of (a hall + 2 rooms + a bathroom + a kitchen) and a studio apartment (a room + a bathroom + a kitchen). * The ground floor has a shop leased as a salon with a monthly rent of K.D. 700, according to the plaintiff's agent's statement. * The building consists of 6 floors, each floor containing 4 identical apartments. Each apartment contains (a hall + 2 rooms + a bathroom + a kitchen), noting that apartment rents vary from K.D. 210-230 per month, according to the plaintiff's agent's statement. * The rooftop is vacant.

chunks.title: Second: Auction Terms:
chunks.text_en: * **First:** The auction shall commence at the base price specified for each property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of the price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . * **Second:** The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the commencement of the sale session. * **Third:** If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit one-fifth of the price at which the auction was awarded. * **Fourth:** If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be resumed in the same session based on the price at which the auction was awarded. * **Fifth:** If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one else comes forward to bid, the bidding shall immediately resume at their expense in that session with any offer not accompanied by the deposit of its full value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. * **Sixth:** In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to K.D. 200, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. * **Seventh:** This announcement shall be published according to the law, and bidders are requested to follow the sale procedures of the Code of Civil Procedure . * **Eighth:** The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. **Notice:** 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette according to Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, according to Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property to be sold as a tenant by force of law, the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court 31 December 2023, and then the fair market value of equity rights in the company Mahmoud Haider and Sons General Trading and Contracting Co. W.L.L. . The total value of Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Company LLC as of December 31st is KWD 7,294,211 (seven million, two hundred ninety-four thousand, and eleven Kuwaiti Dinars). The fair market value of the share of Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah in Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Company LLC as of December 31, 2023, is KWD 72,942.11 (seventy-two thousand, nine hundred forty-two Kuwaiti Dinars and eleven fils). KWD 7,294,211 ÷ 100 = KWD 72,942.11, with the necessity of drawing attention to the following: a) There is an outstanding debit balance due from Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah amounting to KWD 1,341,000, which was received from the company's data and is included within the balance of related party debtors as of December 31, 2023. b) According to the documents reviewed, submitted by the management of Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Company LLC , a document dated December 27, 2020, was reviewed, which is a limited liability company dissolution contract by virtue of an appellate court judgment. The legal opinion issued by the Minister of Justice on June 14, 2022, was adopted, by virtue of which the share of Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah was amended to become 33 shares instead of one share. Accordingly, if this amendment is adopted, the total value of those 33 shares would be KWD 2,407,089.63 (two million, four hundred seven thousand, eighty-nine Kuwaiti Dinars and sixty-three fils). Consequently, the net amount allocated to Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah becomes KWD 1,066,089.63 (one million, sixty-six thousand, eighty-nine Kuwaiti Dinars and sixty-three fils) after deducting the outstanding debit balance due from him as of December 31, 2023. In accordance with the court's decision regarding the appointment of the expert dated November 23, 2023, which is for the valuation of only one share, we have valued the company and determined the value of one share for Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah . The matter is left to the court panel to consider the documents and/or other issued judgments. The comparable study should be read in light of the required costs related to preparing a study to determine the fair value of the shares owned by Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah in Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Company LLC as of December 31, 2023. The comparable study was prepared based on the technical and financial information provided by the management of Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Company LLC as of the date of the comparable study, without any independent verification and/or thorough examination of the unknown aspects of that data/information. The study is considered confidential, and no one has the right to quote it in whole or in part, refer to it, or cite and the buying and selling of lands and real estate solely for the company's account.

chunks.title: 1.6. Valuation Standard
chunks.text_en: The valuation standard used is Fair Market Value, which is defined as "the price at which property would change hands between a willing buyer and a willing seller, neither being under any compulsion to buy or to sell, and both having reasonable knowledge of relevant facts."

chunks.title: 1.7. Professional Standards
chunks.text_en: The present valuation and report have been prepared in accordance with the professional standards of the National Association of Certified Valuation (Analysts) , where appropriate procedures (in our professional opinion) were followed for the scope of work requirements, given the time available to complete the valuation report task.

chunks.title: 1.8. Valuation Date
chunks.text_en: The valuation was conducted as of December 31, 2023.

chunks.title: 1.9. Valuation Conclusion
chunks.text_en: Based on our analysis of the information presented in this report, the underlying assumptions, and the Net Asset Value method, the Net Asset Value as of December 31, 2023, was concluded. chunks.title: 1.2. Subject and Mandate:
chunks.text_en: Pursuant to the judgment issued in Lawsuit No. 159/2023 Sales / 3 dated November 23, 2023, the Court ruled, prior to deciding on the merits of the case, to appoint an auditor (expert) accredited by the Capital Markets Authority . The expert's mandate is to review the case documents and any documents submitted by the litigating parties or other authorized persons to determine the nature and current market value of the share owned by Mr. Maitham Mahmoud Haji Haidar Abdullah in Mahmoud Haidar & Sons General Trading and Contracting Co. W.L.L. as of December 31, 2023, and to prepare a detailed report thereon.

chunks.title: 1.3. Procedures:
chunks.text_en: On January 18, 2024, Ms. Rabia Saad Al-Muhanna , Licensed Auditor, Category A, License No. 152, appeared in her capacity as the esteemed Court's secretary and took the oath. The assigned mandate was received, and the case file and documents were photographed to commence the mandate in execution of the Court's judgment issued on November 23, 2023.

1.1 Income Approach - The Income Approach is based on the economic principle of expectation. In this approach, the value of the investment subject to valuation is the present value of the economic revenues expected to be collected by the investment for valuation purposes. The measurement of economic revenues analyzed can be defined in several ways. 5.1.2 Market Approach - The Market Approach is a method of determining an indicator of the value of a business, a business ownership interest, a security, or an intangible asset using one or more methods that compare the subject with similar businesses, ownership interests, securities, or intangible assets that have been generally sold. This can be accomplished by comparison with companies that offer their shares for public trading or by analyzing an actual transaction of similar sold businesses. It may also include analyzing previous transactions in the company's shares (if any). 5.3.1 Asset Approach - The Asset Approach is based on the value of net assets less liabilities, as commonly used. This approach begins with the book values of the balance sheet as close as possible to the valuation date, and after including assets and liabilities, including those not recorded, at fair value. Different valuation methods can be used, including one or more of the three categories of valuation approaches in any valuation study. All applicable methods must be considered, provided that the most reliable method or methods are chosen for valuing the properties subject to valuation and that are appropriate for the valuation study. 5.2 Chosen Approach/Method - The following valuation approach we adopted to determine the fair value of equity in the company is the Asset Approach (Net Asset Value Method ), given that Mahmoud Haider and Sons General Trading and Contracting Company W.L.L. has no current operating elements and its value lies in its net current assets and investments. 5.3 Asset Approach (Net Asset Value Method) - The fair value of equity in Mahmoud Haider and Sons General Trading and Contracting Company W.L.L. was determined using the Net Asset Value Method under the Asset Approach , detailed as follows: !! Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Thirdly: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the auction shall be re-held at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the auction shall be re-held in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder does not deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the auction shall be immediately re-held at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No bid in this session shall be considered unless accompanied by the deposit of its full value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the sale price of the shares. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the fees for amending the company contract, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to K.D. 200, legal and expert fees, and the costs of advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in application of the law, and bidders are requested for the sale procedures at their own responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the Court of First Instance bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: Partners have the right to object to the joining of the bidder who submitted the best offer, within three days from the date the court approves their offer. Ninthly: The company and any of the partners may - even before the auction award judgment is issued - settle the debt of the debtor partner to the seizing creditor. Tenthly: The company may - even before the auction award judgment is issued - recover a portion of the seized shares to the extent sufficient to settle the creditor's debt.
