chunks.title: Announcement of Property Sale by Public Auction
chunks.text_en: The Department of Clerks at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Wednesday, 2025/5/7, in Hall - 48 - on the second floor of the Palace of Justice at 9:00 AM - this is in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2021/368 Sales/2 . Filed by: Warba Island Real Estate Company LLC Against: The heirs of the late / Abdulredha Hassan Abdullah Al-Shatti , namely: 1- Ahmed Abdulredha Hassan Abdullah Al-Shatti . 2- Rasha Abdulredha Hassan Abdullah Al-Shatti . 3- Rawabi Abdulredha Hassan Abdullah Al-Shatti . 4- Rawan Abdulredha Hassan Abdullah Al-Shatti . 5- Narjis Abdullah Uloom Dashti . 6- The Undersecretary of the Ministry of Justice for Real Estate Documentation Affairs , in his capacity. Property Description: As stated in the Municipality Description Certificate . - Property Document No. 2697/2005 located in Al-Rawda area - Plot No. 22 - Block No. 1 - Plan No. 27263, with an area of 1000 m2, by public auction with a base price of / 1,600,000 K.D. (One Million Six Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** It was stated in the description certificate that the property is located within a private residential area and consists of a basement, ground floor, first floor, second floor, and roof, according to license No. 1290/2001. ** And according to the expert's report: The property in dispute is located in Al-Rawda area, Block No. (1), Street No. (12), House No. (11), Plot No. (226), Automated Address Number (90181094), Automated Unit Number (10674965). The inspection was carried out as follows: . The property in dispute conforms in terms of general and specific specifications to document No. 2697 dated 2005/3/19, and its area is 1000 square meters. . The property in dispute is a private residence (house) overlooking two streets (Damascus Street and internal street No. (12)), bordered on both sides by neighbors. The exterior cladding of the property in dispute overlooking street No. (12) is from Al-Saihma , while the annex overlooking Damascus Street is from Al-Saiha and tiles. The air conditioning of the property in dispute is central, and there is an electric elevator and an internal staircase leading to all floors. The property has parking spaces on two sides overlooking Damascus Street and internal street No. (12). The property in dispute consists of a basement (not built on the entire plot), a ground floor, an annex, a first floor, and a quarter of a second floor, with the following details: The basement consists of two diwaniyas , two storerooms, a kitchen, a bathroom, and washbasins. The ground floor consists of two rooms with a bathroom (master), a hall, a kitchen, and another large hall. There is also a courtyard in the middle of the property, as the construction of the property in dispute is on three sides, with a swimming pool, a shower room, and a bathroom. The first floor consists of three rooms with a bathroom (master), a preparatory kitchen, and a hall. There is also another room with a bathroom and a large hall. The second floor (quarter floor) consists of a room with a bathroom and a laundry room. The first defendant personally stated that the current occupants of the property are the fifth defendant, Ms. Narjis Abdullah Dashti , the second defendant, Ms. Rasha Abdulredha Hassan Al-Shatti , and the first defendant personally, Mr. Ahmed Abdulredha Hassan Al-Shatti . He also stated that his children visit him three times a week and they have rooms in the property in dispute, and that it is not rented to others and does not generate income. Secondly: Auction Conditions: Firstly: The auction shall commence with the base price indicated for the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Department of Execution at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the bidder whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by a full deposit of the auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder does not deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward to bid with the ten percent increase, the bidding shall be immediately reopened based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session, and no bid in this session shall be considered valid unless accompanied by a full deposit of its value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any decrease in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the fees for transfer and registration of ownership, execution procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures do so at their own responsibility, without the Department of Clerks at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates any ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is appealable within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the person whose property has been expropriated is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court whose ownership was expropriated residing in the property shall remain in it as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent. President of the General Court Announcement of Sale of Two Properties by Public Auction The Department of Clerks at the General Court announces the public auction of the two properties described below, on Wednesday, 2025/5/7, in Hall - 48 - on the second floor of the Palace of Justice at 9:00 AM - this is in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 368/2021 Sales/2 . Filed by: National Bank of Kuwait Against: Awlad Khalid Al-Fulaij General Trading and Contracting Company , Salah Khalid Fulaij Al-Fulaij Description of the First Property: As stated in the Municipality's Description Certificate. 1- As per Document No. 14997/2012, located in Bneid Al-Qar area – Plot No. 27 – Piece No. 2 – from Plan No. M/38711, with an area of 612 m². This will be offered in a public auction with a base price of K.D. 1,200,000 (One Million Two Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** The description certificate indicates a violation, which is an increase in the building area on the ground floor. ** According to the expert's report The property subject of the lawsuit, located in Bneid Al-Qar area – Piece 2 – Street 76 – and according to the Kuwait Finder program, Plot No. 142 – Automated Number 90034617, was inspected as follows: The property subject of the lawsuit conforms in terms of general and specific specifications to Document No. 14997/2012 and has an area of 2612 m². The property subject of the lawsuit is an investment building in an old structural condition – dilapidated – and overlooks two streets, at the corner of Street No. 76, with neighbor service from two sides. The exterior cladding is blue Sigma and it has one entrance. The property subject of the lawsuit consists of a basement, ground floor, and 6 repeated floors, in addition to an external shop. The expert found that the property is unused and abandoned, and the plaintiff's agent stated that the property is abandoned and he has no knowledge of its components. Mr. Knowledge Expert stated that he can estimate the value of the property subject of the lawsuit, land and building, and the quarter , based on the external inspection of the property. Description of the Second Property: As stated in the Municipality's Description Certificate. 2- Document No. 5946/2008, located in Salmiya area – Piece 89 – Plot No. 4 – from Plan No. M/39661, with an area of 3577 m². This will be offered in a public auction with a base price of K.D. 16,867,000 (Sixteen Million Eight Hundred Sixty-Seven Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** The description certificate indicates a violation: Upon inspection, it was found that a commercial complex consisting of a basement + ground floor + first floor + second floor + 3-8 floors + roof has violations: on the right side of the basement, there are non-conforming shops, and also on the front side of the basement, there are four shops instead of one. On the first floor, there is exploitation of distributors, and also from floors 8-1, they do not conform to the plans, and there is an increase in construction. ** According to the expert's report The property subject of the lawsuit, located in Salmiya area – Piece 4 – Salem Al-Mubarak Street – Plot 89 – Complex 57 – Automated Number 90244016, was inspected as follows: - The property subject of the lawsuit conforms in terms of general and specific specifications to Document No. 5946/2008 and has an area of 3577.2 m². - The property subject of the lawsuit is a commercial complex named Al-Wataniya Complex , in an old structural condition, overlooking 3 streets and an independent alleyway, with parking spaces. The exterior cladding is granite, and it has 4 main entrances serving the commercial shops, in addition to 2 towers, each with one external entrance. - The property subject of the lawsuit is a commercial complex with commercial shops, consisting of a basement, ground floor, first floor, and 2 towers. The attendees representing the plaintiff provided details of the property's components as follows: 1- The Commercial Complex with Commercial Shops - The basement consists of 18 commercial shops. - The ground floor consists of 21 commercial shops, 11 of which have an external entrance and an entrance inside the complex. - The first floor consists of 18 commercial shops. - There are 7 shops on the external facade of the complex. - The air conditioning is central, and there is one elevator serving the commercial shops, reaching from the basement to the first floor, in addition to 2 escalators + one staircase reaching only from the ground floor to the basement. 2- The Two Towers - Each tower has an external entrance and an internal entrance to the commercial shops. - Each tower consists of 7 floors, and each floor contains 3 offices in a duplex system (two floors). - There is one elevator for each tower connecting the ground, first, third, fifth, and seventh floors. - The expert found that the commercial complex is utilized and leased as commercial shops, while the two towers are utilized and leased as commercial offices. The quarterly rent for the property is K.D. 70,450. Secondly: Auction Terms: Firstly: The auction shall commence at the base price indicated for the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the bidder whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, otherwise the bidding will be resumed in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by a deposit of the full price. In this case, the bidding will be resumed in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder does not deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid for the price, the bidding shall immediately be resumed at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no bid not accompanied by a full deposit of its value shall be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees. and private legal fees, boundaries, and expenses for advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the law and calls for bidding on the described property in a public auction. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at the market rent ." Chief Justice of the Court of First Instance

Fifth: If the first bidder does not deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid for the price, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by the deposit of its full value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property price and the costs of advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Registry Department of the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Additionally, in the other side yard towards the front facade, there is an annex consisting of a room and a bathroom, and another side annex towards the rear facade consisting of a room and a bathroom. Behind the fence at the rear facade of the property, there is a garden and shaded parking with interlocking tile flooring, shaded towards the back alley overlooking Rumaithiya Secondary School and the electricity transformer. - The property has central air conditioning. - The property has three entrances: the first is a front entrance at the facade overlooking Street No. 100, the second is a side entrance towards the yard overlooking the neighbor's house No. 25, and the third is at the rear facade overlooking the alley. - The disputed property consists of a ground floor, first floor, and a quarter floor, with the following details: - The ground floor consists of a large hall with a dining room, washbasins, and a bathroom, a small hall, and a kitchen with a laundry room and refrigerators. The first floor consists of a hall and two suites. The first suite consists of two master bedrooms with their bathrooms, and the second suite consists of a room and a bathroom, and a master bedroom with its bathroom and a balcony overlooking the rear facade of the property towards the alley. - The quarter floor on the roof consists of a maid's room and a bathroom. - Property finishes: The flooring on the ground floor and stairs is marble, and the rest of the property's flooring is ceramic. The property's lighting consists of spotlights and chandeliers, and the ceilings are gypsum. The plaintiff stated that the adjacent property, house No. 25, belongs to the late / Hamoud Abdullah Al-Shaiji .

chunks.title: Inspection:
chunks.text_en: On 5/2/2024 CE, the disputed property was visited and inspected on site, in the presence and under the guidance of the plaintiff's agent, and despite the proper notification of the other parties, they were absent. With the assistance of Mr. Expert of Knowledge , the inspection was conducted as follows: - The disputed property is located in Fahaheel area, Block 10, Street 12, Plot 11208, Building 40, Automated Number 13784377. - The disputed property is a government house consisting of two and a half floors and is located on a single street. - The exterior cladding of the property is Sigma Beige. - We were unable to enter and inspect the property from the inside. - Mr. Expert of Knowledge estimated the total value of the disputed property through inspection. Second: Auction Terms: First: The auction shall commence at a basic price of / KWD 745,200 (seven hundred forty-five thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The successful bidder must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If the successful bidder does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was initially awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by a deposit of the full price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifth: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward to bid with a ten percent increase accompanied by a deposit of the full value, the defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to KWD 200, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Registry Department of the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Secondly: The person whose bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the person whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was previously awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no bid not accompanied by the deposit of its full value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, execution procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in accordance with the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Department of Clerks at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is appealable within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the person whose ownership has been dispossessed is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent ." There is a violation, which is a canopy on the roof. *Inspection: The disputed property located in Al-Andalus area – Plot 10 – Street 2 – House 231 – bearing automated number 15009737, was inspected as follows: -The house consists of a ground floor and a first floor, and a large structure (Kirby) was built on the roof. -There are 3 entrances to the house: an entrance to the Diwaniya , an entrance to the ground floor, and a side entrance leading to the stairs and the first floor. -It was found that the property was previously divided by a prepared division, whereby the plaintiff utilizes the ground floor, and the defendant utilizes the first floor and the Diwaniya . -It was revealed that the entire ground floor is rented by the plaintiff, as the tenant of this entire floor and Diwaniya admitted to paying a monthly rent of 850 K.D. , but she refused to have her name recorded in the minutes. -The ground floor consists of a hall + two apartments + a reception hall + washbasins + a bathroom + a kitchen + a maid's room + a laundry room + a courtyard. -The hall floors are ceramic, the ceilings are gypsum board, and the maid's room and laundry room floors are tile. -The first floor consists of: a hall + a kitchen + a storage room + 3 apartments. -The first apartment on the first floor consists of: a corridor with a small preparatory kitchen + two rooms + a bathroom (occupied by the family of the parties' son). -The second apartment on the first floor consists of: two rooms + two bathrooms. (Occupied by the family of the parties' daughter). -The rooms in both apartments have parquet flooring, hidden gypsum board ceilings, and the corridor floors are marble-colored ceramic, with plain painted walls. -The hall floors are ceramic, and the wall has hidden gypsum board decor with hidden lighting. -The third apartment on the first floor consists of: a bedroom + a bathroom + an external washbasin. Its floors are ceramic, and the ceiling is plain gypsum board. (Occupied by the defendant and his wife). -The kitchen: its floors are ceramic, and the walls are decorated with stones and ceramic. -The external stairs are made of tile. -The roof has a hall made of Kirby and a preparatory kitchen with a glass facade wall and a glass door leading to an indoor garden where the floors are tiled with artificial grass and outdoor seating (chairs and tables) has been placed, utilized by the plaintiff. -There is also a Kirby room on the roof for the defendant's son, containing a bedroom and a bathroom. Its floors are tile, and the bathroom is plain ceramic. -The Diwaniya : a hall + a small washbasin + a small bathroom. Its floors are ceramic, the walls are plain painted, and the bathroom has old commercial porcelain. It has been utilized as a storage room. -The defendant acknowledged that the house is divisible and that the plaintiff has been benefiting from the ground floor for four years for 850 K.D. monthly, and he rents the apartments on the first floor to his children. The inspection revealed that the first apartment is occupied by the defendant's son and his wife, and the second apartment by the defendant's daughter and her husband. (One Million Two Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). **Second:** Property Document No. 2019/8359 located in Hawally – Plot No. 45 – Parcel 19 – from Plan No. 19263 – with an area of 763 m², by public auction at a base price of 1,000,000 K.D. According to the Certificate of Description. The expert visited the properties subject to the dispute located in Hawally area on 8/5/2017, with the assistance of Mr. Knowledge Expert / Khalid Al-Khudair , and under the guidance of the plaintiffs' agent and their authorized representative, to inspect the properties in preparation for their valuation. The inspection was carried out as follows: **Property One:** It is an investment building located in Hawally area, Plot 145, Parcel 4, Street 16, branching off Ibn Khaldoun Street , with an area of 912 m². The building is old, with normal air conditioning. The building facades are made of limestone with paint and it does not contain an elevator. The expert noted that the apartments are occupied and independently used by others, and internal inspection was not possible. Fourth: In the event that the bidder whose offer was approved deposits at least one-fifth of the price, the sale shall be postponed with a ten percent increase. Fifth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session who accepts to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be re-conducted in the same session based on this price. Sixth: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be re-conducted on their account based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no offer not accompanied by a full deposit of its value shall be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Seventh: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Eighth: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are requested to proceed at their own responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Ninth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates any ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure stipulates that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at the market rent ." chunks.text_en: Property document No. 2010/8902 located in Abdullah Al-Mubarak Suburb – Plot No. 5 – Parcel 183 – Plan M/38235, with an area of 400 m². Note: There is a violation report, which is / excess construction on the roof estimated at 25m². According to the description certificate, on 26/7/2023, the expert moved to the dispute site in Abdullah Al-Mubarak Sabah area – Plot 5 – Street 508 – House 3 – Parcel 183 – Automated Number 16258049 – with an area of 400 m², in the presence of Mr. Expert / Al-Diraya Abdul Rahman Al-Habib and under the personal guidance of the defendant, while the plaintiff (violator) was absent. The expert was unable to enter the house at the time of inspection as follows:- 1- The property subject to the dispute is a private residence located on a corner, with neighbors on two sides. It has 4 entrances, two of which are main entrances leading to the ground floor, the third entrance leads to the diwaniya and kitchen and the staircase leading to the rented second floor, and the fourth leads to the kitchen. 2- The property subject to the dispute consists of a ground floor, first floor, second floor, and a roof. The exterior cladding is Sigma Beige, and the house has central air conditioning. The property includes a staircase and an electric elevator. 3- The ground floor consists of 2 halls, 2 bathrooms, a living room, a master bedroom, a main kitchen, a diwaniya , a driver's room, and a bathroom. 4- The first floor consists of 4 master bedrooms, 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, and a preparatory kitchen. 5- The second floor consists of two rented apartments that the expert was unable to inspect as they are rented. a- The first apartment consists of 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, a living room, and a preparatory kitchen. b- The second apartment consists of 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, a living room, and a preparatory kitchen. 6- The roof consists of a staircase house, a room, a bathroom, and a storage room made of Kiri . 7- Mr. Expert Al-Diraya inspected the property in preparation for estimating its current market value and submitting a report thereon.

chunks.text_en: First: The auction shall commence at a base price of KWD 387,000 (Three Hundred Eighty-Seven Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) for the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of the price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If they do not deposit The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any deficit in the property's value. Sixth: The successful bidder shall in all cases bear the fees for dissolution and registration of ownership, the expenses of execution procedures amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and the costs of advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in application of the law and is requested by those directly involved in the sale procedures, at their own responsibility, without the Department of Clerks at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Firstly: Property Description: (According to the attached Description Certificate) Document No. 2007/21244 located in Salwa area - Plot No. 50 - Block No. 10 - Plan No. 33808 - with an area of 1000 m² for the amount of K.D. 721,710 (Seven Hundred Twenty-One Thousand Seven Hundred Ten Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** According to the attached Description Certificate: - The property is a private residence consisting of a basement + ground floor + first floor + second floor and a roof, with a violation being the construction of a side annex from concrete and light materials, and fencing of the ground floor balcony from the rear and front sides of the division. ** According to the Expert's Report: - The property subject of the lawsuit conforms to the general and specific specifications of Document No. 2007/21244 with an area of 1000 m². - The property subject of the lawsuit is a building - a private residence in an old structural condition, overlooking two streets - the front and back of Fahaheel Expressway and Street No. 1. It is bordered by a neighbor on one side and a neighbor on the other side. The exterior cladding is stone. The property has one entrance overlooking Street 1 and three entrances overlooking Fahaheel Expressway . - The property subject of the lawsuit consists of a basement, ground floor, first floor, second floor, roof, courtyard, and annex. The expert knocked on the door, but no one answered. The fourth defendant and the plaintiff's agent stated that they would not be able to enable the expert to inspect the interior of the property subject of the lawsuit. - The fourth defendant provided information about the components of the property subject of the lawsuit from the outside and stated the following:- . The basement is divided into two parts. . Ground floor up to the second floor: Each floor consists of two identical apartments in terms of components, with each apartment consisting of a hall + 3 rooms + 2 bathrooms + kitchen. . The roof has no components. . The annex: consists of a guard's room in addition to a part consisting of a room + kitchen + bathroom. . The air conditioning is central, and there is no elevator in the property. - The study expert stated that he would be able to estimate the value of the property subject of the lawsuit and the quarter + second + roof ** And according to the Expert's Report The property subject of the dispute, as it exists, located in Al-Salam area, Block No. (2) - Street No. (214), House No. (30). The inspection was carried out as follows: -1 The property subject of the dispute conforms to the general and specific specifications of Document No. (4231) dated 12/4/1998 and an area of 500 m² - Automated No. 15620421. -2 The property subject of the dispute is a private residence house overlooking one street and bordered by a neighbor on three sides. We knocked on the door, but there was no response, and the plaintiff's agent was unable to enable the expert to inspect the interior of the property. Therefore, we decided to take the specifications and inspect the property from the outside. -3 The property subject of the dispute consists of a basement, ground floor, first floor, and second floor, and the property's condition is good. -4 The exterior cladding is white stone. -5 The expert could not ascertain the possessor or beneficiary of the property or its use. -6 The study expert inspected the property from the outside in preparation for estimating its value as land and buildings and a quarter, and the special report for that was submitted. Secondly: Auction Terms: Firstly: The auction shall commence at the basic price indicated in the expert's report. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the bidder whose bid was approved fails to deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full price. In this case, the bidding shall be re-opened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding shall immediately be re-opened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No bid in this session shall be considered valid unless accompanied by the deposit of its full value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the ownership fees, procedural and enforcement expenses, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement shall be published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures shall do so at their own responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall be considered a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent ." The disputed property is a relatively old house consisting of a ground floor, first floor, and a rooftop, an internal corner location, with external brick cladding, two entrances to the house, unit air conditioning, and tiled floors. - The disputed property is used as bachelor accommodation, as the ground floor has 15 rooms, 4 bathrooms, and 3 kitchens. The side door has been closed and used as a room, and the garage is used as a room. - The first floor has 13 rooms, 9 of which are large, 3 bathrooms, and kitchens along the corridor throughout the house. A bridge structure has also been built in the middle of the courtyard. - The expert asked the landlord, Mr. Khorshid Alam Asadullah , about the rental value, and he stated that the rental value is 750 K.D. as of 10/7/2021. - The plaintiff submitted a portfolio of documents and acknowledged that the quarterly rent was 1250 K.D. before the deceased's death and up to the date of her death, and that the current rent of 750 K.D. received by the third defendant is not correct, and there is a discrepancy in the rent amounts, which are not fixed. - The study expert recorded his observations in preparation for estimating the property's value and the quarterly rent. It is bordered by a neighbor to the west and a sandy area to the east. It consists of a quarter basement, a ground floor, a mezzanine, and 3 upper floors. The exterior is finished with stone cladding (brown color) and Sigma paint (white color). It is fully finished and has been connected to electricity for over a year, with no active businesses operating at the time of opening. It contains a total of 226 commercial shops for sale (as a whole) and not for rent, as stated by the agent of the first defendant. The details are as follows:

Article (7) The company's fiscal year shall begin on the first of January and end on the last of December of each year. An exception to this is the first fiscal year, which shall begin from the date of the company's registration in the Commercial Register and end in December of the following fiscal year. Article (8) The company's authorized capital is set at K.D. 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only), distributed over 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand) shares, with each share valued at K.D. 1 (One Dinar). All 2,400,000 shares are valued at K.D. 2,400,000. The issued capital is K.D. 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only). The company's capital consists of cash shares paid as follows: – The paid-up capital is K.D. 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only), paid by the company to Warba Bank pursuant to the letter issued on 06/08/2024. Article (9) The founders who signed the contract subscribed to the full capital of the company as follows: Founders' Contributions to Company Capital | No. | Shareholder Name | Number of Shares | Value in Kuwaiti Dinars | |:-----|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Faisal Bader Al-Sayer Trading and Contracting Company | 780000 | 780000 | | 2 | Musaed Bader Al-Sayer and Sons General Trading and Contracting Company | 780000 | 780000 | | 3 | Istijlab Holding Company | 800000 | 800000 | | 4 | Bader Faisal Bader Mohammed Al-Sayer | 20000 | 20000 | | 5 | Nasser Musaed Bader Al-Sayer | 20000 | 20000 | | | Total | 2400000 | 2400000 |
The founders acknowledge that they have deposited the cash capital of the company at a rate of (100%) of the company's cash capital from the total value of the shares they subscribed to, amounting to (2,400,000), each according to their subscription percentage, with Warba Bank pursuant to the certificate issued by the said bank and dated (06/08/2024), attached to the original of this contract. Article (10) Expenses, wages, disbursements, and costs that the company is committed to paying due to its establishment, in accordance with the statement attached to the original contract, shall be deducted from the general expenses account. Article (11) The founders who signed this contract undertake to carry out all necessary procedures to complete the establishment of the company and commit to excluding and canceling fictitious and duplicate subscription requests and taking the necessary measures to achieve this. For this purpose, they have authorized Mr. Nasser Musaed Bader Al-Sayer to take legal actions, complete the necessary documents, and introduce any amendments that official authorities deem necessary to be included in the contract or its attached Articles of Association. Within three months from the date of completion of the subscription and before the constituent assembly meeting, the founders must submit to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry a statement detailing the number of shares subscribed to by each founder, their payment of the due installments, and their completion of * 402 of 2019 dated 04/02/2019 * 2607 of 2022 dated 06/10/2022 * 274 of 2024 dated 22/01/2024 The ownership of a limited liability company named and addressed as Imtiyazat Al-Sayer Holding Company , with a capital of K.D. 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars), has been confined between the parties to this contract, pursuant to the amendment contract documented under No. 274 of 2024. The founders acknowledge their legal capacity to establish a Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company (Imtiyazat Al-Sayer Holding Company ) among themselves and their commitment to the rules stipulated for its establishment in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments and executive regulations. They also acknowledge their commitment to all rules stipulated for the establishment of the company in accordance with the provisions of the following articles: – Article (1) The preceding preamble is considered an integral part of this contract. Article (2) The founders acknowledge that the company was established in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. (1) of 2016 and its amendments, its executive regulations, and the provisions of this contract. The company shall acquire legal personality from the date of registration. It shall not be permitted to commence its activities except from the date of registration and the approval of all regulatory bodies for its engagement in the activity. Article (3) Company Name / Imtiyazat Al-Sayer Holding Company and its commercial address is a Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company. Article (4) The company's main center is located in the State of Kuwait and its domicile is in the State of Kuwait . The partners may transfer the main center to any other location within Kuwait, and open branches and agencies for the company inside and outside the State of Kuwait. The company shall have the right to conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either as a principal or as an agent. The company may also engage in similar, complementary, necessary, or related activities to its stated purposes. The company's headquarters shall be considered its legal domicile for directing correspondence and judicial notices to it, and its data shall be recorded in the Commercial Register. Any change to this headquarters shall not be recognized until it is registered in the Commercial Register. Article (5) The company's duration is unlimited, commencing from the date of registration. Article (6) Without prejudice to the provisions of Decree-Law No. 1/2016 concerning Companies and its amendments – and its executive regulations, the purposes for which the company was established are: 1 – Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities. 2 – Owning real estate and movable assets necessary for conducting its business within the limits permitted by law. 3 – Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes, and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation percentage in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than 20 percent. 4 – Owning intellectual property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchise rights, and other intangible rights, and exploiting them.

chunks.title: Article (12)
chunks.text_en: The founders of the company shall be jointly and severally liable among themselves for compensating any damage incurred by the company, its shareholders, or third parties due to the invalidity of the company's contract.

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: Al-Sayer Privileges Holding Company Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company (K.C.S.C.) Closed Company Articles of Association Establishment of the Company A – Elements of Company Establishment

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: The company was established in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments and executive regulations, and these Articles of Association, between the shareholders whose provisions are set forth hereinafter, as a Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company (K.C.S.C.) .

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: The company's head office and legal domicile are in the State of Kuwait , which is the address to be relied upon for directing correspondence and judicial notices. Any change to this domicile shall not be recognized unless the change is registered in the Commercial Register . The Board of Directors may establish branches, agencies, or offices for it within the State of Kuwait or abroad.

chunks.title: Article (3)
chunks.text_en: The duration of this company is unlimited and commences from the date of registration.

chunks.title: Article (4)
chunks.text_en: Without prejudice to the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments and executive regulations, the purposes for which the company was established are: 1 – Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities. 2 – Owning real estate and movable assets necessary for conducting its business within the limits permitted by law. 3 – Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes, and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than 20 percent. 4 – Owning intellectual property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchises, and other intangible rights, and exploiting and leasing them to its subsidiary holding companies and others. The company shall have the right to conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either directly or by agency. The company may also engage in activities that are common, complementary, necessary, or related to its stated purposes.

chunks.title: Article (5)
chunks.text_en: B – Capital and Methods of Increasing It

chunks.title: Article (5)
chunks.text_en: The company's issued capital is set at (K.D. 2,400,000) (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only), distributed over (2,400,000) (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand shares), with each share valued at (K.D. 1) (One Dinar ). The company's authorized capital is set at (K.D. 2,400,000) (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only). The company's capital consists of cash shares paid as follows: Paid-up capital (K.D. 2,400,000) (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only) was paid by the company to Warba Bank as per the certificate issued by the said bank dated 06/08/2024 (and attached to the original of this contract). The company's capital is set at K.D. 2,400,000 (Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only).

chunks.title: Article (6)
chunks.text_en: The company's shares are foreign (may be owned) by non-Kuwaitis in accordance with the provisions of the law and relevant ministerial decisions.

chunks.title: Article (7)
chunks.text_en: The authorized capital may not be increased unless the value of the original shares has been fully paid. The Extraordinary General Assembly may authorize the Board of Directors to determine the date of its implementation. However, by a resolution of the company's Board of Directors , the issued capital may be increased within the limits of the authorized capital, provided that the issued capital has been fully paid. New shares may not be issued for less than their nominal value.

chunks.title: Article (8)
chunks.text_en: If the value of the original shares has been fully paid, the company's authorized capital may be increased by a resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly after obtaining the approval of the regulatory authorities , based on a reasoned proposal from the Board of Directors and a report from the auditor in this regard, provided that the resolution issued for the capital increase specifies the amount and methods of the increase.

chunks.title: Article (9)
chunks.text_en:
The company's capital is set at K.D. 2,400,000 – Two Million Four Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only. Distribution of Company Shares: Distribution of Company Shares | No. | Name of Shareholder | Number of Shares | Value in Kuwaiti Dinars | |:-----|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Faisal Bader Al-Sayer General Trading and Contracting Company | 780000 | 780000 | | 2 | Musaed Bader Al-Sayer and Sons General Trading and Contracting Company | 780000 | 780000 | | 3 | Al-Fayyadah Stables Company | 800000 | 800000 | | 4 | Bader Faisal Bader Mohammed Al-Sayer | 20000 | 20000 | | 5 | Nasser Musaed Bader Al-Sayer | 20000 | 20000 | | Total | 2400000 | 2400000 | 2400000 |
If it is decided to increase the company's capital and some shareholders do not exercise their pre-emptive right to subscribe to the capital increase shares, the unsubscribed shares shall be allocated to those shareholders of the company who wish to subscribe. If the subscription requests exceed the number of shares offered, they shall be allocated to the subscribers in proportion to their subscription. In all cases where the new shares are not fully subscribed, the Board of Directors may allocate the unsubscribed shares to new shareholders, and the unsubscribed new shares shall be considered null and void by operation of law.

chunks.title: Article (10)
chunks.text_en: The company may increase its capital through public subscription as stipulated in Article (160) of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments and executive regulations.

chunks.title: Article (11)
chunks.text_en: The shareholder's right to dispose of their shares is restricted by one or both of the following restrictions: 1 . Requiring a pre-emptive right for the company's shareholders to purchase shares that their owner wishes to sell. 2 . Requiring the approval of the Board of Directors for the share purchaser. Dispositions referred to in Article (172) of the Companies Law are excluded from these two restrictions. (This text does not apply to companies listed on the stock exchange). Article (12) (Mandatory text if the first restriction in Article 11 of this Bylaw is adopted) Before disposing of his shares, the shareholder must notify the company of the terms of sale. The disposition of shares shall not be effective until ten days have elapsed from the date of notification without any of the shareholders submitting a request to purchase the shares. If a shareholder submits a request to purchase the shares, it must be done at the price stated in the terms of sale. Article (13) (Mandatory text if the second restriction in Article 11 of this Bylaw is adopted) Without prejudice to the provisions regarding the company's purchase of its own shares, the Board of Directors , in the event of rejecting the buyer's person, must purchase the shares on behalf of the company within ten days from the date the Board is notified of the approval request. In this case, the purchase shall be at the price agreed upon by the shareholder for the sale of his shares. Article (14) Subscription Article (14) The founders who signed the Articles of Association subscribed to the entire capital of the company with shares totaling (2,400,000) (two million four hundred thousand shares) with a nominal value of (one Dinar ) distributed among them, each according to their subscription ratio specified in the Articles of Association . The nominal value of the shares they subscribed to has been paid at Warba Bank as per the attached bank certificate dated 06/08/2024 AD. Article (15) Ownership of a share necessarily entails the shareholder's acceptance of the Articles of Association , the provisions of the company's Bylaws , and the resolutions of its General Assemblies . Article (16) Every share entitles its owner to an equal share without discrimination in the ownership of the company's assets and in distributed profits. Article (17) The company shall have a special register kept with a clearing agency , in which the names of shareholders, their nationalities, domiciles, the number and type of shares owned by each, and the value paid for each share shall be recorded. Any changes occurring to the data recorded therein shall be endorsed in the shareholders' register according to the information received by the company or the clearing agency . Any interested party may request the company or the clearing agency to provide them with the data from this register. Article (18) Founding Assembly Within three months from the date of subscription and before the meeting of the Founding Assembly , the founders must submit to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry a statement detailing the number of subscribed shares, the names and addresses of subscribers, the number of shares subscribed by each, and the value paid thereof. Article (19) The founders must invite the Founding Assembly to convene within three months from the date of completion of the subscription. If the period expires without the Assembly being held, the Ministry of Commerce and Industry shall, within fifteen days from the date of expiry of the said period, convene the Founding Assembly . Article (20) The invitation to attend the meeting of the Founding Assembly , including the agenda, time, and place of the meeting, shall be sent by one of the following methods: 1. Registered letters sent to all subscribers at least two weeks before the scheduled date of the meeting. 2. Announcement, and the announcement must be made twice, provided that the second announcement is made after a period of no less than seven days from the date of publication of the first announcement and at least seven days before the meeting. 3. Hand delivery of the invitation to shareholders or their legal representatives at least one day before the meeting, with an endorsement on a copy of the invitation confirming receipt. 4. Any other modern means of communication specified in the Executive Regulations of the Companies Law . The Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be notified in writing of the agenda, time, and place of the meeting at least seven days before its convening for its representative to attend. The meeting of the Founding Assembly shall be chaired by whomever the Assembly elects for this purpose. Article (21) The meeting of the Founding Assembly shall not be valid unless attended by shareholders with voting rights representing more than half of the subscribed shares. If this quorum is not met, the Assembly must be invited to a second meeting with the same agenda, to be held within a period of no less than seven days and no more than thirty days from the date of the first meeting. The second meeting shall be valid regardless of the number of attendees. A new invitation for the second meeting is not required if its date was specified in the invitation to the first meeting. Resolutions shall be issued by an absolute majority of the shares present at the meeting. Article (22) The founders shall submit to the Founding Assembly a report containing comprehensive information about all incorporation operations and the amounts expended, along with supporting documents. This report shall be placed in a location designated by the founders for subscribers to review, at least seven days before the Assembly meeting, and this shall be indicated in the invitation to subscribers to attend the meeting: Article (23) The Founding Assembly shall be competent to address the following matters: 1. Approving the company's incorporation procedures after verifying their validity and conformity with the provisions of the Companies Law and the company's Articles of Association . 2. Approving the valuation of in-kind contributions, if any, as stipulated in Article (11) of the Companies Law . 3. Electing the first Board of Directors . 4. Appointing the auditor and determining their fees. 5. Declaring the final incorporation of the company. A copy of the minutes of the Founding Assembly meeting, including the resolutions adopted, shall be sent to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . The first Board of Directors must publish the company's Articles of Association and register it in the Commercial Register within thirty days from the date of the final declaration of the company's incorporation. Article (24) Rights and Obligations of Shareholders A member of the company shall specifically enjoy the following rights: - In accordance with its purposes, and this authority shall only be limited by what is stipulated by law, these Bylaws , or the resolutions of the General Assembly . The Board of Directors may sell any of the company's assets and real estate, as well as mortgage them, issue guarantees, conclude loans, donations, gifts, releases, arbitration, reconciliation, and approve controlling participations. **Article (30)** The company may have a Chief Executive Officer appointed by the Board of Directors from among its members or from outside, who shall be entrusted with the management of the company. The Board shall define his/her powers and authorities to sign on behalf of the company. **Article (31)** The Board of Directors may distribute work among its members according to the nature of the company's business. The Board may also delegate one of its members, a committee from among its members, or a third party to perform one or more specific tasks, supervise any aspect of the company's activity, or exercise some of the powers or authorities vested in the Board. **Article (32)** Any shareholder, whether a natural or legal person, may appoint representatives to the company's Board of Directors in proportion to their shareholding. The number of Board members selected in this manner shall be deducted from the total number of Board members to be elected. Shareholders who have participated in the election of the remaining Board members with other shareholders may only do so to the extent exceeding the proportion used for appointing their representatives to the Board. A group of shareholders may form an alliance among themselves to appoint one or more representatives to the Board of Directors, in proportion to their combined ownership. These representatives shall have the same rights and duties as elected members, and the shareholder shall be responsible for the actions of their representatives towards the company, its creditors, and its shareholders. **Article (33)** A meeting of the Board of Directors shall not be valid unless attended by half of the members, provided that the number of attendees is not less than three. A higher percentage or number may be agreed upon. Meetings may be held using modern communication means, and decisions may be passed by circulation with the approval of all Board members. The Board of Directors must meet at least six times during the year, and a greater number of meetings may be agreed upon. **Article (34)** Minutes of the Board of Directors meetings shall be recorded and signed by the attending members and the Board Secretary. Any member who did not approve a decision taken by the Board may record their objection in the meeting minutes. **Article (35)** If a Board member's position becomes vacant, it shall be filled by the shareholder who received the highest number of votes among those who did not win Board membership. If that person is prevented from taking the position, it shall be filled by the next in line. The new member shall complete only the term of their predecessor. However, if the vacant positions reach one-quarter of the original positions, the Board of Directors must call for a General Assembly of shareholders to convene within two months from the date the last position became vacant, to elect those who will fill the vacant positions. **Article (36)** The following conditions must be met by anyone nominated for Board membership: 1. Must possess legal capacity to act. 2. Must not have been previously convicted of a felony with a freedom-restricting penalty or a bankruptcy offense. 1. Receiving dividends and obtaining bonus shares decided for distribution. 2. Participating in the company's management through Board membership and attending General Assemblies and participating in their deliberations, in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Law and these Articles of Association. Any agreement to the contrary is null and void. 3. Obtaining, at least seven days before the General Assembly meeting, the company's financial statements for the past accounting period, the Board of Directors' report, and the Auditor's report. 4. Disposing of owned shares and priority in subscribing to new shares. 5. Obtaining a share of the company's assets upon liquidation after settling its debts. **Article (25)** The member in the company is particularly obligated to: 1. Pay the installments due on their shares upon their due dates and pay compensation for delay in payment. 2. Pay the expenses incurred by the company to collect unpaid installments on the value of their shares, and the company has the right to enforce on the shares to satisfy its rights. 3. Implement the decisions issued by the company's General Assembly. 4. Refrain from any act that harms the financial or moral interests of the company and commit to compensating for damages arising from violating this. **Article (26)** The General Assembly of Shareholders may not do the following: 1. Increase the financial burdens of the shareholder or increase the nominal value of the share. 2. Decrease the percentage of net profits to be distributed to shareholders as specified in the company's Articles of Association. 3. Impose new conditions, other than those mentioned in the Memorandum of Association or these Articles, related to the shareholder's right to attend and vote in General Assemblies. 4. These provisions may be deviated from with the written acceptance of all shareholders or by a unanimous vote involving all shareholders, and by completing the necessary procedures for amending the company's Articles of Association. **Article (27)** A- Board of Directors The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of (5) five members. The term of membership shall be three years, renewable. If it is not possible to elect a new Board of Directors within the specified period, the existing Board shall continue to manage the company's affairs until the reasons cease and a new Board of Directors is elected. **Article (28)** Shareholders shall elect the members of the Board of Directors by secret ballot. The company's Articles of Association may stipulate that a number not exceeding half of the first Board of Directors members shall be elected from among the company's founders. **Article (29)** The Board of Directors shall elect by secret ballot a Chairman and a Vice-Chairman. The Chairman of the Board of Directors shall represent the company in its relations with third parties and before the judiciary, in addition to other powers specified in the Articles of Association. His/her deputies shall be entrusted with managing the company's affairs in its relations with third parties, and he/she must implement the Board's decisions. The Vice-Chairman shall act in place of the Chairman in his/her absence or if he/she is prevented from exercising his/her powers. The Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers to manage the company and perform all acts required for the company's management. 3. To personally own or represent the owner of (20,000) twenty thousand shares of the company's stock. If a member of the Board of Directors loses any of the aforementioned conditions, their membership shall cease.
