Obtaining a share of the company's assets upon liquidation after settling its debts.
Pay the installments due on their shares upon their due dates and pay compensation for delay in payment.
Pay the expenses incurred by the company to collect unpaid installments on the value of their shares, and the company has the right to enforce on the shares to satisfy its rights.
Implement the decisions issued by the company's General Assembly.
Refrain from any act that harms the financial or moral interests of the company and commit to compensating for damages arising from violating this.
Increase the financial burdens of the shareholder or increase the nominal value of the share.
Decrease the percentage of net profits to be distributed to shareholders as specified in the company's Articles of Association.
Impose new conditions, other than those mentioned in the Memorandum of Association or these Articles, related to the shareholder's right to attend and vote in General Assemblies.

These provisions may be deviated from with the written acceptance of all shareholders or by a unanimous vote involving all shareholders, and by completing the necessary procedures for amending the company's Articles of Association.
To personally own or represent the owner of (20,000) twenty thousand shares of the company's stock.

chunks.title: Article (37)
chunks.text_en: Membership in the company's Board of Directors shall not be included within the maximum number of memberships referred to in Article (234) of the Companies Law . A person may be the chairman of the board of directors of more than one closed joint-stock company.

chunks.title: Article (38)
chunks.text_en: The chairman or a member of the Board of Directors, even if representing a natural or legal person, may not exploit information obtained by virtue of their position to gain benefit for themselves or others. A member of the Board of Directors may dispose of their shares in the company during their membership on the Board, without prejudice to the restrictions on the disposal of shares stipulated in the Companies Law , the company's articles of association, or these bylaws.

chunks.title: Article (39)
chunks.text_en: Members of the Board of Directors may not disclose to shareholders, outside of General Assembly meetings, or to third parties, any company secrets they have become aware of due to their management, otherwise they shall be subject to dismissal and held accountable for compensating damages resulting from the violation.

chunks.title: Article (40)
chunks.text_en: The chairman of the Board of Directors or any of its members may not combine membership in the board of directors of two competing companies, or participate in any work that would compete with the company, or conduct business for their own account or for the account of others in any branch of activity carried out by the company, otherwise the company shall have the right to demand compensation from them or to consider the operations they conducted for their own account as if they were conducted for the company's account, unless with the approval of the Ordinary General Assembly.

chunks.title: Article (41)
chunks.text_en: The total remuneration of the chairman and members of the Board of Directors may not exceed ten percent of the net profit after deducting depreciation and distributing a profit of not less than 5% of the capital to shareholders (a higher percentage may be agreed upon). An annual remuneration not exceeding six thousand Kuwaiti Dinars may be distributed to the chairman of the Board of Directors and each member of this Board from the date of the company's establishment until the achievement of profits that allow it to distribute remuneration in accordance with the preceding paragraph. By a resolution issued by the company's General Assembly, an independent board member may be exempted from the aforementioned maximum remuneration limit (if the company has independent members). The Board of Directors is obligated to submit an annual report to be presented to the company's Ordinary General Assembly for approval, which shall accurately include a detailed statement of the amounts, benefits, and advantages received by the Board of Directors, regardless of their nature or designation.

chunks.title: Article (42)
chunks.text_en: No person representing on the Board of Directors, or the chairman, or a member of the Board of Directors, or a member of the executive management, or their spouses, or their second-degree relatives, may have a direct or indirect interest in contracts and dispositions concluded with or on behalf of the company, unless authorized by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly.

chunks.title: Article (43)
chunks.text_en: The company may not lend to any of its Board members, or the CEO, or their spouses, or their relatives up to the second degree, or their affiliated companies, unless there is authorization from the company's General Assembly. Any action taken in violation thereof shall not be enforceable against the company, without prejudice to the rights of good faith third parties. (This provision does not apply to companies authorized to lend).

chunks.title: Article (44)
chunks.text_en: The chairman and members of the Board of Directors are responsible towards the company, shareholders, and third parties for all acts of fraud, abuse of authority, and for any violation of the law or the company's articles of association, or for errors in management. Without filing a liability lawsuit, the General Assembly may not vote to discharge the Board of Directors from liability. Members of the Board of Directors may not participate in voting on General Assembly resolutions related to discharging them from liability for their management, or those related to a special benefit for them, their spouses, or their first-degree relatives, or to a dispute existing between them and the company.

chunks.title: Article (45)
chunks.text_en: The liability stipulated in the preceding article shall be either personal liability affecting a specific member, or joint liability among all members of the Board of Directors. In the latter case, all members shall be jointly and severally liable for paying compensation, except for those who objected to the decision that incurred the liability and recorded their objection in the minutes.

chunks.title: Article (46)
chunks.text_en: The company may file a liability lawsuit against the members of the Board of Directors due to errors that cause damage to the company. If the company is in liquidation, the liquidator shall undertake filing the lawsuit.

chunks.title: Article (47)
chunks.text_en: Any shareholder may individually file a liability lawsuit on behalf of the company if the company fails to do so. In this case, the company must be joined as a party to be awarded compensation if applicable. A shareholder may file their personal lawsuit for compensation if the error has caused them damage. Any agreement to the contrary shall be null and void. B – General Assembly

chunks.title: Article (48)
chunks.text_en: The Ordinary Annual General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors within three months following the end of the fiscal year, at the time and place designated by the Board of Directors. The Board may invite the General Assembly to a meeting whenever necessary. The Board of Directors must issue an invitation for the General Assembly to meet upon a reasoned request from a number of shareholders owning ten percent of the company's capital, or upon a request from the auditor, within fifteen days from the date of the request. The agenda shall be prepared by the inviting party. The provisions related to the founding assembly stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments shall apply to the procedures for inviting the General Assembly, and the quorum for attendance and voting.

chunks.title: Article (49)
chunks.text_en: Every shareholder, regardless of the number of their shares, has the right to attend the General Assembly and shall have a number of votes equal to the number of votes allocated to the same class of shares. A shareholder may not vote on their own behalf or on behalf of whom they represent in matters related to a special benefit for them, or a dispute existing between them and the company. Any condition or resolution contrary to this shall be null and void. A shareholder may delegate another to attend on their behalf by virtue of a special power of attorney or authorization. Any person claiming a right over shares that conflicts with what is recorded in the company's shareholders' register may apply to the judge of urgent matters to obtain an order on petition to deprive the disputed shares of voting rights for a period determined by the judge issuing the order, or until the dispute is settled by the competent court , in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Civil and Commercial Procedures Law . Article (50) The General Assembly meeting shall be chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors , or his deputy, or a person delegated by the Board of Directors for that purpose, or a person elected by the General Assembly from among the shareholders or others. Article (51) Subject to the provisions of the law, the Ordinary General Assembly shall, in its annual meetings, take decisions on matters falling within its jurisdiction, and in particular the following: 1. The Board of Directors' report on the company's activities and financial position for the ended financial year. 2. The auditor's report on the company's financial statements. 3. A report on any violations observed by regulatory authorities and for which penalties were imposed on the company. 4. The company's financial statements. 5. The Board of Directors' proposals regarding profit distribution. 6. Discharging the Board of Directors' members . 7. Electing or dismissing members of the Board of Directors and determining their remuneration. 8. Appointing the company's auditor , determining their fees, or authorizing the Board of Directors to do so. 9. Reporting on transactions that have occurred or will occur with related parties , where related parties are defined according to international accounting principles. Article (52) By a resolution issued by the company's Ordinary General Assembly , the chairman or one or more members of the Board of Directors may be dismissed, or the company's Board of Directors may be dissolved and a new Board of Directors elected, based on a proposal submitted by a number of shareholders owning no less than one-quarter of the company's issued capital . If a resolution is issued to dissolve the Board of Directors, and it is not possible to elect a new Board of Directors at the same meeting, the Assembly may decide either for the existing Board to continue managing the company's affairs until the new Board is elected, or to appoint a temporary administrative committee whose primary task shall be to call the General Assembly to elect the new Board, within one month of its appointment. Article (53) The Ordinary General Assembly may not discuss matters not included in the agenda, unless they are urgent matters that arose after the agenda was prepared or became apparent during the meeting, or if requested by one of the regulatory authorities , the auditor , or a number of shareholders owning five percent of the company's capital. If, during the discussion, it becomes apparent that the information related to some of the presented matters is insufficient, the General Assembly shall adjourn or postpone the meeting for a period not exceeding ten working days if requested by a number of shareholders representing one-quarter of the issued capital . The postponed meeting shall be convened without the need for new invitation procedures. Article (54) The Board of Directors must implement the resolutions of the General Assembly , unless these resolutions violate the law, the articles of association , or these regulations. The Board of Directors must re-present the violating resolutions to the Ordinary General Assembly in a meeting called for the purpose of discussing the aspects of the violation. Article (55) The provisions related to the General Assembly shall apply to the Extraordinary General Assembly , taking into account the provisions stipulated in the following articles: Article (56) The Extraordinary General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors or upon a reasoned request from shareholders representing fifteen percent of the company's issued capital or from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . The Board of Directors must invite the Extraordinary General Assembly to a meeting within thirty days from the date of submitting the request. If the Board of Directors fails to invite the General Assembly within the period stipulated in the preceding paragraph, the Ministry shall call for the meeting within fifteen days from the date of expiry of the period referred to in the preceding paragraph. Article (57) The meeting of the Extraordinary General Assembly shall not be valid unless attended by shareholders representing three-quarters of the company's issued capital . If this quorum is not met, an invitation shall be extended for a second meeting, which shall be valid if attended by those representing more than half of the issued capital . Resolutions shall be issued by a majority exceeding half of the total shares of the company's issued capital. Article (58) Subject to other competencies stipulated by law, the Extraordinary General Assembly shall be competent in the following matters: 1. Amending the company's articles of association . 2. Selling the entire project for which the company was established, or disposing of it in any other way. 3. Dissolving the company, or its merger, transformation, or division. 4. Increasing or decreasing the company's capital . Article (59) Any resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly shall not be effective until the publication procedures are completed. The approval of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be obtained if the resolution relates to the company's name, objectives, or capital. Article (60) Any shareholder may file a lawsuit for the nullity of any resolution issued by the Board of Directors or the Ordinary or Extraordinary General Assembly that violates the law, the company's articles of association , or these regulations, or that is intended to harm the company's interests, and may claim compensation when necessary. Furthermore, resolutions of the Ordinary and Extraordinary General Assemblies that prejudice the rights of the minority may be challenged. The challenge shall be made by a number of the company's shareholders owning fifteen percent of the issued capital , provided they were not among those who approved those resolutions. Article (61) The company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of its subsidiary partners if the following conditions are met: 1. Insufficiency of the subsidiary company's funds to meet its obligations. 2. The company owns a percentage of the subsidiary company's capital that enables it to control the appointment of the majority of the Board of Directors' members or managers, or the resolutions issued by 3. The subsidiary company makes decisions or takes actions that aim to serve the interests of the parent and controlling company and harm the interests of the subsidiary company or its creditors, and these actions are the primary cause of the subsidiary company's inability to fulfill its obligations, unless the closed company is responsible for the subsidiary company's debts based on another reason. Article (62) The company's fiscal year begins on the first of January and ends on the last of December of each year, with the exception of the first fiscal year, which begins from the date of the company's registration in the Commercial Register and ends on December 31 of the following fiscal year. Article (63) Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors , a percentage of not less than ten percent of the net profits shall be deducted to form a statutory reserve for the company. The General Assembly may cease this deduction if the statutory reserve exceeds half of the company's issued capital . The statutory reserve may only be used to cover the company's losses or to ensure the distribution of dividends to shareholders at a rate not exceeding five percent of the paid-up capital in years when the company's profits do not allow for the distribution of this percentage, due to the absence of an optional reserve that permits the distribution of this percentage of profits. What has been deducted from the statutory reserve must be returned to it when the profits of subsequent years allow, unless this reserve exceeds half of the issued capital . Additionally, (1%) shall be deducted and allocated to the account of the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences from the company's net profit. Article (64) Annually, a percentage shall be deducted from the non-net profits, determined by the Board of Directors after consulting the auditor , for the depreciation of the company's assets or to compensate for the decrease in their value. These funds shall be used to purchase necessary materials, machinery, and facilities or to repair them, and these funds may not be distributed to shareholders. Article (65) The General Assembly must decide to deduct a percentage of the profits to meet the company's obligations under labor laws and social insurance. A special fund may be established to assist the company's workers and employees. Article (66) Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors , a percentage not exceeding ten percent of the net profits may be deducted to form an optional reserve allocated for purposes determined by the General Assembly . Article (67) The General Assembly , based on a proposal from the Board of Directors , may distribute profits to shareholders at the end of the fiscal year. For this distribution to be valid, it must be from real profits, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and this distribution shall not affect the company's paid-up capital . Article (68) Auditor The provisions of Articles 227 to 233 of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments shall apply. Article (69) At the end of each fiscal year, the company must prepare a consolidated balance sheet and statements of profit and loss for itself and all its subsidiary companies, accompanied by explanations and data as required by International Accounting Standards . Article (70) Dissolution and Liquidation of the Company The company shall be dissolved by one of the matters stipulated in Articles 266 to 277 of the Companies Law , its amendments, and its executive regulations. Article (71) The liquidation of the company shall be carried out as set forth in the provisions of Articles 278 to 295 of the Companies Law , its amendments, and its executive regulations. Article (72) The provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the Articles of Association or in these Bylaws . Article (73) An original copy of this contract shall be kept at the company's head office and on its website. An original copy of this contract shall also be kept in the company's file with the competent department at the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . Article (74) The Founders declare the following: * First: That they have subscribed to all shares and deposited the amount required by law to be paid from their value in one of the local banks at the company's disposal. * Second: That the in-kind contribution has been valued in accordance with the provisions of the law, and it has been fully paid. * Third: That they have appointed the necessary administrative bodies for the management of the company. * Fourth: Preservation of the supporting papers and documents for the aforementioned declarations with the official instrument . Article (75) This contract was drawn up based on the letter from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry No. [Number] and registered with the Contracts and Companies Control Department at the Ministry of Justice under automated number 6381635 on 2025/02/17. First Party Second Party Third Party Fourth Party Fifth Party And as stated, this contract was drawn up and its occurrence was confirmed after being read to those present. They also acknowledge their commitment to all rules stipulated for the establishment of the company in accordance with the provisions of the following articles:

The auditor's report on the company's financial statements.
A report on any violations observed by regulatory authorities  and for which penalties were imposed on the company.

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: The preceding preamble is considered an integral part of this contract.

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: The founders acknowledge that the company was established in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. (1) of 2016 , its amendments, its executive regulations, and the provisions of this contract. The company shall acquire legal personality from the date of registration. It shall not be permitted to commence its activities except from the date of registration and the approval of all regulatory authorities for its commencement of activity.

chunks.title: Article (3)
chunks.text_en: Company Name / Hay'a Holding Company - A Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company .

chunks.title: Article (4)
chunks.text_en: The company's main office is located in the State of Kuwait and its domicile is in the State of Kuwait. The partners may transfer the main office to any other location within Kuwait, and open branches and agencies for the company inside and outside the State of Kuwait. The company may conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either directly or by agency. The company may also engage in similar, complementary, necessary, or related activities to its stated purposes. The company's headquarters shall be considered its legal domicile for directing correspondence and judicial notices to it, and its data shall be recorded in the Commercial Register . Any change to this headquarters shall not be valid until it is registered in the Commercial Register.

chunks.title: Article (5)
chunks.text_en: The company's duration is unlimited, commencing from the date of registration.

chunks.title: Article (6)
chunks.text_en: Without prejudice to the provisions of Decree-Law No. 1/2016 concerning Companies and its amendments – and its executive regulations, the purposes for which the company was established are: 1- Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities. 2- Owning real estate and movable assets necessary for conducting its business within the limits permitted by law. 3- Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes, and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation percentage in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than twenty percent. 4- Owning intellectual property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchise rights, and other intangible rights, and exploiting and leasing them to its subsidiaries and others. The company may conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either directly or by agency. The company may also engage in similar, complementary, necessary, or related activities to its stated purposes.

clauses.text_en: Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities.
Owning real estate and movable assets necessary for conducting its business within the limits permitted by law.

Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes, and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation percentage in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than twenty percent.
Owning intellectual property rights  such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchise rights, and other intangible rights, and exploiting and leasing them to its subsidiaries and others.

chunks.title: Article (7)
chunks.text_en: The company's fiscal year begins on the first of January and ends on the last of December of each year, with the exception of the first fiscal year, which begins from the date of the company's registration in the Commercial Register and ends in December of the following fiscal year.

chunks.title: Article (8)
chunks.text_en: The company's authorized capital is set at (KWD 2,100,000) only (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), distributed over (Twenty-One Million) shares, with each share valued at (One Hundred Fils). All shares are (cash shares). The issued capital is (KWD 2,100,000) only (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) only. The company's capital consists of cash shares that have been fully paid by the company to the National Bank of Kuwait as per the letter issued on 19/1/2025.

chunks.title: Article (9)
chunks.text_en:
The founders who signed the contract subscribed to the full capital of the company as follows: Founding Shareholders' Subscribed Capital | No. | Shareholder Name | Number of Shares | Value in Kuwaiti Dinars | |:-----|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Riyadh Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 464421 | 464421 | | 2 | Fawaz Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 464421 | 464421 | | 3 | Ahmed Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 353367 | 353367 | | 4 | Faisal Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 353367 | 353367 | | 5 | Sanaa Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 232210 | 232210 | | 6 | Zaid Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 116106 | 116106 | | 7 | Sabika Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 58054 | 58054 | | 8 | Sara Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 58054 | 58054 |
The founders acknowledge that they have deposited the cash capital of the company, amounting to (100%) of the company's cash capital from the total value of the shares they subscribed to, which is (KWD 2,100,000), each according to their subscription percentage, at the National Bank of Kuwait , as per the certificate issued by the aforementioned bank and dated (19/1/2025), which is attached to the original of this contract.

chunks.title: Article (10)
chunks.text_en: The expenses, wages, disbursements, and costs that the company committed to pay due to its establishment amount to (KWD 3,100) according to the statement attached to the original contract, and shall be deducted from the general expenses account.

chunks.title: Article (11)
chunks.text_en: The founders signing this contract undertake to carry out all necessary procedures to complete the establishment of the company and commit to excluding and canceling fictitious and repeated subscription requests and taking the necessary measures to achieve this. For this purpose, they have appointed Mr. Riyadh Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled to take legal actions, complete the necessary documents, and introduce amendments that official authorities deem necessary to be included in the contract or in the attached Articles of Association. Within three months from the date of completion of the subscription and before the constituent assembly meeting, the founders must submit to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry a statement detailing the number of shares subscribed by each founder, their payment of due installments, their names, addresses, the share value, and the amount paid thereof.

chunks.title: Article (12)
chunks.text_en: The company's founders are jointly and severally liable among themselves for compensating any damage incurred by the company, its shareholders, or third parties due to the invalidity of the company's contract.

chunks.title: Company (Kuwaiti Holding)
chunks.text_en: Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company (K.S.C.C.) Closed Company Articles of Association Establishment of the Company A- Elements of Company Establishment

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: The company was established in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. 1/2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. These Articles of Association specify the owners of the shares, whose provisions are detailed hereinafter, as a Kuwaiti Closed Shareholding Company (K.S.C.C.).

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: The company's head office and legal domicile are in the State of Kuwait , which is the address considered valid for directing correspondence and judicial notices. Any change to this domicile is not valid unless registered in the Commercial Register. The Board of Directors may establish branches, agencies, or offices for it within the State of Kuwait or abroad.

chunks.title: Article (3)
chunks.text_en: The duration of this company is unlimited and commences from the date of registration.

chunks.title: Article (4)
chunks.text_en: The purposes for which the company was established are: Without prejudice to the provisions of Companies Law No. 1/2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations: -1 Investing its funds in trading shares, bonds, and other securities. -2 Owning real estate and movable assets necessary for its operations within the limits permitted by law. -3 Financing or lending to companies in which it owns shares or stakes, and guaranteeing them to third parties. In this case, the holding company's participation in the capital of the borrowing company must not be less than twenty percent. -4 Owning intellectual property rights such as patents, trademarks, industrial designs, franchise rights, and other intangible rights, and exploiting and leasing them to its subsidiary holding companies and others.

chunks.title: Article (5)
chunks.text_en: B- Capital and Methods of Increasing It The authorized capital is set at (2,100,000 K.D.) (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Dinars), the issued capital is (2,100,000 K.D.) (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Dinars), and the paid-up capital is (2,100,000 K.D.) (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Dinars). The company's issued capital consists of 21,000,000 cash shares with a value of 2,100,000 K.D. (Two Million One Hundred Thousand Dinars), which have been fully paid.

chunks.title: Article (6)
chunks.text_en: The company's shares are nominal (not permissible) for Kuwaitis to own them in accordance with the provisions of the law and relevant ministerial decisions.

chunks.title: Article (7)
chunks.text_en: The authorized capital may not be increased unless the value of the original shares has been fully paid. The Extraordinary General Assembly may authorize the Board of Directors to determine the date of its implementation, provided that the issued capital may be within the limits of issuing new shares at less than their nominal value.

chunks.title: Article (8)
chunks.text_en: If the value of the original shares has been fully paid, the company's authorized capital may be increased by a resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly after obtaining the approval of the regulatory authorities , based on a reasoned proposal from the Board of Directors and a report from the auditor in this regard, provided that the resolution issued for the capital increase specifies the amount and methods of the increase.

chunks.title: Article (9)
chunks.text_en:
The company's capital is set at 2,100,000 K.D. - Two Million One Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only. Distribution of Company Shares: Distribution of Company Shares | No. | Shareholder Name | Number of Shares | Value in Kuwaiti Dinars | |:--|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Riyadh Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 464421 | 464421 | | 2 | Fawaz Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 464421 | 464421 | | 3 | Ahmed Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 353367 | 353367 | | 4 | Fadhel Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 353367 | 353367 | | 5 | Sana Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 232210 | 232210 | | 6 | Zaid Hamad Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 116106 | 116106 | | 7 | Sara Hamad Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 58054 | 58054 | | 8 | Sabaika Hamad Abdullah Hamad Al-Khaled | 58054 | 58054 | | | Total | 2100000 | 2100000 |
If it is decided to increase the company's capital and some shareholders do not exercise their priority right to subscribe to the increased shares, and the unsubscribed shares are not allocated to interested company shareholders, then if subscription requests exceed the number of offered shares, they shall be allocated to subscribers proportionally to their subscribed amounts. In all cases where the full new shares are not subscribed, the Board of Directors may allocate the unsubscribed shares to new shareholders, and the unsubscribed new shares shall be considered canceled by operation of law.

chunks.title: Article (10)
chunks.text_en: Optional Text A shareholder's right to dispose of their shares is restricted by one or both of the following restrictions: -1 Requiring a priority right for the company's shareholders to purchase shares that their owner wishes to sell. -2 Requiring the Board of Directors' approval for the share purchaser. Dispositions referred to in Article (172) of the Companies Law are excluded from these two restrictions.

chunks.title: Article (11)
chunks.text_en: (Mandatory text if the first restriction of Article 11 of these Articles of Association is adopted) A shareholder must notify the company of the terms of sale before disposing of their shares. The disposal of shares shall not be effective until ten days have elapsed from the date of notification without any of the shareholders submitting a request to purchase the shares. If a shareholder submits a request to purchase the shares, the purchase must be made at the price stated in the terms of sale.

chunks.title: Article (12)
chunks.text_en: (Mandatory text if the second restriction of Article 11 of these Articles of Association is adopted) Without prejudice to the provisions regarding the company's purchase of its own shares, the Board of Directors , in the event of rejecting the buyer, must purchase the shares on behalf of the company within ten days from the date the Board is notified of the request for approval. In this case, the purchase shall be made at the price agreed upon by the shareholder for the sale of their shares.

chunks.title: Article (13)
chunks.text_en: Subscription The founders who signed the Articles of Association subscribed to the entire capital of the company with 21,000,000.00 shares (twenty-one million shares) with a nominal value of K.D. 100.100 (one million one hundred thousand K.D.) distributed among them, each according to their subscription ratio specified in the Articles of Association . K.D. 2,100,000 (two million one hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) of the nominal value of the shares they subscribed to has been paid to the National Bank of Kuwait , as per the attached bank certificate dated 2025/01/19 CE.

chunks.title: Article (14)
chunks.text_en: Ownership of a share necessarily entails the shareholder's acceptance of the Articles of Association , the provisions of the company's Articles of Incorporation , and the resolutions of its General Assemblies .

chunks.title: Article (15)
chunks.text_en: Each share grants its owner the right to an equal share as others, without discrimination, in the ownership of the company's assets and in the distributed profits.

chunks.title: Article (16)
chunks.text_en: The company shall maintain a special register with a clearing agency in which the names of shareholders, their nationalities, domiciles, the number and type of shares owned by them, and the value paid for each share shall be recorded. Any changes occurring to the data recorded in the shareholders' register shall be endorsed therein according to the information received by the company or the clearing agency . Any interested party may request the company or the clearing agency to provide them with the data from this register.

chunks.title: Article (17)
chunks.text_en: Constituent Assembly Within three months from the date of subscription and before the meeting of the Constituent Assembly , the founders must submit to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry a statement detailing the number of subscribed shares, the payment of due installments by subscribers, the names and addresses of subscribers, the number of shares subscribed by each, the value of the share, and the amount paid thereof.

chunks.title: Article (18)
chunks.text_en: The founders must invite the Constituent Assembly to convene within three months from the date of completion of the subscription. If the period expires without the Assembly being held, the Ministry of Commerce and Industry shall, within fifteen days from the date of expiry of the said period, invite the Constituent Assembly to convene.

chunks.title: Article (19)
chunks.text_en: The invitation to attend the meeting of the Constituent Assembly , including the agenda, time, and place of the meeting, shall be sent by one of the following methods: - 1- Registered letters sent to all subscribers at least two weeks before the scheduled date of the meeting. - 2- Announcement, which must be made twice, provided that the second announcement is made after a period of no less than seven days from the date of publication of the first announcement and at least seven days before the meeting. - 3- Hand delivery of the invitation to shareholders or their legal representatives at least one day before the meeting, with an endorsement on a copy of the invitation confirming receipt. - 4- Any other modern means of communication specified in the Executive Regulations of the Companies Law . The Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be notified in writing of the agenda, date, and place of the meeting at least seven days before its convening for its representative to attend. The meeting of the Constituent Assembly shall be chaired by whomever the Assembly elects for this purpose.

chunks.title: Article (20)
chunks.text_en: The meeting of the Constituent 4 / Disposing of shares owned by him and priority in subscribing to new shares. 5 / Obtaining a share of the company's assets upon liquidation after settling its debts. Article (24) A member of the company is particularly obligated to: 1 / Pay the installments due on his shares when they become due and pay compensation for delay in payment. 2 / Pay expenses incurred by the company to collect unpaid installments of his share value, and the company has the right to execute on his shares to satisfy its rights. 3 / Implement the resolutions issued by the company's General Assembly. 4 / Refrain from any act that harms the financial or moral interests of the company and commit to compensating for damages arising from violating this. Article (25) The General Assembly of Shareholders may not: 1 / Increase the financial burdens of the shareholder or increase the nominal value of the share. 2 / Decrease the percentage of net profits to be distributed to shareholders as stipulated in the company's articles of association. 3 / Impose new conditions other than those mentioned in the articles of incorporation or these regulations concerning the shareholder's right to attend and vote in General Assemblies. These provisions may be deviated from with the written consent of all shareholders or by a unanimous vote involving all shareholders, and by completing the necessary procedures for amending the company's articles of association. Article (26) A - Board of Directors The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of (5) members, and the term of membership shall be three years, renewable. If it is not possible to elect a new Board of Directors within the specified period, the existing Board shall continue to manage the company's affairs until the reasons cease and a new Board of Directors is elected. Article (27) Shareholders shall elect the members of the Board of Directors by secret ballot. The company's articles of association may stipulate that no more than half of the first Board of Directors members shall be elected from among the company's founders. Article (28) The Board of Directors shall elect by secret ballot a Chairman and a Vice-Chairman. The Chairman of the Board of Directors shall represent the company in its relations with third parties and before the judiciary, in addition to other powers specified in the articles of association. His signature shall be considered as the signature of the Board of Directors in the company's relations with third parties. He shall implement the Board's resolutions and adhere to its recommendations. The Vice-Chairman shall act in place of the Chairman in his absence or if he is prevented from exercising his powers. Article (29) The company may have a Chief Executive Officer appointed by the Board of Directors from among its members or from others, who shall be entrusted with the management of the company, and whose allowances and powers to sign on behalf of the company shall be determined. Article (30) The Board of Directors may distribute work among its members according to the nature of the company's business. The Board may also delegate one of its members, a committee from among its members, or a third party to perform one or more specific tasks, supervise an aspect of the company's activity, or exercise some of the powers or competencies entrusted to the Board. Article (31) Any shareholder, whether a natural or legal person, may appoint representatives to the company's Board of Directors in proportion to the shares they own in it. The number of Board members selected in this manner shall replace a corresponding number from the total elected Board members. Shareholders who are represented on the Board of Directors may not participate with other shareholders in electing the remaining Board members except to the extent exceeding the proportion used in appointing their representatives to the Board of Directors. A group of shareholders may form an alliance among themselves to appoint one or more representatives for them on the Board of Directors, in proportion to their combined ownership. These representatives shall have the same rights and duties as elected members. The shareholder shall be responsible for the actions of their representative towards the company, its creditors, and its shareholders. Article (32) A meeting of the Board of Directors shall not be valid unless attended by half of the members, provided that the number of attendees is not less than three. A higher percentage or number may be agreed upon. Meetings may be held using modern communication means, and resolutions may be passed by circulation with the approval of all Board members. The Board of Directors must meet at least four times during the year, and a greater number of meetings may be agreed upon. Article (33) Minutes of Board of Directors meetings shall be recorded and signed by the attending members and the Board Secretary. Any member who did not approve a resolution taken by the Board may record their objection in the meeting minutes. Article (34) If a Board of Directors member's position becomes vacant, they shall be succeeded by the shareholder who received the most votes among those who did not win Board membership. If that person is prevented, they shall be succeeded by the next in line. The new member shall complete only the term of their predecessor. However, if the vacant positions reach one-quarter of the original positions, the Board of Directors must call a General Assembly of Shareholders to convene within two months from the date of the last vacancy, to elect those who will fill the vacant positions. Article (35) The following conditions must be met by anyone nominated for Board of Directors membership: 1 - Must not have been previously convicted of a felony with a custodial sentence, or a crime of bankruptcy due to negligence or fraud, or a crime against honor or trust, or a custodial sentence for violating the provisions of the law, unless their rehabilitation has been restored. If a member of the Board of Directors loses any of the aforementioned conditions, their membership shall cease.

chunks.title: Article (36)
chunks.text_en: Membership in the company's Board of Directors shall not be included within the maximum number of memberships referred to in Article (234). A person may be the chairman of the Board of Directors of more than one closed joint-stock company.

chunks.title: Article (37)
chunks.text_en: The Chairman or a member of the Board of Directors, even if representing a natural or legal person, may not exploit information obtained by virtue of their position to gain benefit for themselves or others. A member of the Board of Directors may dispose of their shares in the company during their membership in the Board, without prejudice to the restrictions on the disposal of shares stipulated in the Companies Law , the company's articles of association, or these regulations.

chunks.title: Article (38)
chunks.text_en: Members of the Board of Directors may not disclose to shareholders, outside of General Assembly meetings, or to third parties, any company secrets they have become aware of due to their management of the company. Otherwise, they shall be subject to dismissal and held accountable for compensating damages resulting from the violation.

chunks.title: Article (39)
chunks.text_en: The Chairman of the Board of Directors or any member of the Board may not combine membership in the board of directors of two competing companies, or participate in any work that would compete with the company, or trade for their own account or for the account of others in any branch of activity carried out by the company. If they do so, the company may demand compensation or consider the operations they carried out for their own account as having been for the company's account, unless this is done with the approval of the General Assembly .

chunks.title: Article (40)
chunks.text_en: The total remuneration of the Chairman and members of the Board of Directors may not exceed ten percent of the net profit after deducting depreciation and reserves and distributing a profit of not less than 5% of the capital to shareholders (a higher percentage may be agreed upon). An annual remuneration not exceeding six thousand Kuwaiti Dinars may be distributed to the Chairman of the Board of Directors and each member of this Board from the date of the company's establishment until the achievement of profits that allow for the distribution of remuneration in accordance with the preceding paragraph. By a resolution issued by the company's General Assembly , independent Board members may be exempted from the aforementioned maximum remuneration limit (if the company has independent members). The Board of Directors is obligated to submit an annual report to the company's General Assembly for approval, which must accurately and in detail include the amounts, benefits, and advantages obtained by the Board of Directors, regardless of their nature or designation.

chunks.title: Article (41)
chunks.text_en: No representative on the Board of Directors, nor the Chairman, nor any member of the Board of Directors, nor any member of the executive management, nor their spouses, nor their second-degree relatives, may have a direct or indirect interest in contracts and dispositions concluded with or on behalf of the company, unless authorized by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly .

chunks.title: Article (42)
chunks.text_en: The company may not lend to any of its Board members, the Chief Executive Officer , their spouses, their relatives up to the second degree, or their affiliated companies, unless there is authorization from the company's General Assembly . Any action taken in violation thereof shall not be enforceable against the company, without prejudice to the rights of good faith third parties.

chunks.title: Article (43)
chunks.text_en: The Chairman and members of the Board of Directors are responsible towards the company, shareholders, and third parties for all acts of fraud, abuse of power, and for any violation of the law or the company's articles of association, or for errors in management. A vote by the General Assembly to discharge the Board of Directors from liability shall not prevent the filing of a liability lawsuit. Members of the Board of Directors may not participate in voting on General Assembly resolutions concerning their discharge from liability or those related to a special benefit for them, their spouses, or their first-degree relatives, or a dispute existing between them and the company.

chunks.title: Article (44)
chunks.text_en: The liability stipulated in the preceding article shall be either personal liability attaching to a specific member, or joint liability among all members of the Board of Directors. In the latter case, all members shall be jointly and severally liable for compensation, except for those who objected to the decision that incurred the liability and recorded their objection in the minutes.

chunks.title: Article (45)
chunks.text_en: The company may file a liability lawsuit against the members of the Board of Directors due to errors that cause damage to the company. If the company is in liquidation, the liquidator shall undertake to file the lawsuit.

chunks.title: Article (46)
chunks.text_en: Any shareholder may individually file a liability lawsuit on behalf of the company if the company fails to do so. In this case, the company must be joined as a party to the lawsuit so that compensation may be awarded to it if warranted. A shareholder may also file their personal lawsuit for compensation if the error has caused them damage. Any agreement to the contrary shall be null and void.

chunks.title: Article (47)
chunks.text_en: B – General Assembly The Ordinary Annual General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors within three months following the end of the fiscal year, at the time and place designated by the Board of Directors. The Board may invite the General Assembly to a meeting whenever necessary. The Board of Directors must issue an invitation for the General Assembly to meet upon a reasoned request from shareholders owning ten percent of the company's capital, or upon a request from the auditor, within fifteen days from the date of the request. The agenda shall be determined, and the provisions related to the Constituent Assembly stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments shall apply to the procedures for inviting the General Assembly and the quorum for attendance and voting. Every shareholder, regardless of the number of shares they hold, has the right to attend the General Assembly, and shall have a number of votes equal to the number of votes allocated to the same class of shares. A shareholder may not vote on their own behalf or on behalf of someone they represent in matters related to their personal interest, or in a dispute existing between them and the company. Any condition or decision that violates this shall be null and void. A shareholder may delegate another person to attend on their behalf by virtue of a special power of attorney or an authorization provided by the company for this purpose. Anyone claiming a right over shares that conflicts with what is recorded in the company's shareholder register may apply to the judge of urgent matters to obtain an order on petition to deprive the disputed shares of voting rights for a period determined by the judge or until the dispute is settled by the competent court, in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Civil and Commercial Procedures Law .

chunks.title: Article (49)
chunks.text_en: Optional Text Voting for candidates for membership of the Board of Directors shall be subject to the cumulative voting system, which grants each shareholder voting power equal to the number of shares they own, entitling them to cast these votes for one or more candidates or distribute them among the candidates they choose without duplicating these votes.

chunks.title: Article (50)
chunks.text_en: The General Assembly meeting shall be chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors , or their deputy, or someone delegated by the Board of Directors for that purpose, or someone elected by the General Assembly from among the shareholders or others.

chunks.title: Article (51)
chunks.text_en: Subject to the provisions of the law, the Ordinary General Assembly shall, in its annual meeting, be competent to take decisions on matters falling within its jurisdiction, and in particular the following: 1 - The auditor's report on the company's financial statements. 2 - The Board of Directors' report on the company's activities and financial position for the ended fiscal year. 3 - A report on any violations observed by regulatory authorities and for which penalties were imposed on the company. 4 - The company's financial statements. 5 - The Board of Directors' proposals regarding profit distribution. 6 - Discharging the liability of the members of the Board of Directors. 7 - Electing or dismissing members of the Board of Directors and determining their remuneration. 8 - Appointing the company's auditor, determining their fees, or authorizing the Board of Directors to do so. 9 - Reporting on transactions that have occurred or will occur with related parties. Related parties are defined according to International Accounting Standards .

chunks.title: Article (52)
chunks.text_en: By a resolution issued by the company's Ordinary General Assembly , the chairman or one or more members of the Board of Directors may be dismissed, or the company's Board of Directors may be dissolved and a new Board of Directors elected, based on a proposal submitted by a number of shareholders owning no less than one-quarter of the company's issued capital. Upon the issuance of a resolution to dissolve the Board of Directors, and appoint a new board at the same meeting, the Assembly may decide either for this board to continue managing the company's affairs until the new board is elected, or to appoint a temporary administrative committee whose primary task shall be to convene the General Assembly to elect the new board, within one month of its appointment.

chunks.title: Article (53)
chunks.text_en: The Ordinary General Assembly may not discuss matters not included in the agenda unless they are urgent matters that arose after the agenda was prepared or became apparent during the meeting, or if requested by a regulatory authority , the auditor , or a number of shareholders owning five percent of the company's capital. If, during the discussion, it becomes apparent that the information related to some presented matters is insufficient, the meeting shall be postponed for a period not exceeding ten working days if requested by a number of shareholders owning one-quarter of the issued capital. The postponed meeting shall be held without the need for new invitation procedures.

chunks.title: Article (54)
chunks.text_en: The Board of Directors must implement the resolutions of the General Assembly unless those resolutions violate the law, the articles of association , or these regulations. The Board of Directors must present the violating resolutions to the Ordinary General Assembly in a meeting convened to discuss the aspects of the violation.

chunks.title: Article (55)
chunks.text_en: The provisions related to the Ordinary General Assembly shall apply to the Extraordinary General Assembly , taking into account the provisions stipulated in the following articles:

chunks.title: Article (56)
chunks.text_en: The Extraordinary General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors or upon a reasoned request from shareholders owning fifteen percent of the company's issued capital or from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . The Board of Directors must invite the Extraordinary General Assembly to a meeting within thirty days from the date of submitting the request. If the Board of Directors fails to invite the General Assembly within the period stipulated in the preceding paragraph, the Ministry shall issue the invitation for the meeting within fifteen days from the expiry date of the period referred to in the preceding paragraph.

chunks.title: Article (57)
chunks.text_en: The meeting of the Extraordinary General Assembly shall not be valid unless attended by shareholders representing three-quarters of the company's issued capital. If this quorum is not met, an invitation shall be issued for a second meeting, which shall be valid if attended by those representing more than half of the issued capital. Resolutions shall be issued by a majority exceeding half of the total shares of the company's issued capital.

chunks.title: Article (58)
chunks.text_en: Subject to other competencies stipulated by law, the Extraordinary General Assembly shall be competent in the following matters: 1 - Amending the company's articles of association . 2 - Selling the entire project for which the company was established or disposing of it in any other way. 3 - Dissolving the company, or its merger, transformation, or division. 4 - Increasing or decreasing the company's capital.

chunks.title: Article (59)
chunks.text_en: Any resolution issued by the Extraordinary General Assembly shall not be effective until the publication procedures are completed. The approval of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be obtained if the resolution relates to the company's name, objectives, or capital.

chunks.title: Article (60)
chunks.text_en: Any shareholder may file a lawsuit for the nullification of any resolution issued by the Board of Directors or the Ordinary or Extraordinary General Assembly if it violates the law, the company's articles of association, or these bylaws, or if it is intended to harm the company's interests, and may claim compensation when necessary. Resolutions of the Ordinary and Extraordinary General Assembly that prejudice the rights of the minority may also be challenged. The challenge shall be made by a number of the company's shareholders owning fifteen percent of the issued capital, provided they were not among those who approved such resolutions.

chunks.title: Article (61)
chunks.text_en: **For the Holding Company** The company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of its subsidiary companies if the following conditions are met: 1 - Insufficiency of the subsidiary company's funds to meet its obligations. 2 - The company owns a percentage of the subsidiary company's capital that enables it to control the appointment of Board of Directors members or managers, or the decisions issued by the management. 3 - The subsidiary company makes decisions or takes actions that target the interest of the owning and controlling company and harm the interest of the subsidiary company or its creditors, and these actions are the primary reason for the subsidiary company's inability to meet its obligations. All of the above applies unless the holding company is liable for the debts of the subsidiary company based on another reason.

chunks.title: Article (62)
chunks.text_en: **Company Accounts** The company's fiscal year begins on January 1st and ends on December 31st of each year, with the exception of the first fiscal year , which begins from the date of the company's registration in the Commercial Register and ends on December 31st of the following fiscal year.

chunks.title: Article (63)
chunks.text_en: Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on the proposal of the Board of Directors , a percentage of not less than ten percent of the net profits shall be deducted to form a statutory reserve for the company. The Assembly may use the statutory reserve only to cover the company's losses or to ensure the distribution of dividends to shareholders at a rate not exceeding five percent of the paid-up capital in years when the company's profits do not allow for the distribution of this percentage, due to the absence of an optional reserve that permits the distribution of this percentage of profits. What has been deducted from the statutory reserve must be returned to it when the profits of subsequent years allow, unless this reserve exceeds half of the issued capital .

chunks.title: Article (64)
chunks.text_en: Annually, a percentage of 5% shall be deducted from the gross profits, or a percentage determined by the Board of Directors after consulting the auditor , for the depreciation of the company's assets or to compensate for their decrease in value. These funds shall be used to purchase necessary materials, machinery, and facilities or to repair them, and these funds may not be distributed to shareholders.

chunks.title: Article (65)
chunks.text_en: The General Assembly must decide to deduct a percentage of the profits to meet the company's obligations under labor laws and social insurance laws. A special fund may be established to assist the company's workers and employees.

chunks.title: Article (66)
chunks.text_en: Annually, by a resolution issued by the Ordinary General Assembly based on the proposal of the Board of Directors , a percentage not exceeding ten percent of the net profits may be deducted to form an optional reserve allocated for purposes determined by the Assembly.

chunks.title: Article (67)
chunks.text_en: The General Assembly , based on the proposal of the Board of Directors , may distribute dividends to shareholders at the end of the fiscal year . For this distribution to be valid, it must be from actual profits, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and must not affect the company's paid-up capital .

chunks.title: Article (68)
chunks.text_en: **Auditor** The provisions of Articles 227 to 233 of Companies Law No. 1/2016 and its amendments shall apply.

chunks.title: Article (69)
chunks.text_en: At the end of each fiscal year , the company must prepare a consolidated balance sheet of profits and losses for itself and all its subsidiary companies , accompanied by explanations and data in accordance with international accounting standards.

chunks.title: Article (70)
chunks.text_en: **Company Dissolution and Liquidation** The company shall be dissolved by one of the matters stipulated in Articles 266 to 277 of the Companies Law , its amendments, and its executive regulations.

chunks.title: Article (71)
chunks.text_en: The company's liquidation shall be carried out as specified in the provisions of Articles 278 to 295 of the Companies Law , its amendments, and its executive regulations.

chunks.title: Article (72)
chunks.text_en: The provisions of Companies Law No. 1/2016 and its executive regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the articles of association or in these bylaws.

chunks.title: Article (73)
chunks.text_en: An original copy of this contract shall be kept at the company's head office and on its website. An original copy of this contract shall also be kept in the company's file with the competent department at the Ministry of Commerce and Industry .

chunks.title: Announcement of Practice No. (1/2025-2026) Tender
chunks.text_en:
Printing various publications and seals for different departments at the State Audit Bureau Tender Details for State Audit Bureau Printing Services | Submission Date | Closing Date | Price | Guarantee | |:---|:---|:------|:--------| | 2025/04/13 | 2025/04/30 | | 2% of the total bid value as an initial guarantee for 90 days | | Public Bid Opening Meeting | 11:00 AM on Thursday, 01/05/2025, at the State Audit Bureau headquarters | | |
The State Audit Bureau announces the tender for Practice No. (1/2025-2026) in accordance with the terms and specifications contained in the tender documents, which can be obtained from the Primary Section, whether through letters of guarantee or certified bank checks bearing the name of the submitting company or establishment and the beneficiary, the State Audit Bureau . It is required to attach (a certificate from the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file regarding government contracts, a clearance certificate issued by the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy , and a certificate of payment of subscriptions for the Public Institution for Social Security ) with the bids, otherwise the bid will be considered void. * Note: - Follow the official website of the State Audit Bureau (WWW.SAB.GOV.KW) for any updates to the practice. * Attend the State Audit Bureau to receive samples.

chunks.title: Announcement of Company Registration for the Year 2025
chunks.text_en: As an approved supplier in the suppliers' register at the State Audit Bureau The State Audit Bureau announces the opening of electronic registration for companies as approved suppliers in the Bureau's suppliers' register. Accordingly, companies wishing to register are requested to attend the State Audit Bureau / Financial Affairs Department / Budget Section) with the company details form (after electronically registering the company on the Bureau's official website). Company registration will not be approved until the fees of 5 K.D. are paid. The payment receipt and registration form must be submitted to the Supply Section for the company to be approved as a supplier for the current calendar year, renewable annually for the same fees. * Note: Follow the official website of the State Audit Bureau (www.sab.gov.kw) for any updates. * To register: Bureau's Homepage --> Electronic Services --> For Companies --> Registration --> The form is downloaded after completing all data. For inquiries, please contact the following number: 24957346 (There is a WhatsApp account on this number) Article (74) The founders acknowledge the following: First: That they have subscribed to all shares and deposited the amount required by law to be paid from their value in one of the local banks at the disposal of the company. Second: That they have appointed the necessary administrative bodies to manage the company. Third: Preservation of the papers and documents supporting the aforementioned declarations with the official editor. Article (75) This contract was drawn up based on the letter from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry No. 20122022442642, registered with the Contracts and Companies Control at the Ministry of Justice under No. 6377301 dated 03/03/2025 AD. From what has been mentioned, this contract was drawn up and, after being read to those present, it was agreed upon. Approval / Fadila Tawfiq Al-Abdulhadi

chunks.title: Supreme Council for Planning and Development <orig>المجلس الأعلى للتخطيط والتنمية</orig>
chunks.text_en: Announcement The General Secretariat announces its desire to issue Practice No. (9-2024/2025) related to the maintenance of devices and systems in the halls of the General Secretariat building of the Supreme Council for Planning and Development . Companies wishing to apply for this practice must observe the following: 1. The company must be registered with the General Secretariat of the Supreme Council for Planning and Development and listed with the Central Agency for Public Tenders before the issuance of this announcement. 2. The applying company must have experience in this field, with proof thereof. 3. The General Secretariat will begin issuing the practice terms starting from the date of the announcement's publication and for a period of ten working days. 4. The practice terms can be obtained from the Financial Affairs Department for a fee of -- / 75 K.D. (only seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars only). 5. A preliminary meeting with the bidders will be held on Wednesday, 16/4/2025, at 10:00 AM in the General Secretariat building of the Supreme Council for Planning and Development . 6. Bids must be submitted in a sealed envelope to the Financial Affairs Department no later than 1:00 PM on Thursday, 24/4/2025.

Announcement Regarding the launch of a new electronic page on the official website of the State Audit Bureau in the Procurement Department for displaying direct purchase requests. The State Audit Bureau announces the launch of a new electronic page on its official website, specifically for the Procurement Department , to display direct purchase requests. The State Audit Bureau offers all companies a new service through this page to view purchase requests and submit price offers, after registering the required data in the price offer request form, by following these steps: 1- Access the official Bureau website here (www.sab.gov.kw) 2- Select 'Electronic Services' item 3- Select 'For Companies' item 4- Select 'Direct Order' item Then, proceed to register the data in the price offer form. For inquiries, please contact the following number: 24957346 (WhatsApp account available on this number) Announcement Company Registration for the year 2025 as an Approved Supplier in the Suppliers Register at the State Audit Bureau The State Audit Bureau announces the opening of electronic registration for companies as approved suppliers in the Bureau's Suppliers Register . Accordingly, companies wishing to register are requested to attend the State Audit Bureau / Financial Affairs Department / Budget Section with the company details form attached (after electronically registering the company on the official Bureau website). Company registration will not be approved until the fee of 5 K.D. is paid. The payment receipt and registration form must be submitted to the Procurement Department for the company to be approved as a supplier for the current calendar year, renewable annually for the same fee. * Note: Follow the official State Audit Bureau website (www.sab.gov.kw) for any updates. * To register: Bureau Homepage --> Services Mohammed Rabie Raja Al-Mutairi | Deceased | 272100300835 | | K.D.) (only Seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars ) non-refundable, payable by certified cheque. The specifications booklet shall be delivered at the (Information Technology Department) (Kuwait Investment Authority Headquarters - Sharq - Block 1, Street 201 - 26th Floor - Office No. 2611). Bids shall be submitted during the Authority's official working hours starting from Sunday, 13/04/2025, with the last day for submission being Sunday, 04/05/2025. Bids must be submitted in sealed envelopes by the specified date before 1:00 PM (Kuwait Investment Authority Headquarters - Sharq - Block 1, Street 201 - 22nd Floor - Room No. 2217). The practitioner must also attach the following documents with the bid: 1- A copy of the Commercial Register. 2- A copy of the certificate from the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 3- A copy of the company's registration receipt with the Kuwait Investment Authority . 4- A copy of the national labor percentage fulfillment certificate, valid for one year. 5- A copy of the registration receipt with the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 6- A copy of the practice purchase receipt. 7- The initial guarantee for the practice, valued at (2%), in the form of a certified cheque or a letter of guarantee issued by one of the accredited banks in the State of Kuwait in favor of the Kuwait Investment Authority , unconditional and valid for at least (90) days from the last date for bid receipt. 8- Attachment of a certificate of payment of subscriptions to the Public Institution for Social Security as stipulated in Article (95) of the Social Security Law . 9- Attachment of a valid "To Whom It May Concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file regarding government contracts registered with the private sector, noting that bids not including this certificate will be excluded. chunks.title: Communication and Information Technology Regulatory Authority <orig>الهيئة العامة للاتصالات وتقنية المعلومات</orig>
chunks.text_en: Announcement Cancellation of Public Tender No. (02-2025/2024) The Communication and Information Technology Regulatory Authority announces the cancellation of Public Tender No. (02-2025/2024) regarding the consultancy project for studying activities related to the telecommunications sector in the State of Kuwait . For inquiries, please contact the Financial Department at 22330357 or send inquiries to the email address procurement@citra.gov.kw

chunks.title: Kuwait Investment Authority <orig>الهيئة العامة للاستثمار</orig>
chunks.text_en:
Announcement Regarding the Offering of Practice No. TM - 277-2028/2025 Providing Support in the Field of Cybersecurity and Information Confidentiality at the Kuwait Investment Authority The Kuwait Investment Authority announces the offering of the aforementioned practice. Therefore, gentlemen, Request to Amend the Bankruptcy Report and Provisional Debt List for the Bankrupt Debtor For the bankruptcy of / Abdulhassan Mohammed Abdullatif Al-Duwaisan Submitted to the Bankruptcy Department in Case No. 2000/43 Public Debts Bankruptcy/1 Dated 13/02/2001 Added debtor whom the Authority deems acceptable in his capacity: Provisional Debt List for Bankrupt Debtor | Statement/Description | Amount | Notes | |:---|:-------|:------| | National Bank of Kuwait | 65,889/410 K.D. | Bankrupt's share of guaranteed debt for Al-Dousat Global Group for Import and Export and Commission Agent, according to Article 13 of Law No. 41 of 1993. |
And pursuant to the text of Article (111) of Law No. 71 of 2020, "The debtor and every creditor, even if their name is not included in the debt list, have the right to appeal to the Bankruptcy Department regarding the debts listed in the list, within ten days from the date of the list's announcement." chunks.title: For the Bankruptcy of / Mahmoud Hussein Ali Mahmoud <orig>محمود حسين علي محمود</orig>
chunks.text_en:
Submitted to the Bankruptcy Department in Case No. 2012/24 Public Debts Bankruptcy/1, dated 2012/2/8 Pursuant to the judgment issued in Case No. 2012/24 Public Debts Bankruptcy/1 dated 2012/2/8, the bankruptcy of the bankrupt / Mahmoud Hussein Ali Mahmoud was declared, and the judgment has become final and acquired the force of res judicata. Whereas the aforementioned bank (Commercial Bank of Kuwait ) stated in its letter dated 2019/9/23 that the debt of the aforementioned bankrupt amounts to 352,759.158 K.D. as of 1990/8/1, given that the debt value mentioned in the bankruptcy declaration judgment, which is 405,818.843 K.D., is as of 1991/12/31 and not as of 1990/8/1, considering that the error in the judgment is a material error, while the current initial data regarding the debt value are correct. Based on the foregoing and in deference to the authority of judgments, the Kuwait Investment Authority , in its capacity as "The Trustee ", relies on what was stated in the bankruptcy declaration judgment. The Authority, in its capacity as the Bankruptcy Department , issued a decision by its letter dated 2025/2/24 approving the publication of the provisional list of debts as follows: Debts that the Authority, in its capacity, deems acceptable: Debts recognized by the Authority for Mahmoud Hussein Ali | Description | Amount | Notes | |:------|:------|:---| | Commercial Bank of Kuwait | 405,818.843 K.D. | Pursuant to Bankruptcy Declaration Judgment No. 2012/24 dated 2012/2/8 as of 1990/8/1 |
Pursuant to the text of Article (111) of Law No. 71 of 2020 , "The debtor and any creditor whose name is not included in the list of debts have the right to appeal to the Bankruptcy Department regarding the debts listed in the list within ten days from the date of the list's announcement."

chunks.title: For the Bankruptcy of / Adel Ali Abdulrahman Al-Bahr <orig>عادل علي عبدالرحمن البحر</orig>
chunks.text_en:
Submitted to the Bankruptcy Department in Case No. 2003/30 Public Debts Bankruptcy/1, dated 2003/4/22 Firstly: Financial and Accounting Procedures: 1) Debt as of 1990/8/1 amounts to 2,079,287.613 K.D. 2) Debt according to Article 13 amounts to 7,899,596.107 K.D. 3) Amounts collected after the bankruptcy declaration judgment amount to 4,226,537.909 K.D. Secondly: Debts The Public Authority for Industry announces a hold on the company's file regarding government contracts registered with the Public Authority for Manpower , and a certificate of interest confirming compliance with the national labor percentage with a recent date contemporary to the tender date. The bidder must also submit with their bid a preliminary bond in the form of a certified check or an unconditional and irrevocable letter of guarantee issued in the name of the bidder from one of the banks approved by the State of Kuwait and in favor of the Public Authority for Industry , valid for the duration of the bid. Any bid not accompanied by the full amount of this bond shall be excluded. The preliminary bond shall not be refunded until (90) days have passed from the date of the closing of the practice or when the winning bidder signs the contract, unless the practice is cancelled. The Authority shall not pay any interest on the amount of this bond, and the bidder's creditors may not seize its amount, noting that this practice is indivisible. Announcement The Public Authority for Industry announces that the Allocation Committee decided in its meeting No. (2025/3) held on 2023/3/13 to cancel Craft License No. 25757 issued to Mr. Ali Hassan Abdul Rahim and to withdraw Craft Units No. (G17-G18) at Fahaheel Craft Complex . Accordingly, the concerned parties must vacate the craft units within 10 days from the date of this announcement and hand them over to the Public Authority for Industry , otherwise the Authority will be compelled to take its procedures for removal at the expense of the concerned party , without prejudice to the Authority's right to claim any other compensation resulting therefrom from the concerned parties.
