This will be at the headquarters of the Bait Al-Mal Control Department, which is affiliated with the Administration, located in Al-Sulaibiya on the Sixth Ring Road. Therefore, those wishing to purchase should attend the auction site on the specified date, while observing the following terms of sale: 1. The Auction Sale Committee has the right to sell the goods in one lot or several lots, depending on the circumstances of the sale. 2. The successful bidder shall bear a brokerage commission of 2% of the sale price. 3. The successful bidder must pay the value of the goods only via K-net , according to the assessment of the Auction Sale Committee. 4. The successful bidder must remove the goods from the headquarters of the Bait Al-Mal Control Department urgently and within 5 (five) to 7 (seven) days after the auction is awarded to them, otherwise any goods will be impounded and sold in the next auction if the successful bidder fails to remove them within the specified period. 5. If the goods are not sold for any reason in the auction, the Committee has the right to offer them for sale in the subsequent auction. 6. The Administration has the right to stop, postpone, or reschedule the sale to another date if a suitable price is not obtained or for any reasons it deems contrary to its interests. 7. The Auction Sale Committee may cancel or postpone the auction for any reason it deems fit. 8. Since the goods will be sold as-is, bidders must inspect them to ascertain the condition, quality, and type of these goods located at the aforementioned address during the auction sale.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en:
Regarding the expiration of the initial approval period for establishing a company engaged in securities activities, the Capital Markets Authority announces the expiration of the initial approval period granted to the founders of a company under establishment on 2025/04/06, in accordance with Resolution No. (134) of 2024 concerning the initial approval for establishing a closed company engaged in securities activities, issued on 2024/10/07, from the following gentlemen: Companies Granted Initial Approval for Financial Activities by Capital Markets Authority | No. | Name | Ownership | Nationality | |:----|:-------------------------------------------------------------------|:----------|:-----------------------------------| | 1 | WB MENA Holding Limited | 84% | United Arab Emirates | | 2 | Seldon Investments Limited (Jordan) Private Shareholding | 4% | Jordan | | 3 | Seldon Investments Limited | 4% | British Virgin Islands | | 4 | Windsor Brokers International Limited | 4% | Seychelles | | 5 | Windsor Global Markets Limited | 4% | British Virgin Islands |
Accordingly, the application for licensing securities activities submitted by the aforementioned founders of the company shall be deemed null and void as of the date of expiration of the initial approval on 2025/04/06, in accordance with the provision of Article 1-7 of Book Five (Securities Activities and Registered Persons) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. 7 of 2010 regarding the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activity and their amendments, which stipulates the following: " If the applicant fails to submit the required documents and information within the period specified by the Authority in the notification without an acceptable excuse from the Authority, the application shall be deemed null and void. " The documents shall be returned after being filled out, with the required supporting documents enclosed, and a preliminary bond of not less than (2%) of the bid value, or in the form of a certified check or a letter of guarantee issued by one of the accredited banks in the State of Kuwait , issued in the name of the bidder and in favor of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation , and not subject to any restriction or condition. Bids not accompanied by this bond will not be considered, even if they conform to specifications. The bond must be valid for the required bid validity period. The documents shall be submitted in an official envelope sealed with red wax, and bids will be delivered to the Tenders and Contracts Department at the Directorate General of Civil Aviation . Please note that the final deadline for submitting bids is (1:00 PM) on (Wednesday) corresponding to (2025/4/30). Any bid submitted after this deadline will be rejected.

chunks.title: Announcement Auction No. (8-2023/2022)
chunks.text_en: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation announces the extension of the closing period for Auction No. (8-2023/2022) for the supply, installation, operation, maintenance, management, and training of the passenger registration and early border security inquiry system at Kuwait International Airport . Please note that the final deadline for submitting bids will be at (1:00 PM) on (Wednesday) corresponding to (2025/4/30) instead of (Tuesday) corresponding to (2025/4/15). Any bid submitted after this deadline will be rejected.

chunks.title: Announcement of Practice Tender No. (45-2024/2025)
chunks.text_en: Supply and installation of arriving passenger baggage inspection devices at Passenger Terminal T4 The Directorate General of Civil Aviation announces the extension of the closing period for Practice Tender No. (45-2024/2025) related to the supply and installation of arriving passenger baggage inspection devices at Passenger Terminal T4. Please note that the final deadline for submitting bids will be at (1:00 PM) on (Monday) corresponding to (2025/4/21) instead of (Monday) corresponding to (2025/4/7). Any bid submitted after this deadline will be rejected.

chunks.title: Announcement 41/2025
chunks.text_en:
The holder of the following license has submitted to the Municipality: Commercial License Details for Kuwait Municipality | Commercial Name | Category | Registration No. | |:---|:------|:---| | Laurent International General Trading and Contracting Company | Municipality Contractor Class B | 6400127 |
a request for the release of the company's bank guarantees deposited with the Municipality in consideration of its classification. Based on Article (19) of Municipality Resolution No. (161/2009) concerning the classification, licensing, and registration of building and demolition contractors in Kuwait Municipality , which stipulates that a contractor may submit a request to the Municipality to cancel their registration before its expiry or to cancel the classification, the committee stipulated in Article 1 of this regulation shall issue its decision after fulfilling the following two conditions: 1- The contractor has completed all contracted works. 2- Fifteen (15) days have elapsed from the date of publication of this request in the Official Gazette . The cancellation of the license or registration entails the refund of the bank guarantee submitted by the contractor, taking into account the last paragraph of Article (10) of this resolution. Accordingly, the Municipality appeals to anyone who has any claims against the aforementioned contractor regarding building contracts concluded between them that have not yet been completed, or judicial attachments in their favor due to violating contracting works or dues arising from the contracting agreement, to review the Contractors Department at the Engineering Systems Department within a maximum period of fifteen days from this date. The Municipality will not consider any claims submitted after the prescribed period. Director General of the Municipality 9. In the event that the successful bidder fails to pay the sale price or part thereof, the General Administration of Customs has the right to resell at his expense. If the price does not reach the amount at which the auction was first awarded, he shall be obligated to pay the difference. In this case, the General Administration of Customs has the right to deprive him from participating in any future auction. 10. The Administration is not responsible for any disposal or use of the sold goods that violates applicable laws and legislations. 11. This sale is conducted in accordance with the provisions of Law No. 10 of 2003 concerning the Unified Customs System and its regulating resolutions. 12. All bidders must adhere to health and preventive requirements. Auction Sale Committee

chunks.title: Announcement Practice Tender No. (1-2026/2025)
chunks.text_en:
Subject: Maintenance and Technical Support for Parking Systems and Automatic Doors for the Parking Building and Commercial Mall T1 The Directorate General of Civil Aviation announces Practice Tender No. (1-2026/2025) for the maintenance and technical support of parking systems and automatic doors for the parking building and commercial mall T1, in accordance with the conditions and specifications mentioned in the practice tender documents, which can be obtained from the Financial Affairs Department – Tenders and Contracts Department at the Directorate General of Civil Aviation starting from (Sunday) corresponding to (2025/4/13) during official working hours, against payment of an amount of seventy-five (75 K.D.) Kuwaiti Dinars only, provided that payment is made via K-Net . A preliminary meeting will be held at the headquarters of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation on (Tuesday) corresponding to (2025/4/22) at (11:00 AM). And returned Director General of the Municipality Further to Kuwait Municipality's Announcements No. (2023/43) and No. (2023/104) regarding the opening of qualification for companies and establishments undertaking solid municipal waste, construction waste, and asbestos waste transportation works, in accordance with what is stipulated in the qualification booklet prepared for this purpose and Ministerial Decision No. 2023/354 regarding the Bylaw on Municipal Waste Management and Public Cleanliness. Kuwait Municipality wishes to remind that it is not permitted to engage in the transportation of solid municipal waste, construction waste, and asbestos waste without obtaining a qualification certificate from the competent committee . Violators will be subject to legal liability. Based on Article (19) of Municipality Decision No. (2009/161) concerning the classification, licensing, and registration of building and demolition contractors in Kuwait Municipality , which stipulates that a contractor may submit a request to the Municipality to cancel their registration before its expiry or to cancel the classification, and the committee stipulated in Article One of this Bylaw may issue its decision to that effect after fulfilling the following two conditions: 1- The contractor has completed all contracted works. 2- Fifteen (15) days have elapsed from the date of publication of this request in the Official Gazette . The cancellation of the license or registration shall result in the refund of the bank guarantee submitted by the contractor, taking into account the last paragraph of Article (10) of this Decision. Accordingly, the Municipality appeals to anyone who has any claims against the aforementioned contractor regarding concluded building contracts that have not yet been completed, or judicial attachments in their favor due to violating contracting works or dues arising from the contracting agreement, to review the Contractors Department at the Engineering Systems Department within a maximum period of fifteen days from this date. The Municipality will not consider any claims submitted after the prescribed period. Requesting Approval for the Completion of an Economic Concentration Process Through the establishment of a joint venture between Alat Technologies Company (Kingdom of Saudi Arabia) with 49% and KB Elevator Iberica Holdings S.L.U. (Spain) with 51%. Companies executing the supply, installation, and implementation of main transformer station projects with a voltage of 11/132 kV, and which meet the list of principles and criteria required from bidders, must apply to purchase the tender documents and submit all documents and papers in accordance with the requirements, fulfilling the conditions, and in the prescribed manner. General Directorate of the National Guard Announcement Issuance of Tender No. (12- 2024/2025) The Committee for Military Material Purchases and Military Facilities Contracts in the National Guard announces the issuance of a tender for the supply, installation, operation, and maintenance of fiber optic network devices. This is open to specialized companies and establishments in accordance with the general and special conditions stated in the tender documents. Therefore, companies and establishments wishing to participate in this tender must register on the website of the General Directorate of the National Guard (www.kng.gov.kw) to purchase the tender documents for a non-refundable fee of (150 K.D.) only, one hundred and fifty Kuwaiti Dinars only, payable through the National Guard's website using the (K-NET) service. A preliminary meeting will be held at 10:00 AM on Sunday, 20/04/2025, at the General Directorate of the National Guard , Colonel Staff / Faisal Al-Jareed Building. Bidders, whether present or absent from this meeting, will be bound by all decisions, recommendations, or clarifications contained therein. Participating companies and establishments must also print the minutes of the preliminary meeting from the National Guard's website (www.kng.gov.kw). Please note that the deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Wednesday, 30/04/2025. Note: Companies and establishments wishing to purchase tender documents must provide us with a copy of the purchase receipt. Companies classified as small and medium enterprises must bring a certificate proving this. For any inquiries, please call the switchboard at (24999999) extensions (66787-66735) Fax (24999858). First: Tender documents can be obtained from the Documents and Contracts Department - Third Floor - at the headquarters of the Public Authority for Housing Welfare in South Surra , according to the following terms and requirements when purchasing tender documents: 1. Submission of the company's registration certificate with the Central Agency for Public Tenders . (A recent certificate for the year the tender is issued), and a certificate of accreditation for authorized signatories with the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 2. Submission of a registration certificate proving the bidder's license from the Ministry of Commerce , a commercial registration certificate, a registration certificate with the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry , and the company's articles of association with all attached amendments, certified by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry (for local companies). 3. Submission of a registration certificate issued by the competent authorities in the country to which the foreign bidder belongs, provided that it is certified by the official authorities in the country of establishment and attested by a Kuwaiti consulate or any other entity authorized to certify the company, along with a copy of its articles of association, including all attached amendments, all of which must be in either Arabic or English, legally notarized, and certified by official authorities. 4. Submission of a valid national labor percentage fulfillment certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower (for local companies). 5. Tender documents shall be handed over against a non-refundable amount as specified in the table above, by certified check issued in favor of the Public Authority for Housing Welfare or K-Net card. 6. Submission of a letter of authorization from the company in the name of the authorized person to purchase tender documents, along with a copy of the civil ID of the same authorized person and a signature accreditation certificate. 7. Companies that have received a valid penalty in state tenders and projects will not be allowed to participate. 8. Companies that have ongoing contracts (under execution) with the Authority and are delayed in delivering any part of the project beyond the contractual completion date by a delay percentage exceeding (50%) of the total contract duration will not be allowed to apply for a new tender until the contract under execution is settled. 9. The deadline for submitting inquiries about the above-mentioned tender is 01/05/2025, provided they are submitted in Arabic (inquiries should be submitted to the Documents and Contracts Department, Third Floor, at the headquarters of the Public Authority for Housing Welfare in South Surra ). No questions or inquiries received by the Authority after this date will be considered. Second: The following conditions must be adhered to when submitting the bid: 1. The deadline for submitting bids (closing) is 1:00 PM at the headquarters of the Public Authority for Housing Welfare - South Surra - Documents and Contracts Department - Third Floor. 2. Submission of proof that the company has executed and completed works similar to the tender works (required and whose controls are specified in item Third: Foundations and Criteria) as a main contractor during the last fifteen years (from the date of preliminary handover of the project), along with a copy of the contract and a certificate of work completion issued by the contracting entity. 3. The bidder must attach a clearance certificate stating that there are no impediments related to the labor registered with them at the Public Authority for Manpower , in accordance with Central Agency for Public Tenders Circular No. (2) for the year 2021, issued on 26/01/2021 (for local companies). 4. Submission of a letter of authorization from the company in the name of the authorized person to submit the bid, along with a copy of the civil ID of the same authorized person and a signature accreditation certificate. 5. The bidder must attach with their bid the preliminary bond in their name in favor of the Public Authority for Housing Welfare in the form of a guarantee letter issued in Arabic, unconditional, free of any reservations, payable on demand, issued by a bank accredited in the State of Kuwait , and valid throughout the bid validity period, and for the value specified in the tender announcement. The bidder is not entitled to cancel it or have it seized by their creditors or claim any interest on it. Cash bonds are not accepted, and any bid not accompanied by the preliminary bond shall be excluded. 6. The authorized representative of the company shall place all tender documents, all required documents in full, as well as qualification documents, in their designated envelopes according to the instructions in the documents and the tender announcement, and seal them with red wax. Then, they must personally, and under their own responsibility, place the first envelope [Bid Submission Envelope] in the designated box, which contains: - Bid form and total bid value (original copies), fully completed, stamped, and signed. - Preliminary bond (preliminary guarantee) - Valid national labor percentage fulfillment certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , noting that bids not including this certificate will be excluded (for local companies). - Company's articles of association and a registration certificate issued by the competent authorities in the country to which it belongs, stamped and signed by the official authorities in the country of establishment, and attested by a Kuwaiti consulate or any other entity authorized to certify (valid). Third: Qualification Documents (Foundations and Criteria): The bidder is obligated to submit all required documents proving their fulfillment of all terms and requirements under the Foundations and Criteria along with the bid documents, and place them inside the envelopes designated for the Foundations and Criteria requirements as follows: Required Technical Documents: - (1) The bidder must meet any of the following conditions: - - The bidder must have completed as a main contractor, within the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) of no less than (3) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher in the State of Kuwait (under the supervision of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy ), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed as a main contractor, within the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) of no less than (5) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher in the Gulf Cooperation Council countries (under the supervision of relevant governmental energy authorities), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed as a main contractor, within the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) of no less than (10) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher (under the supervision of relevant governmental energy authorities or the end-user if the bid/substations are outside the Gulf Cooperation Council countries, and no less than half the number (5) substations) that have been executed outside these countries, and provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, within the last (15) years, contract(s) (supply and installation) for at least (1) main substation with a voltage of (275 kV) or higher in the State of Kuwait (under the supervision of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy ), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and at least (2) power transformers with a capacity of (300 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, within the last (15) years, contract(s) (supply and installation) for at least (2) main substations with a voltage of (275 kV) or higher in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries (under the supervision of the relevant governmental energy authorities), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and at least (2) power transformers with a capacity of (300 MVA) or higher each. (2) The main contractor shall submit the contract document signed with the entity, and the acceptance certificate or completion certificate for the station(s) issued by the relevant governmental energy authorities or by the end-user if the station(s) are outside the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, provided that no more than (15) years have passed since the issuance of the certificate. (3) The main contractor shall submit a client certificate issued by the relevant governmental energy authorities or by the end-user if the station(s) are outside the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, proving that the station(s) have been in service for at least one year from the date of operation. Financial Documents: - The company's capital must not be less than one million Kuwaiti Dinars or its equivalent in other currencies, supported by documentary evidence. - The company must submit full financial statements (income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement) for the last (3) years, certified by an auditing firm and attested by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . - The company must submit certificates from the banks it deals with, indicating its financial standing and the banks' willingness to provide facilities to it, provided that the certificates are valid and issued within the year of the tender submission. These will be subject to financial study and analysis to assess financial capability. ISO Certificates: - A valid (ISO9001) certificate confirming the bidder's compliance with the standard quality management system. - A valid (ISO14001) certificate confirming the bidder's compliance with the environmental management system. Fourth: Rules and Conditions: 1. The bidder must submit (2) original hard copies of the bills of quantities. And (3) CD copies of all required documents. 2. If any of the required documents are issued by an entity outside the State of Kuwait , they must be attested by all official authorities, by the Embassy of the State of Kuwait in the country of origin of the documents, and by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in the State of Kuwait , and translated into Arabic by a certified translation office in the State of Kuwait . 3. All documents and data submitted by companies and establishments must be clear and sequential. 4. The Public Authority for Housing Welfare (PAHW) has the right to request any original copy to verify its conformity with the attached document copies. 5. The bidder must submit a declaration and undertaking regarding the accuracy of the information and data provided with the bid submission. 6. The company is committed to filling out the table and submitting all required documents with the bid papers. In case of failure to meet any of the required clauses or documents, the Authority has the right to disqualify the bid. 7. In implementation and application of Article (25) of the Authority's Tenders Regulations, and Article (59) of Law No. (49) of 2016 regarding Public Tenders, the Authority has the right not to award more than one tender from Tenders No. (PAHW/1442, PAHW/1279) to a single bidder, in accordance with the requirements of public interest. In this case, the Authority has the right to accept subsequent bids for the execution of the tenders at the lowest price offered in the subsequent bids after excluding the bidder to whom one of the two tenders was awarded. Bidders submitting their bids in the aforementioned tenders acknowledge their full and unconditional acceptance and acknowledgment of any decisions taken by the Authority in any form whatsoever, without any liability on the Authority in this regard. 8. All technical, financial, and legal documents will be subject to a study to determine their compliance with the tender participation conditions, and the Public Authority for Housing Welfare (PAHW) has the right to disqualify any bid if it does not meet the tender conditions and requirements. Fifth: This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents.

Cash bonds will not be accepted, and any bid not accompanied by the preliminary bond shall be excluded. 6. The authorized representative of the company shall place all tender documents and all required documents in full, as well as the qualification documents, in the envelope designated for them according to the instructions contained in the documents and their announcement, and submit and seal them with red wax. Then, they must personally place the documents for that, containing: - Bid form and total bid value (original copies), complete with data, stamped, and signed. - Preliminary bond (Preliminary Guarantee ) - Certificate of compliance with national labor percentage issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , valid. Bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded (for local companies). - Company's articles of association and registration certificate issued by the competent authorities in the country of origin, provided that they are generally signed by the official authorities of the country of establishment and attested by the Kuwaiti Consulate or any other entity authorized to attest (valid). Thirdly: Qualification Documents (Foundations and Standards): The bidder is required to submit all necessary documents proving their fulfillment of all conditions and requirements under the Foundations and Standards, along with the bid documents, and place them inside the envelope designated for the Foundations and Standards requirements as follows: Required Technical Documents: (1) The bidder must have met any of the following conditions: - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, during the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) for no less than (3) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher in the State of Kuwait (under the supervision of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy ), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, during the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) for no less than (5) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher in the Gulf Cooperation Council countries (under the supervision of the governmental authorities concerned with energy), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, during the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) for no less than (10) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) or higher (under the supervision of the governmental authorities concerned with energy or the end-user if the project/projects were outside the Gulf Cooperation Council countries), and no less than half the number (5 substations) must have been executed outside the bidder's country, provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, during the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) for no less than (1) main substation with a voltage of (275 kV) or higher in the State of Kuwait (under the supervision of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy ), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (300 MVA) or higher each. Or - The bidder must have completed, as a main contractor, during the last (15) years, contract/contracts (supply and installation) for no less than (2) main substations with a voltage of (275 kV) or higher in the Gulf Cooperation Council countries (under the supervision of the governmental authorities concerned with energy), provided that the substations are (Indoor Substation) and include SF6 gas-insulated switchgear and no less than (2) power transformers with a capacity of (300 MVA) or higher each. (2) The main contractor shall submit the contract document signed with the entity, and the handover and acceptance certificate or completion certificate for the substation/projects issued by the governmental authorities concerned with energy or by the end-user if the substation/projects were outside the Gulf Cooperation Council countries, provided that no more than (15 years) have passed since the issuance of the certificate. (3) The main contractor shall submit a client certificate issued by the governmental authorities concerned with energy or by the end-user if the projects/plans were outside the Gulf Cooperation Council countries, proving that the project/projects have been in service for at least one year from the date of operation. (4) In case of applying for tenders for the supply and installation of main substations with a voltage of 7. In implementation and application of Article (25) of the Foundation's Tenders Regulations, and Article (59) of Law No. (49) of 2016 concerning Public Tenders, the Foundation has the right not to award more than one tender from tenders numbered (M.A.KH.R.S/M/1442, M.A.KH.R.S/M/1279) to a single bidder, in accordance with the requirements of public interest. In this case, the Foundation has the right to accept the subsequent bids for the execution of the tenders at the lowest price offered in the subsequent bids after excluding the bidder to whom one of the two tenders has been awarded. Bidders submitting their offers in the aforementioned tenders acknowledge and fully and unconditionally accept any decisions made by the Foundation in any form whatsoever, without any liability on the Foundation in this regard. 8. All technical, financial, and legal documents will be subject to review for compliance with the tender participation conditions, and the Public Authority for Housing Welfare has the right to exclude any bid if it does not meet the tender conditions and requirements. Fifth: This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. Director General (132 kV) by local companies that do not have prior experience in executing the nature of the tender works. - (a) The bidder must be accredited by the Ministry of Electricity and Renewable Energy (Electrical Transmission Networks Sector) and their name listed in the tender documents as a manufacturer of power transformers with a capacity of (30 MVA), and these transformers must have been installed in no less than (10) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) in the Electrical Transmission Networks Sector in the State of Kuwait , and all these transformers must have been efficiently in service for no less than (3) years from the date of operation. - (b) The bidder must be accredited by the Ministry of Electricity and Renewable Energy (Electrical Transmission Networks Sector) and their name listed in the tender documents as a manufacturer of air-insulated switchgear with a voltage of (11 kV double busbar), and the equipment must have been installed in no less than (10) main substations with a voltage of (132 kV) in the Electrical Transmission Networks Sector in the State of Kuwait (including feeder cells, transformers, main busbar disconnectors, and current transformers), and all this equipment must have been efficiently in service for no less than (3) years from the date of operation. Note: For cases to which item No. 4 (a) or (b) applies, the bidder will not be allowed to enter other future tenders for 132/11 kV main substations if this current tender is awarded to them, until acceptance and commissioning certificates for the substations are submitted, provided that they have been efficiently in service for no less than one year from the date of operation. Kuwait Credit Bank Announcement Kuwait Credit Bank announces the offering of Practice No. (1-2025/2026) for the purchase of SMS service. Interested parties wishing to submit their bids should review the Bank to purchase the booklet of terms and technical specifications for the practice according to the details specified below:

chunks.title: Bid Conditions
chunks.text_en: The bidder must submit a technical offer and a financial offer in two sealed envelopes, one for the technical offer and the other for the financial offer, taking into account the attachment of a copy of the bid on a flash drive, according to the following conditions: - The technical envelope must contain the following: - The preliminary bond in the form of a letter of guarantee for an amount of (500 K.D.) "only five hundred Kuwaiti Dinars ", unconditional, free from any reservations, and irrevocable, issued by one of the banks accredited in the State of Kuwait in its name and in favor of Kuwait Credit Bank , provided that this bond is valid for at least three months from the last date for receiving bids. Any bid not accompanied by the full amount of this bond will be excluded. Preliminary bonds may not be refunded until (90) ninety days have passed from the closing date of the practice or when the winning practitioner submits the final bond and signs the contract, unless the practice is canceled. Kuwait Credit Bank will not pay any interest on the amount of this bond, and the practitioner's creditors may not seize its amount. - General and special conditions and technical data for the submitted offer. - Complete data on Kuwaiti subcontracting companies or individuals to whom part of the execution may be assigned if the practice documents require it. - Any other documents or data that the practice documents may require. - The financial envelope must contain the following: - Bid format approved by the bidder. - Price lists and bills of quantities. - Any other elements that may affect the financial value of the offer in accordance with the terms of the tender. - Any other documents or data required by the practice documents.

chunks.title: Financial Documents:
chunks.text_en: - The company's capital must not be less than one million Kuwaiti Dinars or its equivalent in other currencies, supported by documentary evidence. - The company must submit complete financial statements (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement) for the company for the last (3) years, certified by an auditing firm and attested by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . - The company must provide certificates from the banks with which the bidder deals, indicating its financial standing and the banks' willingness to provide facilities to it, provided that the certificates are valid and issued within the year of the tender's offering. - And will be subject to financial study and analysis to assess financial capability. - ISO Certificates: - Valid (ISO9001) certificate confirming the bidder's compliance with the standard quality management system. - Valid (ISO14001) certificate confirming the bidder's compliance with the environmental management system.

chunks.title: Fourth: Rules and Conditions:
chunks.text_en: 1. The bidder must submit (2) original hard copies of the bills of quantities and (3) CD copies of all required documents. 2. If any of the required documents are issued by an entity outside the State of Kuwait , they must be attested by all official authorities, by the Embassy of the State of Kuwait in the country of origin of the documents, and by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in the State of Kuwait , and translated into Arabic by an accredited translation office in the State of Kuwait . 3. All documents and data submitted by companies and institutions must be clear and sequential. 4. The Public Authority for Housing Welfare has the right to request any original copy to verify its conformity with the attached document copies. 5. The bidder must submit a declaration and undertaking regarding the accuracy of the information and data provided with the bid. 6. The company is obligated to fill out the table and submit all required documents with the bid papers. In case of non-fulfillment of any of the above-mentioned items or required documents, the Foundation has the right to exclude the bid.

chunks.text_en: 1. Must have previous experience in providing the service subject of the bid for no less than three years in similar governmental entities. 2. The bidder must provide a detailed record of the works undertaken by them as required, supported by authenticated certificates from the relevant authorities. 3. Must have a qualified team with professional experience and full knowledge of the nature of the required work.

chunks.text_en: 1. A letter of authorization to purchase the general and special terms booklet for this practice for the person present to purchase it, along with a copy of their civil ID, and approved by the authorized signatory of the company, with a letter of accreditation of the company's authorized signatory, approved by the Central Tenders Committee . 2. Fax number for any inquiries related to the Purchasing Committee at Kuwait Credit Bank . 3. A recent and contemporary copy of the certificate of registration with the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 4. A recent copy of the practice offering announcement. 5. A recent and contemporary copy of the certificate determining the national labor percentage issued by the competent authority. 6. A recent and contemporary copy of the company's commercial register certificate. 7. A recent and contemporary copy of the certificate proving registration with the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 8. A copy of a recent and contemporary certificate of registration with the Central Agency for Information Technology . All documents must be valid. Tender envelopes must be tightly sealed with red wax.

This is according to the terms and specifications contained in the documents. Companies specialized in supplying any of the aforementioned products and wishing to participate in any of the tenders listed in the table above must adhere to the following: 1. Purchase the tender documents from the office of Mr./Secretary of the Tenders Committee at the company's administration building located in (Dhajeej Area – opposite Kuwait Airways Company building) during official working hours. 2. The purchase value of the tender document for a single tender is K.D. 200 (Two Hundred Kuwaiti Dinars ), non-refundable, and shall be paid to the cashier at the company's headquarters against an official receipt to be attached with the tender documents and placed in the sealed envelope. 3. The original preliminary bond for the tender must be attached to the bid, in the form of a bank guarantee letter or a certified cheque for K.D. 3,000 (Three Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) for each tender separately, and it must be valid for the entire bid validity period of 90 days. 4. Companies wishing to participate in any of the aforementioned tenders must adhere to the dates specified in the table above (dates for purchasing tender documents, preliminary meeting, and deadline for bid submission). It should be noted that the bidder in any of the aforementioned tenders must meet the following conditions: * Must be a company or establishment specialized in the works subject of the tender, with experience and a track record in the tender field of no less than 5 years. * The capital of the bidding company or establishment must not be less than K.D. 50,000 (Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). For inquiries, please call the following telephone number: Switchboard No.: 24375500, extensions 5644, 5739.

chunks.text_en:
**Announcement** Kuwait Petroleum International announces its annual plan for tenders to be issued for the fiscal year 2025-2026, as per the attached table, based on Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016, Chapter Five, Section One, Article (33). Kuwait Petroleum Corporation International Annual Tenders 2025-2026 | SI # | Contract Subject | Brief Scope of Work | Method of Contracting | Expected Issue Date | |:-----|:---|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | KPI BMS Corporate Framework | Upgrade KPI BMS Corporate Framework | Tender | Q1 | | 2 | KPI Crisis Management Framework | Upgrade KPI Crisis Management Framework | Tender | Q2 | | 3 | KPI Oil Contingency Plan Framework | Upgrade KPI Oil Contingency Plan Framework | Tender | Q2 | | 4 | Upgrade backup | Upgrade the current KPI | Tender | Q1 |
com.kw)) * The tender was actually launched through the K-Tendering electronic system on 11/03/2025. * Closing date is Thursday, 17/07/2025. * The preliminary bid bond for the tender is K.D. 15,000 valid for (90) days. * The document purchase fee is K.D. 500 . * The deadline for submitting bids is Thursday, 17/07/2025, at 1:00 PM local time for the State of Kuwait (13:00 Noon), noting that bids must be submitted through the commercial electronic platform mentioned above. * The bidder must submit the preliminary bid bond in favor of Kuwait National Petroleum Company in an envelope marked with the tender name, address, and closing date, and place it in the designated box located on the first floor, reception hall, at the Kuwait National Petroleum Company Main Office building in Al-Ahmadi , no later than 1:00 PM (13:00 Noon) on Thursday, 17/07/2025, with a copy of the preliminary bid bond attached to the bid. * The bidder must deposit the tender document purchase fee into the account of Kuwait National Petroleum Company (KW59NBOK0000000000002004811204) and submit the receipt with the bid. Bids not accompanied by the payment receipt will be rejected. If the bid is submitted electronically, a copy of the payment receipt must be attached electronically with the bid. * Please note that this tender is limited and non-divisible among the companies listed in the attached schedule. * Please note that there is no preliminary meeting. * No single person or group of persons acting together may register under more than one name during classification, or submit more than one bid in a tender, except for alternative bids where the tender conditions permit their submission. * However, a person who has an interest in separate companies, each independent with its own legal personality, finances, engineers, employees, and general administrative apparatus, may, during classification, register himself and each of his independent companies. * Any bid from a bidder violating this article shall be deemed null and void by operation of law, in accordance with Article No. (29) of Law No. (49) of 2016 regarding Public Tenders . * Note: - * All required documents and certificates must be attached to the bid through the commercial electronic platform as specified in the tender documents. * The Supreme Purchasing Committee wishes to draw the attention of bidders who have inquiries or reservations regarding the technical specifications to submit such requests no later than (3 weeks) before the closing date (for this tender). These requests will be rejected and will not be considered for any reason after this deadline. * To obtain a username and password to access the commercial electronic platform, please contact phone numbers 23887794/23887792. chunks.title: Announcement of Tender Award
chunks.text_en: No. KPC / T / 474 / 2024 Regarding: Renewal of VMware Software Subscriptions and Maintenance Based on the approval of the Supreme Purchasing Committee for tenders of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation and its subsidiaries, in its meeting No. (25/2025) held on 05/03/2025, Kuwait Petroleum Corporation announces the award of Tender No. KPC / T / 474 / 2024 regarding: Renewal of VMware Software Subscriptions and Maintenance, to Bid No. (2) submitted by Messrs. Al-Kharafi Computer Systems Company (first lowest price and compliant with the required technical specifications and conditions), for a total amount of K.D. 148,104/- (One Hundred Forty-Eight Thousand One Hundred Four Kuwaiti Dinars).

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en:
Please be informed that a preliminary meeting for bidders will be held remotely (online via MS Teams application) at 10:00 AM on Monday, 2025/4/28, to answer bidders' inquiries. The meeting link - and other meeting details - will be sent to all bidders through the K-Tendering portal, which bidders should regularly monitor for updates regarding the tender. No single person or group of persons acting jointly may register under more than one name during classification, or submit more than one bid in a tender, except for alternative bids where the tender conditions permit their submission. However, a person who has a share in separate companies, each registered with its own legal personality, finances, engineers, employees, and general administrative apparatus, may, during classification, register himself and each of his independent companies. Any bid submitted by a bidder in violation of this article shall be deemed null and void by operation of law, in accordance with Article No. (29) of Law No. (49) of 2016 regarding Public Tenders. Note: - All required documents and certificates must be attached to the bid through the K-Tendering portal as specified in the tender documents. - The Higher Purchasing Committee wishes to draw the attention of Messrs./Bidders who have inquiries or reservations regarding technical specifications to submit such requests a maximum of (3 weeks) before the closing date (for this tender). These requests will be rejected and will not be considered for any reason whatsoever after this deadline. - To obtain a username and password for accessing the K-Tendering portal, please contact phone numbers 23887794/23887792. - cwphelp@knpc.com - Illustrative guidelines on how to submit a bid through the K-Tendering portal are available on our website. chunks.title: Details for Tender MNA/01/2025 in Polypropylene Plant
chunks.text_en:
Tender Details for Polypropylene Plant | Issue Date | Closing Date | Document Value | Preliminary Bond | |:---|:---|:---|:---| | 13/4/2025 | 18/5/2025 | KD 50/- | KD 300/- |
> This tender is limited / indivisible to the companies listed in the table below. > > Tender Scope of Work: > MV 6.6KV motors are existing in PIC, which are running continuously from Turnaround to turnaround. As a part of Predictive maintenance, these motors are required to be analyzed for their insulation integrity. > > The tender closing date is Sunday, 18/5/2025, at 1:00 PM, provided that the bid is submitted via the K-Tendering platform. > Firstly, no copy of the bid shall be sent to any other party, otherwise, this bid will be considered null and void. The bid validity period shall be (90) days from the date of opening the bid envelopes, and no bids will be accepted after the specified deadline. > > The preliminary meeting is scheduled for Tuesday, 6/5/2025, at 10:00 AM, noting that the meeting will be held virtually (Virtual Meeting). > > A preliminary bond in the form of a certified check or a letter of guarantee from a bank accredited in the State of Kuwait , issued in the name of the bidder and in favor of Petrochemical Industries Company , for the amount of (300/-) three hundred Kuwaiti Dinars only, with the same validity period as the bid, must be submitted. The original bond must be submitted sufficiently before the closing date, provided that a soft copy is attached to the bid. > > The original preliminary bank guarantee, whose value is specified in this announcement, must be delivered to the Sub-Committee for Opening Bids before the specified tender closing date, at the company's main headquarters in South Sabahiya , Office No. (112-CG), with a soft copy required to be attached to the bid. > > The final deadline for submitting any inquiries, reservations, or requests by participating companies regarding this tender is 8/5/2025. These requests will not be considered for any reason whatsoever after this specified deadline. > > The document purchase value is (50/-) fifty Kuwaiti Dinars . > > Documents can be purchased by bank deposit into the account of Petrochemical Industries Company , with a copy of the bank transfer receipt attached, and the tender name, tender number, and purchasing company name clearly stated on the purchase receipt issued by the bank, according to the following details: > > * Account Name : Petrochemical Industries Company > * Banker's Name : National Bank of Kuwait, Ahmadi Branch , Kuwait > * IBAN # KW77NBOK0000000000001003811717 > * KD Call Account No. : 1003811717 > * Swift Code : NBOKKWKW In this regard, we draw attention to the fact that, based on the text of Article (16) of Law No. (49) of 2016 regarding Public Tenders, the aforementioned tender will be offered to the list of qualified local companies with the Central Qualification Committee affiliated with the Supreme Purchasing Committee under the following two categories: * K 01.12 – Major Roads & Intersections Including Asphalt Works and Associated Services. * K 01.09 – Internal Roads, DYKES and Parking Construction and Maintenance.

chunks.title: To be submitted with the bid:
chunks.text_en: 1. A copy of the preliminary insurance (certified check / or letter of guarantee) from a bank accredited in the State of Kuwait , issued in the name of the bidder and in favor of Petrochemical Industries Company . 2. A copy of the signature authorization (letter of authorization for authorized signatories). 3. A 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Civil Information . 4. A certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage issued by the Public Authority for Manpower - National Labor Development Department – valid for one year. 5. A certificate of payment of contributions to the Public Institution for Social Security as stipulated in Article (95) of the Social Insurance Law . 6. A clearance certificate for the contractor's contribution payment to the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences as stated in item (Fourth) of the letter from the Head of the Cabinet Diwan dated 26/01/2022. 7. A recent copy of the company's articles of association and commercial license from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry and any subsequent amendments thereto. 8. A copy of the registration receipt with the Central Agency for Public Tenders and a copy of the registration certificate with the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 9. A certificate from the Ministry of Finance stating that there is no income tax on the company, and a list of the international agencies represented by the company, with supporting evidence attached. 10. Companies classified as small or medium enterprises and wishing to participate in the tender must submit a valid registration certificate in the National Register for the Care and Development of Small and Medium Enterprises . At least (30) days after the date of publication of this announcement in the Official Gazette "Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm " and on the website of Kuwait Oil Tanker Company , any interested party whose name is not included in the list of companies invited to the tender has the right to appeal their exclusion to the Head of the Supreme Purchasing Committee for Kuwait Petroleum Corporation tenders and its subsidiaries within (30) days from the date of this announcement. The application or request shall be submitted at the headquarters of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation , Main Office – Supreme Purchasing Committee for Kuwait Petroleum Corporation tenders and its subsidiaries. Any party aggrieved by their non-inclusion in the above list, if submitting an appeal / participation request to the Board of Directors of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation , must pay a fee of (250 K.D. ) for appeal / participation requests in consideration of studying the possibility of adding them to that list. The appeal / participation request fees shall be paid by bank deposit receipt into the account of Kuwait Oil Tanker Company mentioned below, provided that the appeal fees are refunded to the contractor / supplier if their appeal is accepted formally and substantively. **National Bank of Kuwait – Main Branch – Kuwait** **Account No.: 1000307805** **SWIFT: NBOKKWKW** * Any changes will be announced through Al-Kuwait Al-Youm newspaper and/or the company's website (www.pic.com.kw). * Contact the company's Contracts Team via email contracts_team@pic.com.kw or by phone at: 23851389 The company's Procurement Team can be contacted via email gpd@kotc.com.kw or by phone at: 24625475 – 24625528 – 24625460 for inquiries regarding the tender.

chunks.title: Regarding the Offering of the Auction/Bid
chunks.text_en:
No.: RFP-2133266 Kuwait Oil Company announces the offering of Auction/Bid No.: RFP-2133266 UTILIZATION OF KOC VACANT PREMISES – COFFEE SHOP AT CITG BUILDING Utilization and investment of spaces designated as a coffee shop in the Information Technology Building. This is in accordance with the terms and specifications stated in the auction/bid documents. These documents can be obtained through the K-Tendering Portal on the website of Kuwait Petroleum Corporation and its subsidiaries: https://www.ktendering.com.kw RFQs/Tenders/Auctions (Open to all Suppliers) → Search for the RFP Title using the Filter. The final time for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on the aforementioned day. Please note that bids must be submitted exclusively through the commercial electronic page indicated above. **Pre-bid Meeting Date** 10:00 AM on Sunday, 20/4/2025, at all company office buildings in Ahmadi – Building (B1) – Ground Floor – Marrat Meeting Room – Room No. (101167) (Marrat Conference Room, Room No. (101167) , Ground Floor, Building B-1, New KOC Complex, (ex-KOC Industrial Area, Ahmadi) **Site Visit Date** On the same day as the pre-bid meeting. * This auction/bid is open to specialized companies in accordance with the terms and specifications stated in the auction/bid documents. * Bidders wishing to submit inquiries, clarifications, etc., must submit them to the relevant party at the time and place specified above in the table for the pre-bid meeting (inquiry session). * The last date for receiving inquiries and clarifications from companies is 24/4/2025, and no inquiries or clarifications received after this date will be considered. * The right to attend the pre-bid meeting is restricted to bidders who have purchased the auction/bid documents, with a maximum of two persons per company, accompanied by a letter of authorization from the company and a copy of the receipt for purchasing the auction/bid documents. * All required documents, papers, and certificates must be submitted through the electronic system indicated above, as specified in the auction/bid documents. * The bidder must deposit the fee for purchasing the auction/bid document into the Kuwait Oil Company account mentioned below, with the necessity of adding the auction/bid number and company name to the purchase receipt issued by the bank and submitting the receipt with the bid. Bids not accompanied by the payment receipt will be excluded. * The final time for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on the aforementioned deposit date. * Note: Bids must be submitted through the e-business page mentioned above. * Bid validity period (90 days from the closing date) in favor of Kuwait Oil Company . **Pre-Bid Meeting Date** At 09:00 AM on Thursday, 10/4/2025, at Hubara Center – Meeting Hall – Ahmadi Area . Hubara Center/Assembly Hall – Ahmadi Meeting * Based on the text of Article (29) of Law No. 49 of 2016 regarding Public Tenders : "No single person or group of persons combined may register under more than one name during classification or submit more than one bid in a tender, except for alternative bids where the tender conditions permit their submission. However, a person who has a share in separate companies, each independent with its legal personality, finances, engineers, employees, and general administrative apparatus, may register himself and each of his independent companies during classification. Any bid from a tenderer violating this article shall be deemed null and void by operation of law." * The tenderer must deposit the bid form with the original initial security deposit in an envelope marked with the tender name, address, and closing date, and place it in the designated bid box located at the company's headquarters – Kuwait Oil Company Office Buildings Complex – Ahmadi – Gate No. (1) – Building (B1) – Ground Floor. * All required documents, papers, and certificates must be submitted through the electronic system mentioned above and as specified in the tender documents. * Please note that this tender is divisible, and alternative bids are not permitted. * The tenderer must deposit the tender document purchase fee into the Kuwait Oil Company account mentioned below, with the tender number and company name clearly added to the bank-issued purchase receipt. ACCOUNT NO.: 8801243 SWIFT CODE: GULBKWKW IBAN: KW62GULB000000000000008801243. ACCOUNT NAME: KUWAIT OIL COMPANY Bidders wishing to submit inquiries, clarifications, etc., must adhere to submitting them to the relevant party at the time and place specified above, taking into account the pre-bid meeting (inquiry session). * The right to attend the pre-bid meeting is restricted to bidders who have purchased the tender documents, with a maximum of two persons per company, accompanied by a letter of authorization from the company and a copy of the tender document purchase receipt. * The deadline for receiving inquiries and clarifications submitted by companies is 24/04/2025, and no inquiries or clarifications received after this date will be considered. * A certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage, issued by the Public Authority for Manpower and valid for one year, must be attached with the bid submission. * Attach a valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority due to any of the violations mentioned in Article (10) of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector. * Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempt from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article (39) of Law No. 74 of 2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016. * Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to bid for the tender must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development , confirming the accreditation of their project or their status as a medium enterprise. * Any changes to the tender will be announced through Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm newspaper or via the e-business portal mentioned above.
